You are auditing source code for an INDEPENDENT external code review (client: Telus). Tools: Bash, Read.

STEP 0 — SKIP IF DONE. Run: test -s /tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/name_findings/shard_53.tsv && echo DONE || echo TODO
If it prints DONE, this shard is already complete — reply exactly "shard 53: cached" and STOP. Do nothing else.

STEP 1 — get your file list: /opt/conda/bin/python -c "import json; print(chr(10).join(json.load(open('/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/.name_shards.json'))[53]))"
It prints lines "relpath:START-END" relative to /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent.

STEP 2 — GENUINELY READ every assigned file/range line by line with the Read tool (offset/limit from START-END; use limit up to 2000 per call to minimize calls). Do NOT grep-and-skim — read the code.

STEP 3 — flag EVERY name (file/module, class, func/method, var, const, enum value) that looks UNPROFESSIONAL in an external review:
  - marketing/quality adjectives: honest, real, true, smart, magic, ultimate, enhanced, improved, robust, aggressive, killer, awesome, blazing, super, mega, best
  - temporal/version markers: v2/v3/v4, r2/r3, _new, _old, legacy, deprecated, next/nextgen, final(meaning 'this version'), fixed, temp, wip, draft, backup, orig, working, experimental, proto
  - jokes/informal/vague/slang: junk, garbage, hack, spy, stub(as a real name), yolo, stuff, misc, foo/bar, doit, thing, blah, landmine, nuke, magic-number-as-word, terse cryptic abbreviations
Judge at a STRICT bar: **when in doubt, mark RENAME.** Only KEEP-BUT-NOTED for unambiguously standard, descriptive, professional vocabulary or a REAL domain term genuinely implemented (finalize, proper_noun, evict_older_than, SmartArt only if it truly makes SmartArt).

STEP 4 — APPEND TAB-separated rows to /tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/name_findings/shard_53.tsv (one per flagged name), using printf with real tabs:
  VERDICT<TAB>NAME<TAB>relpath:LINE<TAB>KIND<TAB>WHY<TAB>SUGGESTED_NAME
  VERDICT=RENAME or KEEP-NOTED; KIND=file/class/func/var/const/enum. If a file is fully clean, write: NONE<TAB>-<TAB>relpath<TAB>-<TAB>clean<TAB>-

Reply ONLY: "shard 53: <R> renames, <K> kept across <F> files".

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	"""Retrieval projection (S2) — the no-starvation core, compile-only + offline.
2	
3	This is the typed *retrieval view* of a pinned :class:`PlanningGateArtifact`
4	(contract + plan). It is the S2 lever the consolidated design (§5, §4 retrieval
5	row) calls out: the gate's scope must reach FS-Researcher **query generation**
6	and backend **routing** BEFORE the first fetch — never as a post-fetch filter of
7	a frozen corpus (the banned 997->131 anti-pattern).
8	
9	A :class:`RetrievalProjection` COMPILES the contract+plan into exactly the three
10	levers ``live_retriever.run_live_retrieval`` already exposes but the driver never
11	populates from the gate:
12	
13	  * ``research_frame``  — a :class:`research_planner.ResearchFrame` whose
14	    ``evidence_needs`` are ROUTED from the contract's ``source_types`` (journal /
15	    academic / scholarly -> ``primary_literature`` = *GO FIND journals*, routing
16	    the S2 / OpenAlex scholarly adapters), plus entities / comparators /
17	    jurisdictions for the field-agnostic need-type registry;
18	  * ``amplified_queries`` — query TEXT built from the plan's MANDATORY query
19	    intents: concepts + required-terms, plus native-language sub-queries for a
20	    hard ``source_languages`` term, plus per-entity sub-queries, plus the hard
21	    scope terms folded into the query text so a "journal-only" rule shapes
22	    discovery from the first query;
23	  * ``protocol`` — the frame's anchor protocol (``to_anchor_protocol``), so the
24	    amplified sub-queries validate against the frame's tokens.
25	
26	Guardrail posture
27	-----------------
28	* **Compile-only. No network, no LLM, no I/O.** This module builds plain data
29	  (lists of strings, a dataclass) from an already-pinned artifact. It NEVER
30	  fetches. The only cross-module import is the champion ``ResearchFrame`` (a pure
31	  dataclass) which it constructs; that construction runs the frame's own
32	  ``validate_evidence_needs`` so a routed need is always a valid enum member.
33	* **ADD / ROUTE, never filter.** Every method returns queries or routing hints
34	  that ENLARGE or STEER discovery. There is no drop path here. The mechanized
35	  997-guard (:func:`candidate_query_count`) counts the candidate query/evidence
36	  lanes so a caller can assert the gate path never REDUCES the lane count vs the
37	  no-gate path — at planning level, before any fetch.
38	* **Hard vs soft.** A HARD scope term (``force==hard``, which the schema
39	  guarantees is explicit/user-backed) is folded into query TEXT and routed to a
40	  backend need. A SOFT term (preference/open) contributes ranking/entity breadth
41	  but is NEVER allowed to gate. This module cannot introduce a hard filter — it
42	  only emits query strings + need tokens.
43	* **Fail-open.** Every builder tolerates a degraded/empty contract: an empty
44	  contract yields an empty projection (no needs, no amplified queries), so the
45	  caller reads the champion default (``research_frame=None`` path) and the run is
46	  byte-identical. Constructing the projection NEVER raises for a shape problem.
47	
48	Nothing here is wired ON by default. ``fs_researcher_query_gen`` /
49	``live_retriever`` / ``run_one_query`` accept a ``retrieval_plan=None`` kwarg
50	(default None => champion behaviour); the projection is threaded only when the
51	``PG_GATE`` flag is set and an artifact exists. See :mod:`gate_flags`.
52	"""
53	
54	from __future__ import annotations
55	
56	import os
57	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
58	from typing import Any, Optional
59	
60	
61	def _source_restriction_hard_enabled() -> bool:
62	    """GENERALIZED Fix 5(d): a HARD source-KIND STARVATION mask (``prefer`` + ``hard``
63	    strictness -> ``constraint_enforcement`` drops EVERY out-of-scope-kind row from
64	    grounding) is armed ONLY when ``PG_SOURCE_RESTRICTION_HARD`` is explicitly ON. Kind-
65	    AGNOSTIC (reads the kinds from the policy; no journal literal). Default OFF: a hard
66	    ``allowed_source_kinds`` term is projected as a visible PREFERENCE (demote-not-drop),
67	    never a corpus-starving mask. The genuine hard restriction is additionally gated behind
68	    a runtime corpus-adequacy + acquisition-receipt pre-check at the eligibility seam."""
69	    return os.getenv("PG_SOURCE_RESTRICTION_HARD", "0").strip().lower() in (
70	        "1", "true", "on", "yes", "enabled",
71	    )
72	
73	from src.polaris_graph.planning.planning_gate_schema import (
74	    FORCE_HARD,
75	    PlanningGateArtifact,
76	    QueryIntent,
77	    ResearchContract,
78	    ResearchExecutionPlan,
79	)
80	
81	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
82	# source_type -> evidence_need routing (the "GO FIND journals" map)
83	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
84	# Maps a contract ``scope.source_types`` value (free-string, model-emitted) onto
85	# the field-agnostic ``EVIDENCE_NEEDS`` enum the need-type router routes adapters
86	# off. This is the load-bearing S2 routing: a "journal articles only" contract
87	# term (task-72) routes ``primary_literature`` = the S2 + OpenAlex + scholarly-
88	# graph adapters, so FS goes and FINDS journals from the first query instead of
89	# filtering a general corpus afterwards.
90	#
91	# Each key is matched as a case-insensitive SUBSTRING of the normalized source
92	# type, so "peer-reviewed journal article", "academic paper", "scholarly
93	# article" all route primary_literature. Unmatched source types contribute no
94	# need (the router's safe generic fallback applies), never an error.
95	_SOURCE_TYPE_NEED_MAP: tuple[tuple[str, str], ...] = (
96	    ("journal", "primary_literature"),
97	    ("peer-review", "primary_literature"),
98	    ("peer review", "primary_literature"),
99	    ("academic", "primary_literature"),
100	    ("scholarly", "primary_literature"),
101	    ("scientific", "primary_literature"),
102	    ("paper", "primary_literature"),
103	    ("study", "primary_literature"),
104	    ("preprint", "primary_literature"),
105	    ("regulation", "regulatory"),
106	    ("regulatory", "regulatory"),
107	    ("guideline", "regulatory"),
108	    ("law", "legal"),
109	    ("legal", "legal"),
110	    ("case law", "legal"),
111	    ("statute", "legal"),
112	    ("court", "legal"),
113	    ("statistic", "statistical"),
114	    ("census", "statistical"),
115	    ("survey data", "statistical"),
116	    ("dataset", "datasets"),
117	    ("standard", "standards"),
118	    ("news", "news_press"),
119	    ("press", "news_press"),
120	    ("media", "news_press"),
121	    ("filing", "company_filings"),
122	    ("sec ", "company_filings"),
123	    ("10-k", "company_filings"),
124	    ("annual report", "company_filings"),
125	)
126	
127	
128	def _norm(s: Any) -> str:
129	    return str(s or "").strip().lower()
130	
131	
132	def source_type_to_need(source_type: Any) -> Optional[str]:
133	    """Route a single contract ``source_type`` value onto an ``EVIDENCE_NEEDS``
134	    token, or ``None`` if it matches no known family (the router falls back to
135	    the safe generic set — never an error)."""
136	    st = _norm(source_type)
137	    if not st:
138	        return None
139	    for needle, need in _SOURCE_TYPE_NEED_MAP:
140	        if needle in st:
141	            return need
142	    return None
143	
144	
145	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
146	# contract source-kind VALUE -> ontology facet_id (the bridge's vocabulary reuse)
147	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
148	
149	
150	def _load_scope_ontology_safe() -> dict[str, Any]:
151	    """Load the scope facet ontology, or ``{}`` on any fault (fail-open: the bridge
152	    then emits opaque facet ids and the enforcer masks nothing)."""
153	    try:
154	        from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.scope_facet_classifier import (  # noqa: PLC0415
155	            load_scope_ontology,
156	        )
157	        return load_scope_ontology() or {}
158	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
159	        return {}
160	
161	
162	def _resolve_facet_id(value: Any, ontology: dict[str, Any]) -> tuple[str, str]:
163	    """Map a contract source-kind VALUE (e.g. ``"journal article"``) to an ontology
164	    ``(facet_id, dimension)`` via the SAME synonym table the deterministic scope
165	    extractor uses (LAW VI — no new vocabulary). A value matching no facet synonym
166	    is returned as an OPAQUE id (``opaque:<value>``, dimension ``source_type``) so it
167	    is NEVER dropped — it simply classifies no source (fail-open), staying disclosed.
168	    """
169	    v = _norm(value)
170	    if not v:
171	        return "", "source_type"
172	    best: tuple[str, str, int] = ("", "source_type", -1)
173	    for facet in (ontology.get("facets") or []):
174	        if not isinstance(facet, dict):
175	            continue
176	        fid = str(facet.get("id") or "")
177	        dim = str(facet.get("dimension") or "source_type")
178	        if not fid:
179	            continue
180	        for syn in (facet.get("synonyms") or []):
181	            synl = str(syn).strip().lower()
182	            if not synl:
183	                continue
184	            # longest matching synonym wins (most specific) — substring both ways to
185	            # tolerate plurals ("journal articles" == "journal article").
186	            if synl in v or v in synl:
187	                if len(synl) > best[2]:
188	                    best = (fid, dim, len(synl))
189	    if best[0]:
190	        return best[0], best[1]
191	    return f"opaque:{v}", "source_type"
192	
193	
194	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
195	# Typed retrieval POLICY (spec item 2) — the enforcement-facing compiled view
196	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
197	
198	
199	@dataclass
200	class RetrievalPolicy:
201	    """A typed, hash-stamped policy compiled from the pinned contract + plan.
202	
203	    This is the enforcement-facing companion to :class:`RetrievalProjection`
204	    (which owns query TEXT + routing). ``RetrievalPolicy`` is the DECLARATIVE
205	    predicate set a caller executes as backend filters, prefetch ranking, and a
206	    post-fetch citable-eligibility verdict — ALWAYS upstream of the frozen
207	    verifier (it changes which rows are ELIGIBLE to cite, never HOW a claim is
208	    verified). It is pure data: no network, no LLM, no I/O.
209	
210	    Fields:
211	      * ``allowed_source_kinds`` — hard ``scope.source_types`` values. A candidate
212	        whose kind matches NONE (when non-empty) is out of the citable menu.
213	      * ``excluded_source_kinds`` — NEGATIVE predicate (``scope.prohibited`` etc.).
214	        A candidate whose kind matches ANY is masked. NEVER positive query text.
215	      * ``date_from`` / ``date_to`` — inclusive publication-date interval (ISO
216	        ``YYYY-MM-DD`` or ``YYYY``), routed server-side to OpenAlex
217	        ``from/to_publication_date`` where a backend supports it.
218	      * ``languages`` — hard ``scope.source_languages`` codes/names.
219	      * ``named_inclusions`` / ``named_exclusions`` — specific named sources/domains
220	        to pin in / mask out.
221	      * ``quality_profile`` — a REF to a domain-neutral quality profile (e.g.
222	        "high") selected over already-fetched metadata; never a query word.
223	      * ``predicate_force`` — per-predicate ``{predicate_key: "hard"|"soft"}`` so a
224	        caller knows which are gating vs ranking-only.
225	      * ``contract_hash`` — the pinned ``contract_sha256`` this policy was compiled
226	        from, so retrieval/audit can assert hash identity with the compiler.
227	    """
228	
229	    allowed_source_kinds: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
230	    excluded_source_kinds: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
231	    date_from: Optional[str] = None
232	    date_to: Optional[str] = None
233	    languages: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
234	    named_inclusions: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
235	    named_exclusions: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
236	    quality_profile: Optional[str] = None
237	    # OPAQUE eligibility predicates (F2): hard un-normalized deontic clauses
238	    # preserved verbatim as post-fetch eligibility judgements (a source is judged
239	    # against each). NEVER a query string; NEVER a positive facet.
240	    opaque_eligibility: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
241	    predicate_force: dict[str, str] = field(default_factory=dict)
242	    contract_hash: str = ""
243	
244	    def is_empty(self) -> bool:
245	        """True when the policy carries no executable predicate (the permissive /
246	        empty-contract case) — a caller then applies NO filter (byte-identical)."""
247	        return not (
248	            self.allowed_source_kinds
249	            or self.excluded_source_kinds
250	            or self.date_from
251	            or self.date_to
252	            or self.languages
253	            or self.named_inclusions
254	            or self.named_exclusions
255	            or self.quality_profile
256	            or self.opaque_eligibility
257	        )
258	
259	    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
260	        return {
261	            "allowed_source_kinds": list(self.allowed_source_kinds),
262	            "excluded_source_kinds": list(self.excluded_source_kinds),
263	            "date_from": self.date_from,
264	            "date_to": self.date_to,
265	            "languages": list(self.languages),
266	            "named_inclusions": list(self.named_inclusions),
267	            "named_exclusions": list(self.named_exclusions),
268	            "quality_profile": self.quality_profile,
269	            "opaque_eligibility": list(self.opaque_eligibility),
270	            "predicate_force": dict(self.predicate_force),
271	            "contract_hash": self.contract_hash,
272	        }
273	
274	    # -- the contract -> protocol BRIDGE (spec item 1) ------------------------
275	
276	    def to_scope_protocol(
277	        self, *, ontology: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None
278	    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
279	        """Compile this policy into the LEGACY ``protocol`` shape the FROZEN
280	        ``constraint_enforcement.build_scope_enforcement`` reads.
281	
282	        This is the single wire (Sol §4 "Enforcement") that makes the EXISTING
283	        weight-demote / mask / named-pin / hard-timeline enforcer act on the GATE's
284	        pinned terms instead of the parallel legacy regex extraction. It emits:
285	
286	          * ``scope_constraints`` = ``{facets:[{facet_id, dimension, op, strictness,
287	            trigger_span}], named_include:[...], named_exclude:[...]}`` — the exact
288	            ``ScopeConstraints.to_dict()`` shape. ``allowed_source_kinds`` ->
289	            ``op=prefer`` facets; ``excluded_source_kinds`` -> ``op=exclude`` facets;
290	            ``named_inclusions``/``named_exclusions`` -> the named lists. Strictness
291	            is ``hard`` iff the policy marks that predicate hard, else ``weight``.
292	          * ``date_range`` = ``{"start","end"}`` from ``date_from``/``date_to``.
293	          * ``user_constraints`` = ``{timeline_strictness}`` (``hard`` iff the date
294	            predicate is hard — the wire that fires the enforcer's hard-timeline
295	            mask), plus ``language`` when a hard language predicate is present.
296	
297	        Source-kind VALUES are resolved to ontology ``facet_id``s via the SAME
298	        synonym table the deterministic scope extractor uses (no new vocabulary,
299	        LAW VI). A value that resolves to no facet is emitted as an OPAQUE facet id
300	        (``opaque:<value>``) so it is NEVER silently dropped — it simply matches no
301	        source in ``classify_source_facets`` (fail-open demote), but it stays
302	        disclosed. Pure + deterministic; an empty policy compiles an empty protocol.
303	        """
304	        facets: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
305	        seen_facets: set[tuple[str, str]] = set()
306	
307	        def _facet(facet_id: str, dimension: str, op: str, hard: bool) -> None:
308	            key = (facet_id, op)
309	            if not facet_id or key in seen_facets:
310	                return
311	            seen_facets.add(key)
312	            facets.append({
313	                "facet_id": facet_id,
314	                "dimension": dimension,
315	                "op": op,
316	                "strictness": "hard" if hard else "weight",
317	                "trigger_span": "",
318	            })
319	
320	        ont = ontology if ontology is not None else _load_scope_ontology_safe()
321	        allowed_hard = str(
322	            (self.predicate_force or {}).get("allowed_source_kinds", "soft")
323	        ).lower() == "hard"
324	        excluded_hard = str(
325	            (self.predicate_force or {}).get("excluded_source_kinds", "soft")
326	        ).lower() == "hard"
327	
328	        # GENERALIZED Fix 5(d): allowed-source-KIND = visible PREFERENCE + measured compliance,
329	        # NOT starvation. ``allowed_source_kinds`` already projects as ``op=prefer``; its strictness
330	        # decides whether ``constraint_enforcement`` HARD-EXCLUDES non-matching rows (prefer+hard,
331	        # the 997->131 starvation) or merely DEMOTES them (prefer+weight, order-not-drop). A HARD
332	        # contract term now degrades to a demoting preference by default; the corpus-starving
333	        # hard restriction arms ONLY under the explicit PG_SOURCE_RESTRICTION_HARD flag (itself
334	        # additionally gated behind a runtime corpus-adequacy + receipt pre-check at the seam).
335	        allowed_prefer_hard = allowed_hard and _source_restriction_hard_enabled()
336	        for kind in self.allowed_source_kinds:
337	            fid, dim = _resolve_facet_id(kind, ont)
338	            _facet(fid, dim, "prefer", allowed_prefer_hard)
339	        for kind in self.excluded_source_kinds:
340	            fid, dim = _resolve_facet_id(kind, ont)
341	            _facet(fid, dim, "exclude", excluded_hard)
342	
343	        # named in/exclusions -> the enforcer's named_include/named_exclude lists
344	        # (label + identity; named-exclude is always hard per the enforcer).
345	        named_include = [
346	            {"label": str(n).strip(), "op": "include", "strictness": "weight",
347	             "identity": {}}
348	            for n in self.named_inclusions if str(n).strip()
349	        ]
350	        named_exclude = [
351	            {"label": str(n).strip(), "op": "exclude", "strictness": "hard",
352	             "identity": {}}
353	            for n in self.named_exclusions if str(n).strip()
354	        ]
355	
356	        # timeline: date_range + hard-strictness wire (fires the frozen mask).
357	        date_hard = str(
358	            (self.predicate_force or {}).get("date", "soft")
359	        ).lower() == "hard"
360	        lang_hard = str(
361	            (self.predicate_force or {}).get("languages", "soft")
362	        ).lower() == "hard"
363	
364	        quality_hard = str(
365	            (self.predicate_force or {}).get("quality_profile", "soft")
366	        ).lower() == "hard"
367	
368	        user_constraints: dict[str, Any] = {
369	            "timeline_strictness": "hard" if (date_hard and (self.date_from or self.date_to))
370	            else "weight",
371	        }
372	        if self.date_from:
373	            user_constraints["date_start_iso"] = self.date_from
374	        if self.date_to:
375	            user_constraints["date_end_iso"] = self.date_to
376	        # F6: emit ALL hard languages (not just languages[0]) so a multi-language
377	        # source rule reaches the enforcer intact. ``language`` keeps the legacy
378	        # scalar (first) for back-compat; ``languages`` carries the full list.
379	        if lang_hard and self.languages:
380	            user_constraints["language"] = self.languages[0]
381	            user_constraints["languages"] = list(self.languages)
382	            user_constraints["language_strictness"] = "hard"
383	        # F6: emit the quality_profile so it is no longer decorative — the enforcer
384	        # (or the post-fetch eligibility stage) reads a domain-neutral quality ref
385	        # + its strictness. It is NEVER a query word (LAW VI); it selects a profile
386	        # over already-fetched metadata.
387	        if self.quality_profile:
388	            user_constraints["quality_profile"] = self.quality_profile
389	            user_constraints["quality_strictness"] = "hard" if quality_hard else "weight"
390	        # F2: opaque eligibility clauses are surfaced (disclosed) as post-fetch
391	        # eligibility predicates — never a facet, never query text.
392	        if self.opaque_eligibility:
393	            user_constraints["opaque_eligibility"] = list(self.opaque_eligibility)
394	
395	        return {
396	            "scope_constraints": {
397	                "facets": facets,
398	                "named_include": named_include,
399	                "named_exclude": named_exclude,
400	            },
401	            "date_range": {
402	                "start": self.date_from,
403	                "end": self.date_to,
404	            },
405	            "user_constraints": user_constraints,
406	        }
407	
408	
409	def _year_to_iso_from(value: Any) -> Optional[str]:
410	    """Coerce a scope.date value (``"2024"`` / ``"2024-03"`` / ``"from 2024"``)
411	    into an inclusive ISO ``from`` date (``YYYY-01-01`` for a bare year). Returns
412	    ``None`` when no 4-digit year is present — a caller then applies no date
413	    filter (fail-open)."""
414	    import re as _re
415	    s = str(value or "").strip()
416	    m = _re.search(r"(19|20)\d{2}", s)
417	    if not m:
418	        return None
419	    year = m.group(0)
420	    # a bare year -> Jan 1 of that year (inclusive "from YEAR onward").
421	    tail = s[m.end():].lstrip("-/ ")
422	    mo = _re.match(r"(\d{1,2})", tail)
423	    if mo:
424	        month = max(1, min(12, int(mo.group(1))))
425	        return f"{year}-{month:02d}-01"
426	    return f"{year}-01-01"
427

429	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
430	# The projection
431	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
432	
433	
434	@dataclass
435	class RetrievalProjection:
436	    """Compile-time retrieval view of a pinned contract + plan.
437	
438	    Built by :func:`from_artifact` (or :meth:`ResearchContract.to_research_frame`
439	    via the thin methods added to the schema). Holds only plain data; the
440	    ``research_frame`` is materialized lazily by :meth:`to_research_frame` so this
441	    dataclass has no heavy import at construction.
442	    """
443	
444	    contract: ResearchContract
445	    plan: ResearchExecutionPlan
446	    original_prompt: str = ""
447	
448	    # Precomputed routing tokens (validated against the frame enum lazily).
449	    evidence_needs: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
450	    jurisdictions: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
451	    entities: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
452	    comparators: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
453	    # Hard scope-term VALUES (verbatim), folded into query text + surfaced for a
454	    # backend-native filter by a caller that supports one. NEVER a drop here.
455	    hard_scope_terms: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
456	    # Soft/preference scope-term values — ranking hints only, never a gate.
457	    soft_scope_terms: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
458	    # EXCLUSIONS (scope.prohibited): NEGATIVE predicates. These are values the
459	    # user forbade ("no blogs", "exclude press releases"). They are NEVER folded
460	    # into positive query text (the pre-Phase-C bug: a prohibition became a
461	    # positive query suffix, steering discovery TOWARD the excluded kind). They
462	    # are surfaced ONLY as a negative eligibility predicate a caller applies
463	    # against a candidate's metadata (post-fetch, upstream of the frozen verifier).
464	    excluded_scope_terms: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
465	    # NEGATIVE named-source predicates ("don't use Reuters"): NEVER query text,
466	    # surfaced only as named exclusions the eligibility stage masks on.
467	    named_exclusions: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
468	    # Named-source INCLUSIONS ("Use Reuters and AP"): a retrieval boost, surfaced
469	    # as named inclusions — never a hard mask on their own.
470	    named_inclusions: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
471	    # OPAQUE eligibility terms (F2): a hard, un-normalized deontic clause preserved
472	    # verbatim as a POST-FETCH eligibility predicate (a source is judged against
473	    # it later). It is NEVER folded into positive query text — doing so re-steers
474	    # discovery toward the very thing an exclusion forbids (the pre-Phase-C bug).
475	    opaque_eligibility_terms: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
476	    # Hard source-language codes/names (e.g. "en"); drive native-language lanes.
477	    hard_languages: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
478	    # Champion-plan breadth: sub-queries ADDED to the amplified frontier so the
479	    # contract-driven projection keeps the champion planner's discovery breadth
480	    # (spec item 1: "merged additively with the champion plan's sub_queries so we
481	    # keep breadth but the CONTRACT drives scope"). Purely ADDITIVE — never a
482	    # filter; each is scope-anchored like a mandatory intent's text.
483	    extra_amplified_queries: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
484	
485	    # -- amplified query text (the FS frontier seed) --------------------------
486	
487	    def to_amplified_queries(self, *, base_question: str = "") -> list[str]:
488	        """The gate's query TEXT for ``run_live_retrieval(amplified_queries=...)``.
489	
490	        Built from the plan's MANDATORY query intents (concepts + required
491	        terms), each scope-anchored by folding the hard scope terms into the
492	        query string so the FIRST query already carries "journal" / "English"
493	        etc. Then per-entity sub-queries and native-language lanes are ADDED.
494	        Order-stable, de-duplicated (case-insensitively), never truncated here.
495	
496	        This is strictly ADDITIVE: it returns candidate query strings; the caller
497	        (FS-Researcher / live_retriever) runs each through the UNCHANGED
498	        per-query retrieval + frozen faithfulness engine. Nothing is dropped.
499	        """
500	        out: list[str] = []
501	        seen: set[str] = set()
502	
503	        def _add(q: str) -> None:
504	            qs = (q or "").strip()
505	            if not qs:
506	                return
507	            key = qs.lower()
508	            if key in seen:
509	                return
510	            seen.add(key)
511	            out.append(qs)
512	
513	        scope_suffix = " ".join(self.hard_scope_terms).strip()
514	
515	        for qi in self.plan.mandatory_query_intents():
516	            for text in _intent_query_texts(qi):
517	                if scope_suffix and scope_suffix.lower() not in text.lower():
518	                    _add(f"{text} {scope_suffix}")
519	                else:
520	                    _add(text)
521	
522	        # champion-plan breadth: ADD the champion planner's sub-queries, each
523	        # scope-anchored so the CONTRACT's hard scope still shapes discovery while
524	        # the champion's breadth is preserved (spec item 1). Purely additive.
525	        for text in self.extra_amplified_queries:
526	            text = (text or "").strip()
527	            if not text:
528	                continue
529	            if scope_suffix and scope_suffix.lower() not in text.lower():
530	                _add(f"{text} {scope_suffix}")
531	            else:
532	                _add(text)
533	
534	        # per-entity sub-queries (sub-entity discovery), anchored on the base
535	        # question so a bare entity does not drift into its broad field.
536	        anchor = (base_question or self.original_prompt or "").strip()
537	        for ent in self.entities:
538	            ent = (ent or "").strip()
539	            if not ent:
540	                continue
541	            if anchor:
542	                _add(f"{ent} {anchor}")
543	            else:
544	                _add(ent)
545	
546	        # native-language lanes: for a HARD source-language, ADD the scope-anchored
547	        # query in that language slot (the routing hint; the multilingual lane in
548	        # FS translates). We surface a language-tagged variant of the first few
549	        # mandatory concepts so a "source: English-language" rule reaches the
550	        # multilingual reserve as an explicit lane rather than being inferred.
551	        # (English is the pipeline default; a non-English hard language adds a
552	        # native tag so the reserve fires.)
553	        for lang in self.hard_languages:
554	            lang = (lang or "").strip()
555	            if not lang or lang.lower() in ("en", "english"):
556	                continue
557	            for qi in self.plan.mandatory_query_intents()[:2]:
558	                for text in _intent_query_texts(qi):
559	                    _add(f"{text} ({lang})")
560	
561	        return out
562	
563	    # -- scope terms ----------------------------------------------------------
564	
565	    def to_scope_terms(self) -> dict[str, list[str]]:
566	        """The hard/soft scope split + language lanes, as plain lists.
567	
568	        A caller that supports a backend-native filter reads ``hard`` for the
569	        explicit prohibitions/requirements (journal-only, English-only) and
570	        ``soft`` for ranking preferences. This is a compile view — it never
571	        applies a filter itself. The 997-guard reads the union count.
572	        """
573	        return {
574	            "hard": list(self.hard_scope_terms),
575	            "soft": list(self.soft_scope_terms),
576	            "excluded": list(self.excluded_scope_terms),
577	            "languages": list(self.hard_languages),
578	            "evidence_needs": list(self.evidence_needs),
579	        }
580	
581	    # -- research frame (backend routing) -------------------------------------
582	
583	    def to_research_frame(self) -> Any:
584	        """Materialize a champion :class:`ResearchFrame` for backend routing.
585	
586	        ``evidence_needs`` route the need-type registry (primary_literature ->
587	        S2/OpenAlex scholarly adapters = GO FIND journals). Entities/comparators
588	        seed the anchor tokens. Jurisdictions route jurisdiction scopes. Runs the
589	        frame's own ``validate_evidence_needs`` so a routed need is always a valid
590	        enum member; an unroutable source type simply contributes no need (safe
591	        generic fallback), never an error.
592	
593	        Returns ``None`` when the projection carries NOTHING to route (empty
594	        needs AND no entities/jurisdictions) so the caller keeps the champion
595	        ``research_frame=None`` (byte-identical) path.
596	        """
597	        if not (self.evidence_needs or self.entities or self.jurisdictions
598	                or self.comparators):
599	            return None
600	        from src.polaris_graph.planning.research_planner import (  # noqa: PLC0415
601	            ResearchFrame,
602	            validate_evidence_needs,
603	            validate_jurisdiction_shapes,
604	        )
605	        # validate/normalize; drop any that fail SHAPE rather than raise (compile
606	        # view is fail-open — a bad token contributes nothing, never aborts).
607	        try:
608	            needs = validate_evidence_needs(self.evidence_needs)
609	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
610	            needs = [n for n in self.evidence_needs
611	                     if _safe_need(n)]
612	        try:
613	            juris = validate_jurisdiction_shapes(self.jurisdictions)
614	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
615	            juris = []
616	        return ResearchFrame(
617	            entities=list(self.entities),
618	            comparators=list(self.comparators),
619	            constraints=list(self.hard_scope_terms),
620	            evidence_needs=needs,
621	            jurisdictions=juris,
622	        )
623	
624	    def to_protocol(self, *, base_question: str = "") -> Optional[dict[str, Any]]:
625	        """The anchor protocol for ``validate_amplified_queries`` (from the frame).
626	
627	        ``None`` when there is no frame to anchor (keeps the champion path)."""
628	        frame = self.to_research_frame()
629	        if frame is None:
630	            return None
631	        anchor = (base_question or self.original_prompt or "").strip()
632	        return frame.to_anchor_protocol(anchor)
633	
634	    # -- the mechanized 997-guard (planning level) ----------------------------
635	
636	    def candidate_query_count(self, *, base_question: str = "") -> int:
637	        """Count the candidate query/evidence lanes this projection ADDS.
638	
639	        The 997-guard: a caller asserts ``gate_count >= no_gate_count`` so the
640	        gate path can NEVER reduce the candidate query/evidence-need count vs the
641	        no-gate path — at PLANNING level, before any fetch. Counts distinct
642	        amplified queries plus routed evidence needs (each a discovery lane)."""
643	        return (
644	            len(self.to_amplified_queries(base_question=base_question))
645	            + len(self.evidence_needs)
646	        )
647	
648	    # -- typed retrieval policy (spec item 2) ---------------------------------
649	
650	    def to_retrieval_policy(self) -> RetrievalPolicy:
651	        """Compile the typed, hash-stamped :class:`RetrievalPolicy`.
652	
653	        Walks the SAME pinned contract the projection was built from and reads its
654	        scope terms into declarative predicates: allowed/excluded source kinds, a
655	        publication-date interval, languages, named in/exclusions, a quality
656	        profile ref, and per-predicate hard/soft force. Stamps the contract's
657	        canonical ``contract_sha256`` as ``contract_hash`` so retrieval + audit can
658	        assert hash identity with the compiler. Pure + deterministic; an empty
659	        contract compiles an empty policy (``is_empty()`` -> caller applies no
660	        filter, byte-identical).
661	        """
662	        from src.polaris_graph.planning.planning_gate_schema import sha256_of
663	
664	        allowed: list[str] = []
665	        # Seed the NEGATIVE + named + opaque predicate sets from the projection
666	        # buckets already computed by ``from_contract_and_plan`` (one routing truth:
667	        # exclusions/named/opaque were classified there, never re-derived from a
668	        # drifting dimension-name list — F6). The contract walk below only fills the
669	        # positive/date/language/quality predicates + per-predicate force.
670	        excluded: list[str] = list(self.excluded_scope_terms)
671	        languages: list[str] = list(self.hard_languages)
672	        named_incl: list[str] = list(self.named_inclusions)
673	        named_excl: list[str] = list(self.named_exclusions)
674	        opaque: list[str] = list(self.opaque_eligibility_terms)
675	        quality_profile: Optional[str] = None
676	        date_from: Optional[str] = None
677	        date_to: Optional[str] = None
678	        force: dict[str, str] = {}
679	
680	        def _add(lst: list[str], v: Any) -> None:
681	            s = str(v or "").strip()
682	            if s and s.lower() not in {x.lower() for x in lst}:
683	                lst.append(s)
684	
685	        for term in self.contract.scope:
686	            dim = _norm(term.dimension)
687	            val = term.value
688	            if val in (None, "", [], {}):
689	                continue
690	            vals = val if isinstance(val, (list, tuple)) else [val]
691	            is_hard = term.force == FORCE_HARD
692	
693	            if dim == "scope.source_types":
694	                for v in vals:
695	                    _add(allowed, v)
696	                force.setdefault("allowed_source_kinds", "hard" if is_hard else "soft")
697	            elif dim in _EXCLUSION_DIMENSIONS:
698	                # values already captured in excluded_scope_terms; record force.
699	                force.setdefault("excluded_source_kinds", "hard" if is_hard else "soft")
700	            elif dim in _NAMED_EXCLUSION_DIMENSIONS:
701	                # values already captured in named_excl; record force. A named
702	                # exclusion is always hard downstream (identity-enforced).
703	                force.setdefault("named_exclusions", "hard")
704	            elif dim in _OPAQUE_DIMENSIONS:
705	                # F2: opaque terms are a hard eligibility predicate to be JUDGED
706	                # later — never a query string / positive facet. Record force only.
707	                force.setdefault("opaque_eligibility", "hard" if is_hard else "soft")
708	            elif dim in ("scope.date", "scope.dates", "scope.published_after",
709	                         "scope.recency"):
710	                iso = _year_to_iso_from(vals[0])
711	                if iso and (date_from is None or iso > date_from):
712	                    date_from = iso
713	                force.setdefault("date", "hard" if is_hard else "soft")
714	            elif dim in ("scope.published_before", "scope.date_to"):
715	                iso = _year_to_iso_from(vals[0])
716	                if iso:
717	                    date_to = iso
718	            elif dim in _LANGUAGE_DIMENSIONS:
719	                if is_hard:
720	                    for v in vals:
721	                        _add(languages, v)
722	                    force.setdefault("languages", "hard")
723	            elif dim == "scope.source_quality":
724	                quality_profile = str(vals[0]).strip() or None
725	                force.setdefault("quality_profile", "hard" if is_hard else "soft")
726	            elif dim in ("scope.named_sources", "scope.include_sources",
727	                         "scope.inclusions"):
728	                for v in vals:
729	                    _add(named_incl, v)
730	                force.setdefault("named_inclusions", "hard" if is_hard else "soft")
731	
732	        contract_hash = ""
733	        try:
734	            contract_hash = sha256_of(self.contract.to_dict())
735	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — hash is best-effort provenance, never fatal
736	            contract_hash = ""
737	
738	        return RetrievalPolicy(
739	            allowed_source_kinds=allowed,
740	            excluded_source_kinds=excluded,
741	            date_from=date_from,
742	            date_to=date_to,
743	            languages=languages,
744	            named_inclusions=named_incl,
745	            named_exclusions=named_excl,
746	            quality_profile=quality_profile,
747	            opaque_eligibility=opaque,
748	            predicate_force=force,
749	            contract_hash=contract_hash,
750	        )
751	
752	
753	def _safe_need(n: Any) -> bool:
754	    from src.polaris_graph.planning.research_planner import EVIDENCE_NEEDS  # noqa: PLC0415
755	    return _norm(n) in EVIDENCE_NEEDS
756	
757	
758	def _intent_query_texts(qi: QueryIntent) -> list[str]:
759	    """The query string(s) a single :class:`QueryIntent` contributes.
760	
761	    Prefers a concept+required-term join; falls back to alternate phrasings, then
762	    the raw thread question via concepts. Always returns at least one non-empty
763	    string when the intent carries any text."""
764	    parts: list[str] = []
765	    core = " ".join([*qi.concepts, *qi.required_terms]).strip()
766	    if core:
767	        parts.append(core)
768	    for alt in qi.alternate_phrasings:
769	        alt = (alt or "").strip()
770	        if alt:
771	            parts.append(alt)
772	    if not parts:
773	        # nothing structured — fall back to any single concept/phrasing.
774	        for cand in [*qi.concepts, *qi.alternate_phrasings]:
775	            if (cand or "").strip():
776	                parts.append(cand.strip())
777	                break
778	    return parts
779	
780	
781	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
782	# Compilation from a pinned artifact / contract
783	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
784	
785	# Scope dimensions whose VALUES are folded into query text as scope terms.
786	_SCOPE_TEXT_DIMENSIONS: tuple[str, ...] = (
787	    "scope.source_types",
788	    "scope.source_quality",  # high-quality/peer-reviewed steers the citable menu
789	    "scope.domains",
790	    "scope.geographies",
791	    # NOTE: ``scope.prohibited`` is DELIBERATELY absent. A prohibition is a
792	    # NEGATIVE predicate — it must NEVER be folded into positive query text (the
793	    # pre-Phase-C bug where "no blogs" became a "blogs" query suffix). It is
794	    # routed to ``excluded_scope_terms`` and surfaced as a negative eligibility
795	    # predicate instead. See ``_EXCLUSION_DIMENSIONS``.
796	)
797	# Scope dimensions whose VALUES are NEGATIVE predicates (exclusions). Never query
798	# text; only a masking eligibility predicate a caller applies to candidate metadata.
799	# ``scope.excluded_source_kinds`` is the canonical F1 target ("no blogs" / "do not
800	# cite blogs"); the legacy aliases are kept so an older persisted contract routes.
801	_EXCLUSION_DIMENSIONS: tuple[str, ...] = (
802	    "scope.excluded_source_kinds",
803	    "scope.prohibited",
804	    "scope.excluded_source_types",
805	    "scope.exclusions",
806	)
807	# NEGATIVE named-source predicates ("don't use Reuters") — routed to
808	# ``RetrievalPolicy.named_exclusions`` + an ``op=exclude`` named entry, never query text.
809	_NAMED_EXCLUSION_DIMENSIONS: tuple[str, ...] = (
810	    "scope.excluded_sources",
811	    "scope.exclude_sources",
812	)
813	# OPAQUE dimensions: a hard OPAQUE clause is a first-class eligibility predicate
814	# (judged post-fetch against each source), NEVER positive query text. (F2.)
815	_OPAQUE_DIMENSIONS: tuple[str, ...] = (
816	    "scope.opaque",
817	    "content.opaque",
818	)
819	_LANGUAGE_DIMENSIONS: tuple[str, ...] = (
820	    "scope.source_languages",
821	    "scope.languages",
822	)
823	
824	
825	def from_contract_and_plan(
826	    contract: ResearchContract,
827	    plan: ResearchExecutionPlan,
828	    *,
829	    original_prompt: str = "",
830	) -> RetrievalProjection:
831	    """Compile a :class:`RetrievalProjection` from a contract + plan.
832	
833	    Pure + deterministic. Walks the contract's scope + content terms and the
834	    plan's query intents to derive routing tokens. Fail-open: a degraded/empty
835	    contract yields an empty projection (the caller keeps the champion path).
836	    """
837	    evidence_needs: list[str] = []
838	    jurisdictions: list[str] = []
839	    entities: list[str] = []
840	    comparators: list[str] = []
841	    hard_scope_terms: list[str] = []
842	    soft_scope_terms: list[str] = []
843	    hard_languages: list[str] = []
844	    excluded_scope_terms: list[str] = []
845	    named_exclusions: list[str] = []
846	    named_inclusions: list[str] = []
847	    opaque_eligibility_terms: list[str] = []
848	
849	    def _dedup_add(lst: list[str], val: Any) -> None:
850	        s = str(val or "").strip()
851	        if not s:
852	            return
853	        if s.lower() not in {x.lower() for x in lst}:
854	            lst.append(s)
855	
856	    for term in contract.scope:
857	        dim = _norm(term.dimension)
858	        val = term.value
859	        if val in (None, "", [], {}):
860	            continue
861	        vals = val if isinstance(val, (list, tuple)) else [val]
862	
863	        # EXCLUSIONS FIRST: a prohibition is a NEGATIVE predicate. Route its values
864	        # to the excluded bucket and CONTINUE — it must NEVER reach the positive
865	        # query-text fold below (the pre-Phase-C "no blogs" -> "blogs" bug).
866	        if dim in _EXCLUSION_DIMENSIONS:
867	            for v in vals:
868	                _dedup_add(excluded_scope_terms, v)
869	            continue
870	
871	        # NEGATIVE NAMED predicates ("don't use Reuters"): a named exclusion, never
872	        # query text. CONTINUE so it cannot fold into hard_scope_terms below.
873	        if dim in _NAMED_EXCLUSION_DIMENSIONS:
874	            for v in vals:
875	                _dedup_add(named_exclusions, v)
876	            continue
877	
878	        # NAMED INCLUSIONS ("Use Reuters and AP"): a retrieval boost, surfaced as a
879	        # named inclusion. Kept out of hard_scope_terms — a named source is a source
880	        # IDENTITY predicate, not a generic scope adjective folded into query text.
881	        if dim in ("scope.named_sources", "scope.include_sources", "scope.inclusions"):
882	            for v in vals:
883	                _dedup_add(named_inclusions, v)
884	            continue
885	
886	        # OPAQUE (F2): a hard un-normalized deontic clause. It is a POST-FETCH
887	        # eligibility predicate, NEVER positive query text. CONTINUE so the raw
888	        # clause text can never land in hard_scope_terms and be suffixed onto every
889	        # query (which would steer discovery toward what an exclusion forbids). An
890	        # opaque term whose operator is NOT_IN (an exclude-cue clause) routes to the
891	        # NEGATIVE bucket so it masks rather than merely being judged.
892	        if dim in _OPAQUE_DIMENSIONS:
893	            op = _norm(getattr(term, "operator", ""))
894	            for v in vals:
895	                if op == "not_in":
896	                    _dedup_add(excluded_scope_terms, v)
897	                else:
898	                    _dedup_add(opaque_eligibility_terms, v)
899	            continue
900	
901	        if dim in _LANGUAGE_DIMENSIONS:
902	            if term.force == FORCE_HARD:
903	                for v in vals:
904	                    _dedup_add(hard_languages, v)
905	            continue
906	
907	        # source_types route evidence needs (the GO-FIND-journals map).
908	        if dim == "scope.source_types":
909	            for v in vals:
910	                need = source_type_to_need(v)
911	                if need:
912	                    _dedup_add(evidence_needs, need)
913	
914	        if dim in ("scope.jurisdictions", "scope.geographies"):
915	            for v in vals:
916	                _dedup_add(jurisdictions, str(v).strip().upper()[:4])
917	
918	        # fold the value into the hard/soft scope text bucket.
919	        bucket = hard_scope_terms if term.force == FORCE_HARD else soft_scope_terms
920	        if dim in _SCOPE_TEXT_DIMENSIONS or term.force == FORCE_HARD:
921	            for v in vals:
922	                _dedup_add(bucket, v)
923	
924	    # entities + comparators from the content spec (sub-entity discovery breadth).
925	    for ent in contract.content_terms:
926	        if _norm(ent.dimension).startswith("content.entit") and ent.value:
927	            _dedup_add(entities, ent.value)
928	    for cov in contract.coverage:
929	        if cov.kind in ("entity",) and cov.statement.value:
930	            _dedup_add(entities, cov.statement.value)
931	        if cov.kind in ("comparison",):
932	            for c in cov.compare_on:
933	                if c.value:
934	                    _dedup_add(comparators, c.value)
935	
936	    return RetrievalProjection(
937	        contract=contract,
938	        plan=plan,
939	        original_prompt=original_prompt,
940	        evidence_needs=evidence_needs,
941	        jurisdictions=jurisdictions,
942	        entities=entities,
943	        comparators=comparators,
944	        hard_scope_terms=hard_scope_terms,
945	        soft_scope_terms=soft_scope_terms,
946	        hard_languages=hard_languages,
947	        excluded_scope_terms=excluded_scope_terms,
948	        named_exclusions=named_exclusions,
949	        named_inclusions=named_inclusions,
950	        opaque_eligibility_terms=opaque_eligibility_terms,
951	    )
952	
953	
954	def from_artifact(artifact: PlanningGateArtifact) -> RetrievalProjection:
955	    """Compile a :class:`RetrievalProjection` from a pinned artifact."""
956	    return from_contract_and_plan(
957	        artifact.contract,
958	        artifact.plan,
959	        original_prompt=artifact.original_prompt,
960	    )
961	
962	
963	def from_artifact_with_champion_breadth(
964	    artifact: PlanningGateArtifact,
965	    research_plan: Any = None,
966	    *,
967	    original_prompt: str = "",
968	) -> RetrievalProjection:
969	    """The live FS seam projection: the CONTRACT drives scope, the champion plan
970	    ADDS breadth.
971	
972	    Compiles ``from_artifact(artifact)`` (so the pinned contract's hard scope
973	    terms, evidence-need routing, entities and languages are authoritative), then
974	    ADDITIVELY folds the champion ``research_plan``'s ``sub_queries`` into the
975	    amplified frontier and its ``frame`` routing tokens (entities / evidence needs
976	    / jurisdictions / comparators) into the projection — never dropping a lane.
977	
978	    Spec item 1: ``from_champion_plan`` shipped an EMPTY contract (scope never
979	    reached retrieval); this keeps the champion's breadth but lets the CONTRACT
980	    own scope. ``research_plan=None`` degrades to a plain :func:`from_artifact`.
981	    """
982	    proj = from_artifact(artifact)
983	    if research_plan is None:
984	        if original_prompt and not proj.original_prompt:
985	            proj.original_prompt = original_prompt
986	        return proj
987	
988	    def _dedup_extend(lst: list[str], vals: Any) -> None:
989	        seen = {x.lower() for x in lst}
990	        for v in vals or []:
991	            s = str(v or "").strip()
992	            if s and s.lower() not in seen:
993	                seen.add(s.lower())
994	                lst.append(s)
995	
996	    proj.extra_amplified_queries = list(
997	        getattr(research_plan, "sub_queries", []) or []
998	    )
999	
1000	    frame = getattr(research_plan, "frame", None)
1001	    if frame is not None:
1002	        _dedup_extend(proj.entities, getattr(frame, "entities", []))
1003	        _dedup_extend(proj.comparators, getattr(frame, "comparators", []))
1004	        _dedup_extend(proj.jurisdictions, getattr(frame, "jurisdictions", []))
1005	        _dedup_extend(proj.evidence_needs, getattr(frame, "evidence_needs", []))
1006	
1007	    if original_prompt and not proj.original_prompt:
1008	        proj.original_prompt = original_prompt
1009	    return proj
1010	
1011	
1012	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
1013	# Transitional adapter: champion ResearchPlan -> projection (the FS seam fix)
1014	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
1015	
1016	
1017	class _ChampionPlanProjection(RetrievalProjection):
1018	    """A projection whose amplified queries ARE the champion plan's ``sub_queries``.
1019	
1020	    S2 seam fix (design §5): the FS-Researcher branch in ``run_one_query``
1021	    currently passes only ``_clean_question`` and DISCARDS
1022	    ``_research_plan.sub_queries``. This adapter wraps a champion
1023	    ``research_planner.ResearchPlan`` as a :class:`RetrievalProjection` so the FS
1024	    frontier is seeded with those sub-queries (the gate's pre-retrieval lanes)
1025	    plus the frame's ``evidence_needs`` routing — BEFORE any fetch. Until the full
1026	    gate artifact is wired into ``run_one_query`` (a later step), this carries the
1027	    already-computed champion plan into the FS query frontier instead of throwing
1028	    it away. It NEVER drops a source; it only ADDS the sub-queries the FS branch
1029	    was discarding, and only when ``PG_GATE`` is ON.
1030	    """
1031	
1032	    def __init__(self, research_plan: Any, *, original_prompt: str = "") -> None:
1033	        frame = getattr(research_plan, "frame", None)
1034	        sub_queries = list(getattr(research_plan, "sub_queries", []) or [])
1035	        super().__init__(
1036	            contract=ResearchContract(),
1037	            plan=ResearchExecutionPlan(),
1038	            original_prompt=original_prompt,
1039	            evidence_needs=list(getattr(frame, "evidence_needs", []) or []),
1040	            jurisdictions=list(getattr(frame, "jurisdictions", []) or []),
1041	            entities=list(getattr(frame, "entities", []) or []),
1042	            comparators=list(getattr(frame, "comparators", []) or []),
1043	        )
1044	        self._sub_queries = sub_queries
1045	        self._frame = frame
1046	
1047	    def to_amplified_queries(self, *, base_question: str = "") -> list[str]:
1048	        out: list[str] = []
1049	        seen: set[str] = set()
1050	        for q in self._sub_queries:
1051	            qs = (q or "").strip()
1052	            if qs and qs.lower() not in seen:
1053	                seen.add(qs.lower())
1054	                out.append(qs)
1055	        return out
1056	
1057	    def to_research_frame(self) -> Any:
1058	        return self._frame
1059	
1060	    def to_protocol(self, *, base_question: str = "") -> Optional[dict[str, Any]]:
1061	        if self._frame is None:
1062	            return None
1063	        try:
1064	            return self._frame.to_anchor_protocol(
1065	                (base_question or self.original_prompt or "").strip()
1066	            )
1067	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
1068	            return None
1069	
1070	
1071	def from_champion_plan(
1072	    research_plan: Any, *, original_prompt: str = ""
1073	) -> Optional[RetrievalProjection]:
1074	    """Adapt a champion ``ResearchPlan`` into a projection, or ``None`` if it has
1075	    no usable sub-queries (keep the champion FS path)."""
1076	    if research_plan is None:
1077	        return None
1078	    if not list(getattr(research_plan, "sub_queries", []) or []):
1079	        return None
1080	    return _ChampionPlanProjection(research_plan, original_prompt=original_prompt)
1081

1	"""CRAG Retrieval Pipeline (v2 Layer 1).
2	
3	Replaces v1's 126-call LLM analyzer with $0 local embeddings.
4	Pipeline: Fetch -> Chunk -> Enrich -> Dedup -> Score -> Gate -> Register
5	
6	Modules:
7	    crag_retriever       Main orchestrator (replaces analyze node)
8	    source_registry      Global Source Registry (SRC-NNN, Loophole L1)
9	    section_blueprint    Cross-section evidence assignment (Loophole L5)
10	    citation_normalizer  Compound citation splitting + SRC->number resolution
11	    pooled_embedder      Mean-pooling sub-chunking for 256-token model limit (Fix R4-#1)
12	    fetch_limiter        Semaphore + retry for rate-limited URL fetching (Fix R4-#4)
13	    verify_context       Citation-priority evidence selection for verifier (Fix R4-#5)
14	    llm_throttle         LLM concurrency limiter for TPM burst prevention (Fix R5-#3)
15	    verify_schemas       CoT verification schema to prevent sycophancy (Fix R5-#4)
16	    synthesis_prompts    Section writer prompt with phantom figure ban (Fix R5-#5)
17	"""
