content.content:
1	"""I-cap-002 feature 3/4 (#1060): harvest DISCOVERED URLs from an agentic-search result + a pure
2	seed-URL evidence merge core.
3	
4	The agentic loop (``agents/searcher.execute_agentic_search``) is used in the benchmark for **URL
5	DISCOVERY ONLY**. Its LLM-written page summaries (``agentic_research_notebook``) must NEVER become
6	evidence — POLARIS's core invariant is verbatim-span faithfulness, and a model paraphrase masquerading
7	as a ``direct_quote`` would be a fabrication. So we read ONLY the discovered URLs here; the discovered
8	URLs are then fetched **verbatim** by ``live_retriever.run_live_retrieval(seed_urls=…, seed_only=True)``
9	and verified by strict_verify + the 4-role seam, exactly like the rest of the corpus.
10	
11	Pure, stdlib-light functions (no network, no LLM, no new dependency):
12	- ``harvest_agentic_url_records`` — BUG-8 (I-arch-006 #1262): ordered, canonical-deduped, capped list
13	  of discovered candidates as ``HarvestedUrl`` records carrying the URL **plus** the search-engine
14	  ``title``/``snippet`` ANCHOR so a downstream topical-relevance screen has text to screen on. The
15	  snippet/title is search-API result metadata (the search-engine excerpt, NOT a POLARIS LLM
16	  paraphrase) used ONLY as a topical-screen WEIGHT input — it is NEVER promoted into evidence.
17	- ``harvest_agentic_urls`` — the URL-ONLY view (a thin wrapper over the record harvester): an ordered,
18	  canonical-deduped, capped list of discovered URLs (originals), for callers that want just URLs.
19	- ``harvest_storm_urls`` — BB-006 (#1171): the same URL-ONLY harvest applied to a STORM-interview
20	  result's ``web_results`` / ``academic_results`` streams (the 478/540 interview search-result URLs the
21	  benchmark previously discarded). NEVER reads STORM's synthesized answer/key_findings/snippet text.
22	- ``merge_seed_url_evidence`` — the deepener's dedup-by-URL + global evidence-id renumber core, factored
23	  out so it is unit-testable (and a future PR can repoint the deepener at it).
24	"""
25	
26	from __future__ import annotations
27	
28	from dataclasses import dataclass
29	from typing import Any
30	
31	from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.saturation import canonical_source_url
32	
33	
34	@dataclass(frozen=True)
35	class HarvestedUrl:
36	    """A discovered candidate URL carried together with its search-engine ANCHOR (BUG-8, #1262).
37	
38	    The ``title``/``snippet`` come from the upstream SEARCH-API result record (e.g.
39	    ``searcher.execute_agentic_search`` populates ``snippet`` from the result's abstract/title) — they
40	    are the search engine's own excerpt, NOT a POLARIS-LLM paraphrase. They are carried here ONLY so a
41	    later topical-relevance screen can WEIGHT a candidate (does it look on-topic?) without re-fetching.
42	
43	    FAITHFULNESS (why this is safe): the anchor is a topical-screen WEIGHT input, never evidence. The
44	    candidate URL is still fetched VERBATIM by ``live_retriever.run_live_retrieval(seed_urls=…,
45	    seed_only=True)`` and every claim is gated by strict_verify + the 4-role seam exactly as before —
46	    promoting the snippet into a ``direct_quote`` would be a fabrication and is NOT done anywhere here.
47	    Per §-1.3 DNA this ENABLES a future WEIGHT-not-drop screen; it adds NO drop/filter/cap of its own.
48	    """
49	
50	    url: str
51	    title: str = ""
52	    snippet: str = ""
53	    source_stream: str = ""  # "web_results" | "academic_results" | "agentic_url_accumulator"
54	
55	
56	def harvest_agentic_url_records(
57	    agentic_result: dict[str, Any] | None,
58	    cap: int = 200,
59	) -> list[HarvestedUrl]:
60	    """Return the discovered candidates from an ``execute_agentic_search`` result as ``HarvestedUrl``
61	    records — the URL **plus** its search-engine ``title``/``snippet`` anchor (BUG-8, I-arch-006 #1262).
62	
63	    THE BUG (BUG-8 half-2): ``harvest_agentic_urls`` returned URL-ONLY, so a downstream topical-relevance
64	    screen had nothing to screen on. THE FIX: also carry a short anchor (title + snippet, from the search
65	    result the harvester ALREADY iterates) so a later WEIGHT-not-drop topical screen can judge relevance
66	    without a re-fetch. WHY FAITHFULNESS IS SAFE: the anchor is search-API metadata used only as a screen
67	    WEIGHT input; it is NEVER promoted into evidence (URLs are still fetched VERBATIM and gated by
68	    strict_verify + the 4-role seam). This harvester adds NO drop/filter/cap of its own (§-1.3 DNA).
69	
70	    Order-preserving and DETERMINISTIC: harvests from the ordered ``web_results`` then ``academic_results``
71	    streams first (the authoritative, complete, append-ordered streams — incl. post-loop DDG / citation-
72	    chase URLs), then supplements with any ``agentic_url_accumulator`` entries not already seen.
73	    De-duplicates by ``canonical_source_url`` (scheme/tracking-stripped) but RETURNS the original fetchable
74	    URL (canonical strings would hurt the live fetch yield) and keeps the FIRST record's anchor. Returns at
75	    most ``cap`` records. NEVER reads ``agentic_research_notebook`` summaries (an LLM paraphrase). Robust to
76	    missing/empty keys (returns ``[]`` rather than raising). ``cap <= 0`` → ``[]``.
77	    """
78	    if cap <= 0:
79	        return []
80	    result = agentic_result or {}
81	    seen_canonical: set[str] = set()
82	    out: list[HarvestedUrl] = []
83	
84	    def _consider(raw: Any, *, title: Any = "", snippet: Any = "", stream: str = "") -> None:
85	        if len(out) >= cap:
86	            return
87	        url = (raw or "").strip() if isinstance(raw, str) else ""
88	        if not url:
89	            return
90	        try:
91	            key = canonical_source_url(url) or url
92	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — a malformed URL must not abort discovery
93	            key = url
94	        if key in seen_canonical:
95	            return
96	        seen_canonical.add(key)
97	        out.append(
98	            HarvestedUrl(
99	                url=url,
100	                title=title.strip() if isinstance(title, str) else "",
101	                snippet=snippet.strip() if isinstance(snippet, str) else "",
102	                source_stream=stream,
103	            )
104	        )
105	
106	    # Ordered result streams first (deterministic, complete). Anchor = search-engine title/snippet.
107	    for stream_key in ("web_results", "academic_results"):
108	        for rec in result.get(stream_key, []) or []:
109	            if len(out) >= cap:
110	                return out
111	            if isinstance(rec, dict):
112	                _consider(
113	                    rec.get("url"),
114	                    title=rec.get("title", ""),
115	                    snippet=rec.get("snippet", ""),
116	                    stream=stream_key,
117	                )
118	    # Supplement with the accumulator (a URL-only subset — no per-URL anchor available there).
119	    for url in result.get("agentic_url_accumulator", []) or []:
120	        if len(out) >= cap:
121	            return out
122	        _consider(url, stream="agentic_url_accumulator")
123	    return out
124	
125	
126	def harvest_agentic_urls(
127	    agentic_result: dict[str, Any] | None,
128	    cap: int = 200,
129	) -> list[str]:
130	    """Return ONLY the discovered URLs from an ``execute_agentic_search`` result (URL-only view).
131	
132	    Backward-compatible thin wrapper over ``harvest_agentic_url_records`` (BUG-8, I-arch-006 #1262): same
133	    ordered, canonical-deduped, capped discovery, but projected down to the original fetchable URL strings
134	    that existing callers (``run_honest_sweep_r3.py``) already consume. NEVER reads
135	    ``agentic_research_notebook`` / summaries. Robust to missing/empty keys (returns ``[]`` rather than
136	    raising). ``cap <= 0`` → ``[]``.
137	    """
138	    return [rec.url for rec in harvest_agentic_url_records(agentic_result, cap=cap)]
139	
140	
141	def harvest_storm_urls(
142	    storm_result: dict[str, Any] | None,
143	    cap: int = 200,
144	) -> list[str]:
145	    """BB-006 (I-beatboth-fix-000 #1171): return ONLY the discovered URLs from a STORM
146	    ``run_storm_interviews`` result — the EXACT analogue of ``harvest_agentic_urls``.
147	
148	    STORM grounds its multi-perspective interviews in REAL internet search results. Those
149	    URLs (``storm_result["web_results"]`` then ``["academic_results"]``, each a dict with
150	    ``url``/``title``/``snippet``) are legitimate candidate sources — but the run previously
151	    DISCARDED them, only re-using STORM's synthesized interview QUESTIONS as query strings.
152	
153	    HARD URL-ONLY CONTRACT (faithfulness): this reads ONLY ``rec["url"]``. It NEVER reads the
154	    STORM-synthesized ``answer`` / ``key_findings`` / outline / conversation text, and NEVER
155	    the per-record ``snippet`` — promoting any LLM-synthesized STORM text into the evidence
156	    pool would be a fabrication path. The harvested URLs are then fetched VERBATIM by
157	    ``live_retriever.run_live_retrieval(seed_urls=…, seed_only=True)`` and gated by
158	    strict_verify + the 4-role seam exactly like every other candidate (empty direct_quote).
159	
160	    Order-preserving + DETERMINISTIC; de-duplicates by ``canonical_source_url`` but RETURNS the
161	    original fetchable URL. Returns at most ``cap`` URLs. Robust to missing/empty keys (returns
162	    ``[]`` rather than raising). ``cap <= 0`` -> ``[]``.
163	    """
164	    if cap <= 0:
165	        return []
166	    result = storm_result or {}
167	    seen_canonical: set[str] = set()
168	    out: list[str] = []
169	
170	    def _consider(raw: Any) -> None:
171	        if len(out) >= cap:
172	            return
173	        url = (raw or "").strip() if isinstance(raw, str) else ""
174	        if not url:
175	            return
176	        try:
177	            key = canonical_source_url(url) or url
178	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — a malformed URL must not abort discovery
179	            key = url
180	        if key in seen_canonical:
181	            return
182	        seen_canonical.add(key)
183	        out.append(url)
184	
185	    # Ordered result streams (deterministic). URL key ONLY — never answer/key_findings/snippet.
186	    for stream_key in ("web_results", "academic_results"):
187	        for rec in result.get(stream_key, []) or []:
188	            if len(out) >= cap:
189	                return out
190	            if isinstance(rec, dict):
191	                _consider(rec.get("url"))
192	    return out
193	
194	
195	def merge_seed_url_evidence(
196	    staged_sources: list[Any],
197	    staged_rows: list[dict[str, Any]],
198	    new_sources: list[Any],
199	    new_rows: list[dict[str, Any]],
200	) -> tuple[list[Any], list[dict[str, Any]], int, int]:
201	    """Dedup-by-URL source merge + global evidence-id renumber (the deepener's accepted-source core).
202	
203	    - ``new_sources`` whose ``.url`` is not already among ``staged_sources`` are appended; their URLs
204	      form the ACCEPTED set.
205	    - Only ``new_rows`` whose source URL (``source_url`` or ``url``) is in the ACCEPTED set are
206	      appended — so a duplicate source contributes NO rows (no evidence_row_count inflation).
207	    - Appended rows get a fresh ``evidence_id`` = ``ev_{base + i:03d}`` from ``len(staged_rows)`` so ids
208	      never collide/overwrite.
209	
210	    Returns ``(merged_sources, merged_rows, accepted_source_count, accepted_row_count)``. Operates on
211	    COPIES; the inputs are not mutated. No I/O.
212	    """
213	    merged_sources = list(staged_sources)
214	    seen_urls = {getattr(s, "url", None) for s in merged_sources}
215	    accepted_src_urls: set[str] = set()
216	    accepted_sources = 0
217	    for src in new_sources:
218	        url = getattr(src, "url", None)
219	        if url and url not in seen_urls:
220	            seen_urls.add(url)
221	            merged_sources.append(src)
222	            accepted_src_urls.add(url)
223	            accepted_sources += 1
224	
225	    merged_rows = list(staged_rows)
226	    base = len(merged_rows)
227	    accepted_rows = 0
228	    for ev in new_rows:
229	        ev_url = (ev.get("source_url") or ev.get("url") or "").strip()
230	        if not ev_url or ev_url not in accepted_src_urls:
231	            continue  # duplicate / not an accepted source — skip (no inflation)
232	        ev = dict(ev)  # copy so the caller's row object is not mutated
233	        ev["evidence_id"] = f"ev_{base + accepted_rows:03d}"
234	        merged_rows.append(ev)
235	        accepted_rows += 1
236	
237	    return merged_sources, merged_rows, accepted_sources, accepted_rows
238

content.text: This file is generally clean in terms of actual code names (functions/classes/vars all descriptive). The docstrings reference internal ticket IDs but those aren't "names" per the task scope.

content.content:
1	"""Per-WORK blocked-reference deny-list (I-deepfix-001 B2, GH #1346).
2	
3	THE ONE legitimate HARD DROP in the pipeline (CLAUDE.md §-1.3). Everything else in
4	POLARIS is WEIGHT-and-CONSOLIDATE — a source is never dropped to hit a number. This
5	module is the single exception, and ONLY because the drop is an EXPLICIT OPERATOR
6	PROHIBITION: a DeepResearch-Bench-II research question can carry a trailing
7	"do-not-view / blocked references" appendix ("...you are not allowed to view the
8	following article and urls: {...}. ...do not quote it."). That appendix names a
9	specific paper (e.g. the Salari et al. "Impacts of generative artificial intelligence
10	on the future of labor market" systematic review, DOI 10.1016/j.chbr.2025.100652,
11	PII S2451958825000673) that the report must NOT view or cite. This is a prohibition,
12	NOT a relevance / credibility / tier call.
13	
14	Without this deny-list the prohibition is never parsed, so the blocked paper is
15	queried across ~6 mirrors (sciencedirect/linkinghub PII, herts researchprofiles,
16	uhra handle, doaj, scilit, doi.org), fetched OK, tiered T1/T2, selected into
17	evidence_for_gen, corpus-approved, and CITED — containment is pure luck.
18	
19	The registry holds FOUR OR'd normalized key-sets, each an independent matching leg so
20	a mirror that is NOT literally in the appendix still gets caught:
21	
22	* ``canonical_urls`` — every URL in the appendix, normalized via ``w3lib.url`` (scheme/
23	  www/query/fragment/trailing-slash stripped, percent-normalized) so case/scheme/query
24	  variants of a listed mirror match.
25	* ``dois`` — Crossref-canonical DOIs (lowercased, ``https?://`` / ``dx.``/``doi.org/`` /
26	  ``doi:`` prefixes stripped). Catches the ``doi.org`` mirror even when it is not the
27	  exact URL listed.
28	* ``publisher_piis`` — Elsevier PII (``S\\d{16}[\\dX]?``) + arXiv ids, INCLUDING the PII
29	  embedded inside sciencedirect / linkinghub URLs. Catches the ``linkinghub`` mirror
30	  (a different URL carrying the SAME PII) even when it is not in the appendix.
31	* ``title_keys`` — normalized-title fuzzy match (>= ``TITLE_FUZZY_THRESHOLD``). Catches a
32	  mirror (semanticscholar, an institutional repository, etc.) by the fetched page TITLE
33	  when its URL carries neither the listed URL, the DOI, nor the PII. HIGH threshold so a
34	  legitimate same-topic-different-paper is NOT over-blocked.
35	
36	Pure / no network. FAIL-OPEN on any build error (a malformed question must never crash
37	the run — the registry is then empty and ``is_blocked`` is always ``(False, "")``).
38	
39	Kill-switch ``PG_BLOCKED_REFERENCE_DENYLIST`` (default ON). OFF => the registry is empty
40	and every wired seam is byte-identical to the pre-B2 behaviour.
41	
42	The faithfulness engine (strict_verify / NLI / 4-role / provenance / span-grounding) is
43	NEVER touched by this module.
44	"""
45	
46	from __future__ import annotations
47	
48	import hashlib
49	import logging
50	import os
51	import re
52	from dataclasses import dataclass
53	from typing import Any, Mapping
54	
55	from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.injection_appendix import locate_injected_appendix
56	
57	logger = logging.getLogger("polaris_graph.blocked_reference_registry")
58	
59	# --- canonical-URL normalization (w3lib, BSD; already a project dependency) -----------
60	try:  # pragma: no cover - import shim
61	    from w3lib.url import canonicalize_url as _canonicalize_url
62	except Exception:  # pragma: no cover - w3lib absent (should not happen; it is installed)
63	    _canonicalize_url = None  # type: ignore[assignment]
64	
65	# --- title fuzzy backend: rapidfuzz if importable, else stdlib difflib ----------------
66	try:  # pragma: no cover - import shim
67	    from rapidfuzz import fuzz as _rapidfuzz_fuzz
68	except Exception:  # pragma: no cover - rapidfuzz optional
69	    _rapidfuzz_fuzz = None  # type: ignore[assignment]
70	
71	import difflib  # noqa: E402 - stdlib fallback for the fuzzy title leg
72	
73	# --- named constants (LAW VI: no magic numbers) ---------------------------------------
74	_DENYLIST_FLAG = "PG_BLOCKED_REFERENCE_DENYLIST"
75	_OFF_TOKENS = frozenset({"0", "false", "no", "off", "disabled", ""})
76	# Title fuzzy floor. HIGH (>= 0.92) so a same-topic but DIFFERENT paper is not blocked;
77	# a true mirror of the blocked paper carries the same title and scores ~1.0.
78	TITLE_FUZZY_THRESHOLD = 0.92
79	
80	# --- extraction regexes ---------------------------------------------------------------
81	# A URL token inside the appendix (stops at whitespace and the dict/list delimiters the
82	# DRB-II appendix uses: quotes, < > ( ) [ ] { }).
83	_URL_RE = re.compile(r"https?://[^\s'\"<>)\]}]+", re.IGNORECASE)
84	# A Crossref DOI (``10.<registrant>/<suffix>``); suffix stops at the same delimiters.
85	_DOI_RE = re.compile(r"10\.\d{4,9}/[^\s'\"<>)\]}]+", re.IGNORECASE)
86	# Elsevier PII: ``S`` + 16 digits + an optional check char (digit or X).
87	_ELSEVIER_PII_RE = re.compile(r"S\d{16}[\dX]?", re.IGNORECASE)
88	# arXiv id (new-style ``NNNN.NNNNN`` optionally ``vN``), only in an explicit arXiv context
89	# so a bare numeric token is never mistaken for an arXiv id.
90	_ARXIV_RE = re.compile(r"arxiv(?:\.org/abs/|\s*:\s*)(\d{4}\.\d{4,5}(?:v\d+)?)", re.IGNORECASE)
91	# The labelled ``'title': '...'`` field of the appendix dict literal.
92	_TITLE_FIELD_RE = re.compile(r"""['"]title['"]\s*:\s*['"]([^'"]+)['"]""", re.IGNORECASE)
93	# The labelled ``'authors': [...]`` / ``'author': '...'`` field of the appendix dict literal.
94	_AUTHORS_FIELD_RE = re.compile(
95	    r"""['"]authors?['"]\s*:\s*(\[[^\]]*\]|['"][^'"]+['"])""", re.IGNORECASE
96	)
97	_QUOTED_STR_RE = re.compile(r"""['"]([^'"]+)['"]""")
98	
99	# --- DOAJ-id identity leg (I-deepfix-001 P0-1) ----------------------------------------
100	# A DOAJ article id is a 32-hex token. It appears (a) inside a doaj.org/article/<id> URL,
101	# (b) as an explicit ``'doaj_id'`` metadata field / ``DOAJ id: <id>`` mention, and (c) —
102	# crucially — inside a MIRROR repository URL path (library.kab.ac.ug/.../items/<id>). The
103	# BUILD side (registry construction) is CONSERVATIVE: it registers a 32-hex only from an
104	# explicit doaj.org/article URL or a labelled doaj_id field, so a random hex token in the
105	# appendix is never mistaken for a blocked id. The MATCH side (a candidate under test) is
106	# PERMISSIVE: it grabs any 32-hex from the candidate URL/metadata, which is safe because a
107	# hit only fires when that id is in the (conservatively-built) registry set.
108	_DOAJ_ARTICLE_RE = re.compile(r"doaj\.org/article/([0-9a-f]{32})", re.IGNORECASE)
109	_DOAJ_ID_FIELD_RE = re.compile(
110	    r"""doaj[_\s]?id['"\s:]+([0-9a-f]{32})""", re.IGNORECASE
111	)
112	_HEX32_RE = re.compile(r"[0-9a-f]{32}", re.IGNORECASE)
113	_BARE_HEX32_RE = re.compile(r"^[0-9a-f]{32}$", re.IGNORECASE)
114	_SURNAME_ALNUM_RE = re.compile(r"[^a-z0-9]+")
115	
116	
117	def _normalize_doaj_id(value: str) -> str:
118	    """Canonical (lowercased) DOAJ article id from a URL, a ``doaj.org/article/<id>``
119	    link, or a bare 32-hex metadata value. Empty when none is parseable. Never raises."""
120	    if not value:
121	        return ""
122	    v = str(value).strip().lower()
123	    if not v:
124	        return ""
125	    m = _DOAJ_ARTICLE_RE.search(v)
126	    if m:
127	        return m.group(1).lower()
128	    if _BARE_HEX32_RE.match(v):
129	        return v
130	    return ""
131	
132	
133	def _extract_doaj_id_candidates(url: str) -> set[str]:
134	    """MATCH-side DOAJ ids from a candidate URL: the explicit doaj.org/article id AND any
135	    32-hex segment in the path (a mirror repository id). Permissive on purpose — a hit only
136	    fires against the conservatively-built registry set. Never raises ('' on bad input)."""
137	    out: set[str] = set()
138	    if not url:
139	        return out
140	    low = str(url).strip().lower()
141	    m = _DOAJ_ARTICLE_RE.search(low)
142	    if m:
143	        out.add(m.group(1).lower())
144	    for tok in _HEX32_RE.findall(low):
145	        out.add(tok.lower())
146	    return out
147	
148	
149	def _first_author_surname(authors: "Any") -> str:
150	    """Order-robust surname of the FIRST author. Handles a list of author strings or a
151	    single string, and both ``'Surname, Given'`` and ``'Given Surname'`` name orders.
152	    Empty when no author is parseable. Never raises."""
153	    first = ""
154	    if isinstance(authors, (list, tuple)):
155	        for a in authors:
156	            if a is not None and str(a).strip():
157	                first = str(a).strip()
158	                break
159	    elif authors is not None:
160	        first = str(authors).strip()
161	    if not first:
162	        return ""
163	    if "," in first:
164	        surname = first.split(",", 1)[0]
165	    else:
166	        parts = first.split()
167	        surname = parts[-1] if parts else ""
168	    return _SURNAME_ALNUM_RE.sub("", surname.lower()).strip()
169	
170	
171	def _title_author_hash(title: str, authors: "Any") -> str:
172	    """Stable identity key from a normalized title + the first-author surname. Empty when
173	    either component is missing (so the leg never over-blocks on title-only or author-only
174	    input). Surname-based + order-robust so ``'Salari, Amirreza'`` and ``'Amirreza Salari'``
175	    hash identically. Never raises."""
176	    nt = _normalize_title(title)
177	    sn = _first_author_surname(authors)
178	    if not nt or not sn:
179	        return ""
180	    return hashlib.sha1(f"{nt}|{sn}".encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()
181	
182	
183	@dataclass(frozen=True)
184	class SourceIdentity:
185	    """The normalized identity legs of ONE candidate source, extracted once and reused by
186	    the fetch / selection / claim-level seams. Every field is empty when absent (fail-open)."""
187	
188	    doi: str = ""
189	    doaj_id: str = ""
190	    title_author_hash: str = ""
191	    host: str = ""
192	
193	
194	def _candidate_field(candidate: "Any", *names: str) -> "Any":
195	    """First present, non-None value across the given field names (dict OR object)."""
196	    if isinstance(candidate, Mapping):
197	        for n in names:
198	            if n in candidate and candidate.get(n) is not None:
199	                return candidate.get(n)
200	        return None
201	    for n in names:
202	        v = getattr(candidate, n, None)
203	        if v is not None:
204	            return v
205	    return None
206	
207	
208	def extract_source_identity(candidate: "Any") -> SourceIdentity:
209	    """Normalize a candidate source (dict OR object) to its identity legs.
210	
211	    Reuses the module normalizers (``_normalize_doi`` / ``_normalize_url``) plus the DOAJ +
212	    title-author legs. The DOAJ id is recovered from an explicit ``doaj_id`` field, a
213	    doaj.org URL, OR any 32-hex segment of the candidate URL path (so a mirror repository
214	    URL that carries the id is caught). Pure / offline; never raises."""
215	    url = str(_candidate_field(candidate, "url", "source_url") or "")
216	    doi_raw = str(_candidate_field(candidate, "doi") or "")
217	    title = str(_candidate_field(candidate, "title") or "")
218	    authors = _candidate_field(candidate, "authors", "author")
219	    doaj_field = str(_candidate_field(candidate, "doaj_id") or "")
220	
221	    doaj_id = _normalize_doaj_id(doaj_field)
222	    if not doaj_id:
223	        _cands = _extract_doaj_id_candidates(url)
224	        doaj_id = next(iter(sorted(_cands)), "") if _cands else ""
225	    return SourceIdentity(
226	        doi=_normalize_doi(doi_raw) or next(iter(sorted(_extract_dois(url))), ""),
227	        doaj_id=doaj_id,
228	        title_author_hash=_title_author_hash(title, authors),
229	        host=_host_of(url),
230	    )
231	
232	
233	def _host_of(url: str) -> str:
234	    """Lowercased host of ``url`` (empty on blank/garbage). Local, no external dep."""
235	    if not url:
236	        return ""
237	    try:
238	        from urllib.parse import urlsplit  # noqa: PLC0415
239	
240	        netloc = urlsplit(str(url).strip()).netloc.lower()
241	        return netloc.rsplit("@", 1)[-1].split(":", 1)[0]
242	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 - never raise on a garbage url
243	        return ""
244	
245	
246	def is_blocked_source(candidate: "Any", registry: "BlockedRegistry | None") -> tuple[bool, str]:
247	    """Whole-candidate blocked check: extract every identity leg (url / doi / pii / doaj /
248	    title+author) from ``candidate`` (dict OR object) and match it against ``registry``.
249	
250	    ``(False, "")`` on an empty/None registry (byte-identical no-op). Pure; never raises —
251	    a bad candidate is treated as not-blocked (fail-open) so it can never abort a run."""
252	    if registry is None or registry.is_empty:
253	        return (False, "")
254	    try:
255	        url = str(_candidate_field(candidate, "url", "source_url") or "")
256	        doi = str(_candidate_field(candidate, "doi") or "")
257	        title = str(_candidate_field(candidate, "title") or "")
258	        authors = _candidate_field(candidate, "authors", "author")
259	        doaj_id = str(_candidate_field(candidate, "doaj_id") or "")
260	        return registry.is_blocked(
261	            url=url, doi=doi, title=title, doaj_id=doaj_id, authors=authors
262	        )
263	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 - fail-open: a bad candidate is never blocked-by-error
264	        return (False, "")
265	
266	_SCHEME_RE = re.compile(r"^[a-z][a-z0-9+.\-]*://", re.IGNORECASE)
267	_WWW_RE = re.compile(r"^www\.", re.IGNORECASE)
268	_DOI_PREFIX_RE = re.compile(r"^(?:dx\.)?doi\.org/", re.IGNORECASE)
269	_DOI_SCHEME_LABEL_RE = re.compile(r"^doi:\s*", re.IGNORECASE)
270	_TITLE_NONALNUM_RE = re.compile(r"[^a-z0-9]+")
271	_TITLE_WS_RE = re.compile(r"\s+")
272	
273	
274	def denylist_enabled() -> bool:
275	    """Kill-switch. DEFAULT ON. ``PG_BLOCKED_REFERENCE_DENYLIST=0`` => empty registry
276	    everywhere => every wired seam byte-identical to pre-B2 behaviour."""
277	    return os.getenv(_DENYLIST_FLAG, "1").strip().lower() not in _OFF_TOKENS
278	
279	
280	def _normalize_url(url: str) -> str:
281	    """Mirror-robust canonical URL key: w3lib-canonicalize (percent / dot-segment / port /
282	    fragment), then strip scheme + leading ``www.`` + query + trailing slash and lowercase.
283	
284	    The query is dropped on purpose (as the benchmark's own blocked-source matcher does)
285	    so ``?via=ihub``-style mirror variants of the SAME path still match; the path PII / DOI
286	    legs distinguish genuinely different articles. Never raises ('' on bad input)."""
287	    if not url:
288	        return ""
289	    raw = url.strip()
290	    if not raw:
291	        return ""
292	    canon = raw
293	    if _canonicalize_url is not None:
294	        try:
295	            canon = _canonicalize_url(raw)
296	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 - fall back to the raw string, never raise
297	            canon = raw
298	    canon = _SCHEME_RE.sub("", canon)
299	    canon = _WWW_RE.sub("", canon)
300	    canon = canon.split("?", 1)[0].split("#", 1)[0]
301	    return canon.rstrip("/").lower()
302	
303	
304	def _normalize_doi(doi: str) -> str:
305	    """Crossref-canonical DOI key: lowercase, strip ``https?://`` / ``dx.``|``doi.org/`` /
306	    ``doi:`` prefixes, drop any trailing query/fragment + slash. DOI suffixes are
307	    case-insensitive per the Crossref rule. Never raises ('' on bad input)."""
308	    if not doi:
309	        return ""
310	    d = doi.strip().lower()
311	    if not d:
312	        return ""
313	    d = _SCHEME_RE.sub("", d)
314	    d = _DOI_PREFIX_RE.sub("", d)
315	    d = _DOI_SCHEME_LABEL_RE.sub("", d)
316	    d = d.split("?", 1)[0].split("#", 1)[0]
317	    return d.rstrip("/.")
318	
319	
320	def _extract_dois(text: str) -> set[str]:
321	    """Every normalized DOI appearing in ``text`` (handles bare DOIs AND DOIs embedded in
322	    ``doi.org`` URLs)."""
323	    out: set[str] = set()
324	    for raw in _DOI_RE.findall(text or ""):
325	        nd = _normalize_doi(raw)
326	        if nd:
327	            out.add(nd)
328	    return out
329	
330	
331	def _extract_piis(text: str) -> set[str]:
332	    """Every publisher PII / arXiv id in ``text`` (incl. PIIs embedded in sciencedirect /
333	    linkinghub URLs). Stored uppercased / namespaced so the match is case-insensitive."""
334	    out: set[str] = set()
335	    for raw in _ELSEVIER_PII_RE.findall(text or ""):
336	        out.add(raw.upper())
337	    for raw in _ARXIV_RE.findall(text or ""):
338	        out.add("ARXIV:" + raw.lower())
339	    return out
340	
341	
342	def _parse_appendix_authors(appendix: str) -> list[str]:
343	    """Author strings from the appendix ``'authors': [...]`` / ``'author': '...'`` field
344	    (build-side title+author leg). Empty when no author field is present. Never raises."""
345	    m = _AUTHORS_FIELD_RE.search(appendix or "")
346	    if not m:
347	        return []
348	    return _QUOTED_STR_RE.findall(m.group(1))
349	
350	
351	def _normalize_title(title: str) -> str:
352	    """Lowercase, strip punctuation/whitespace to a single space-joined token string."""
353	    if not title:
354	        return ""
355	    low = title.lower()
356	    low = _TITLE_NONALNUM_RE.sub(" ", low)
357	    return _TITLE_WS_RE.sub(" ", low).strip()
358	
359	
360	def _title_ratio(a: str, b: str) -> float:
361	    """Similarity in [0, 1] between two normalized titles (rapidfuzz if available, else
362	    stdlib difflib). Both inputs are already ``_normalize_title``-d."""
363	    if not a or not b:
364	        return 0.0
365	    if _rapidfuzz_fuzz is not None:
366	        return float(_rapidfuzz_fuzz.ratio(a, b)) / 100.0
367	    return difflib.SequenceMatcher(None, a, b).ratio()
368	
369	
370	@dataclass(frozen=True)
371	class BlockedRegistry:
372	    """Six OR'd normalized key-sets of operator-prohibited references for ONE work.
373	
374	    The original FOUR legs (canonical url / DOI / publisher PII / fuzzy title) are joined by
375	    the I-deepfix-001 P0-1 identity legs — DOAJ article id + title+first-author hash — so a
376	    DOAJ MIRROR that carries the same article id (in its URL path or metadata) but NOT the
377	    listed URL / DOI / PII / a matchable title is still caught."""
378	
379	    canonical_urls: frozenset[str] = frozenset()
380	    dois: frozenset[str] = frozenset()
381	    publisher_piis: frozenset[str] = frozenset()
382	    title_keys: tuple[str, ...] = ()
383	    doaj_ids: frozenset[str] = frozenset()
384	    title_author_hashes: frozenset[str] = frozenset()
385	
386	    @classmethod
387	    def empty(cls) -> "BlockedRegistry":
388	        """An empty registry — ``is_blocked`` is always ``(False, "")``."""
389	        return cls()
390	
391	    @property
392	    def is_empty(self) -> bool:
393	        return not (
394	            self.canonical_urls
395	            or self.dois
396	            or self.publisher_piis
397	            or self.title_keys
398	            or self.doaj_ids
399	            or self.title_author_hashes
400	        )
401	
402	    def is_blocked(
403	        self,
404	        url: str = "",
405	        doi: str = "",
406	        title: str = "",
407	        doaj_id: str = "",
408	        authors: "Any" = None,
409	    ) -> tuple[bool, str]:
410	        """True + the matched key/reason iff ANY leg matches the blocked work; else
411	        ``(False, "")``. The DOI + PII + DOAJ legs ALSO inspect identifiers embedded in
412	        ``url`` so a mirror that is not literally listed still matches. Pure; never raises."""
413	        if self.is_empty:
414	            return (False, "")
415	        # 1) canonical-URL leg
416	        norm_url = _normalize_url(url)
417	        if norm_url and norm_url in self.canonical_urls:
418	            return (True, f"url:{norm_url}")
419	        # 2) DOI leg — explicit doi arg OR a DOI embedded in the url
420	        candidate_dois = _extract_dois(url)
421	        explicit = _normalize_doi(doi)
422	        if explicit:
423	            candidate_dois.add(explicit)
424	        candidate_dois |= _extract_dois(doi)
425	        for cd in candidate_dois:
426	            if cd in self.dois:
427	                return (True, f"doi:{cd}")
428	        # 3) PII leg — identifiers embedded in the url / doi text
429	        for pii in _extract_piis(url) | _extract_piis(doi):
430	            if pii in self.publisher_piis:
431	                return (True, f"pii:{pii}")
432	        # 4) DOAJ-id leg (I-deepfix-001 P0-1) — explicit doaj_id arg OR an id embedded in
433	        # the candidate URL path (the mirror-repository case). Conservatively-built set, so
434	        # a permissive candidate scan never over-blocks.
435	        if self.doaj_ids:
436	            _cand_doaj = _extract_doaj_id_candidates(url)
437	            _ex_doaj = _normalize_doaj_id(doaj_id)
438	            if _ex_doaj:
439	                _cand_doaj.add(_ex_doaj)
440	            for cdj in _cand_doaj:
441	                if cdj in self.doaj_ids:
442	                    return (True, f"doaj:{cdj}")
443	        # 5) title+first-author hash leg — exact-match fallback when no id is available
444	        if self.title_author_hashes:
445	            tah = _title_author_hash(title, authors)
446	            if tah and tah in self.title_author_hashes:
447	                return (True, f"title_author:{tah[:16]}")
448	        # 6) title fuzzy leg
449	        norm_title = _normalize_title(title)
450	        if norm_title and self.title_keys:
451	            for bt in self.title_keys:
452	                if _title_ratio(norm_title, bt) >= TITLE_FUZZY_THRESHOLD:
453	                    return (True, f"title:{bt[:80]}")
454	        return (False, "")
455	
456	
457	def build_blocked_registry(question_text: str) -> BlockedRegistry:
458	    """Parse the operator do-not-view / blocked-references appendix out of
459	    ``question_text`` and return the deny-list. Empty registry when the kill-switch is
460	    OFF, when there is no appendix, or on ANY parse error (FAIL-OPEN — never crash a run).
461	
462	    The appendix is located with the SAME regexes the report-echo strip uses
463	    (``injection_appendix.locate_injected_appendix``) so the two legs can never drift.
464	    """
465	    if not denylist_enabled():
466	        return BlockedRegistry.empty()
467	    try:
468	        appendix = locate_injected_appendix(question_text or "")
469	        if not appendix:
470	            return BlockedRegistry.empty()
471	        urls: set[str] = set()
472	        for raw in _URL_RE.findall(appendix):
473	            nu = _normalize_url(raw)
474	            if nu:
475	                urls.add(nu)
476	        dois = _extract_dois(appendix)
477	        piis = _extract_piis(appendix)
478	        titles: list[str] = []
479	        raw_titles = _TITLE_FIELD_RE.findall(appendix)
480	        for raw_title in raw_titles:
481	            nt = _normalize_title(raw_title)
482	            if nt and nt not in titles:
483	                titles.append(nt)
484	        # I-deepfix-001 P0-1: DOAJ article ids — CONSERVATIVE build (explicit doaj.org/article
485	        # URL or a labelled doaj_id field only, never a bare hex token) so a stray 32-hex in the
486	        # appendix is not registered.
487	        doaj_ids: set[str] = set()
488	        for _m in _DOAJ_ARTICLE_RE.findall(appendix):
489	            doaj_ids.add(_m.lower())
490	        for _m in _DOAJ_ID_FIELD_RE.findall(appendix):
491	            doaj_ids.add(_m.lower())
492	        # I-deepfix-001 P0-1: title+first-author identity hash — pair the appendix title(s)
493	        # with the appendix authors field (order-robust, surname-based).
494	        title_author_hashes: set[str] = set()
495	        appendix_authors = _parse_appendix_authors(appendix)
496	        if appendix_authors and raw_titles:
497	            for raw_title in raw_titles:
498	                tah = _title_author_hash(raw_title, appendix_authors)
499	                if tah:
500	                    title_author_hashes.add(tah)
501	        registry = BlockedRegistry(
502	            canonical_urls=frozenset(urls),
503	            dois=frozenset(dois),
504	            publisher_piis=frozenset(piis),
505	            title_keys=tuple(titles),
506	            doaj_ids=frozenset(doaj_ids),
507	            title_author_hashes=frozenset(title_author_hashes),
508	        )
509	        logger.info(
510	            "[blocked_registry] built per-work deny-list: urls=%d dois=%d piis=%d "
511	            "titles=%d doaj_ids=%d title_author=%d",
512	            len(registry.canonical_urls),
513	            len(registry.dois),
514	            len(registry.publisher_piis),
515	            len(registry.title_keys),
516	            len(registry.doaj_ids),
517	            len(registry.title_author_hashes),
518	        )
519	        return registry
520	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 - FAIL-OPEN: a bad question never aborts a run
521	        logger.warning(
522	            "[blocked_registry] build failed (FAIL-OPEN, empty registry): %s", exc
523	        )
524	        return BlockedRegistry.empty()
525

1	"""Citation Normalizer (v2 Layer 3).
2	
3	Handles the compound citation rebellion: LLMs inevitably write
4	"[SRC-001, SRC-002]" instead of "[SRC-001][SRC-002]", or even
5	"[SRC-001, 002]". This module:
6	
7	1. Provides the CITATION_RULES prompt block for the section writer system prompt
8	2. Provides normalize_citations() to split/fix compound citations post-generation
9	3. Provides resolve_to_numbers() to remap [SRC-NNN] -> [1], [2], [3] in assembly
10	
11	Fix R3-#3: Without this, compound brackets like [SRC-001, SRC-002] are invisible
12	to a simple regex like \\[SRC-\\d+\\], and those sources become permanently
13	orphaned from the bibliography.
14	"""
15	
16	from __future__ import annotations
17	
18	import logging
19	import re
20	from typing import Optional
21	
22	logger = logging.getLogger("polaris_graph")
23	
24	
25	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
26	# Prompt injection block (for section writer system prompt)
27	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
28	
29	CITATION_RULES = """
30	CITATION FORMAT (MANDATORY — violations will fail quality gate):
31	- Cite sources inline as [SRC-001], [SRC-002], etc.
32	- For multiple sources: [SRC-001][SRC-002][SRC-003] (separate brackets).
33	- NEVER combine citations: [SRC-001, SRC-002] is FORBIDDEN.
34	- NEVER abbreviate: [SRC-001, 002] is FORBIDDEN.
35	- NEVER use parentheses: (SRC-001) is FORBIDDEN.
36	- Every factual claim MUST have at least one [SRC-NNN] citation.
37	""".strip()
38	
39	
40	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
41	# Post-generation normalization
42	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
43	
44	# Pattern: [SRC-001, SRC-002, ...] or [SRC-001, 002, ...]
45	_COMPOUND_RE = re.compile(
46	    r"\["                          # opening bracket
47	    r"(SRC-\d{3}"                  # first full SRC-NNN
48	    r"(?:\s*[,;]\s*"               # comma or semicolon separator
49	    r"(?:SRC-)?\d{3})+)"           # subsequent SRC-NNN or bare NNN
50	    r"\]",                         # closing bracket
51	)
52	
53	# Pattern: (SRC-001) with parentheses instead of brackets
54	_PAREN_RE = re.compile(r"\((SRC-\d{3})\)")
55	
56	# Pattern: bare SRC-NNN without brackets
57	_BARE_RE = re.compile(r"(?<!\[)(SRC-\d{3})(?!\])")
58	
59	
60	def normalize_citations(text: str) -> str:
61	    """Split compound citations and fix formatting.
62	
63	    Transforms:
64	        [SRC-001, SRC-002]      -> [SRC-001][SRC-002]
65	        [SRC-001, 002, 003]     -> [SRC-001][SRC-002][SRC-003]
66	        [SRC-001; SRC-002]      -> [SRC-001][SRC-002]
67	        (SRC-001)               -> [SRC-001]
68	        bare SRC-001 in text    -> [SRC-001]
69	        [SRC-001][SRC-001]      -> [SRC-001]  (adjacent dedup)
70	    """
71	    # Step 1: Split compound brackets
72	    def _split_compound(match: re.Match) -> str:
73	        inner = match.group(1)
74	        # Extract all NNN values
75	        parts = re.split(r"\s*[,;]\s*", inner)
76	        ids: list[str] = []
77	        last_prefix = ""
78	        for part in parts:
79	            part = part.strip()
80	            if part.startswith("SRC-"):
81	                ids.append(part)
82	                last_prefix = part[:4]  # "SRC-"
83	            elif re.match(r"^\d{3}$", part) and last_prefix:
84	                ids.append(f"SRC-{part}")
85	        return "".join(f"[{sid}]" for sid in ids)
86	
87	    text = _COMPOUND_RE.sub(_split_compound, text)
88	
89	    # Step 2: Fix parenthesized citations
90	    text = _PAREN_RE.sub(r"[\1]", text)
91	
92	    # Step 3: Fix bare SRC-NNN (no brackets)
93	    text = _BARE_RE.sub(r"[\1]", text)
94	
95	    # Step 4: Deduplicate adjacent identical citations [SRC-001][SRC-001] -> [SRC-001]
96	    text = re.sub(r"(\[SRC-\d{3}\])(?:\1)+", r"\1", text)
97	
98	    return text
99	
100	
101	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
102	# Assembly: SRC-NNN -> [1], [2], [3]
103	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
104	
105	def resolve_to_numbers(
106	    text: str,
107	    citation_map: dict[str, int],
108	) -> str:
109	    """Replace [SRC-NNN] with sequential citation numbers [1], [2], etc.
110	
111	    Args:
112	        text: Report text with [SRC-NNN] markers.
113	        citation_map: Mapping from SRC-NNN -> citation number.
114	                      Obtain from SourceRegistry.to_citation_map().
115	
116	    Returns:
117	        Text with [SRC-NNN] replaced by [N].
118	    """
119	    # First normalize any compound citations that slipped through
120	    text = normalize_citations(text)
121	
122	    def _replace(match: re.Match) -> str:
123	        src_id = match.group(1)
124	        num = citation_map.get(src_id)
125	        if num is not None:
126	            return f"[{num}]"
127	        logger.warning("Orphaned citation: [%s] not in registry", src_id)
128	        return match.group(0)  # leave as-is for debugging
129	
130	    return re.sub(r"\[(SRC-\d{3})\]", _replace, text)
131	
132	
133	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
134	# Validation: count citation coverage
135	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
136	
137	def citation_stats(text: str) -> dict[str, int]:
138	    """Count citation markers in text for quality gate.
139	
140	    Returns dict with:
141	        total_citations: number of [SRC-NNN] markers
142	        unique_sources: number of distinct SRC-NNN IDs
143	        uncited_paragraphs: paragraphs with 0 citations
144	        compound_violations: remaining compound brackets (should be 0)
145	    """
146	    all_cites = re.findall(r"\[SRC-(\d{3})\]", text)
147	    compound = len(_COMPOUND_RE.findall(text))
148	
149	    paragraphs = [p.strip() for p in text.split("\n\n") if len(p.strip()) > 50]
150	    uncited = sum(1 for p in paragraphs if "[SRC-" not in p)
151	
152	    return {
153	        "total_citations": len(all_cites),
154	        "unique_sources": len(set(all_cites)),
155	        "uncited_paragraphs": uncited,
156	        "total_paragraphs": len(paragraphs),
157	        "compound_violations": compound,
158	    }
159
