1	"""
2	Contradiction detector — HONEST-REBUILD Phase 3.
3	
4	Finds pairs of evidence statements that directly contradict each other
5	on the same (subject, predicate, numeric value) tuple. Surfaces these
6	to the user before synthesis so the generator cannot silently pick one
7	side.
8	
9	ADDRESSES PG_LB_SA_02_CONTENT_AUDIT Section E-06: the pre-rebuild
10	pipeline found two conflicting efficacy numbers for semaglutide weight
11	loss (STEP 1: 14.9% vs STEP 5: 17.4%), and the final report cited only
12	one without disclosing the range. A reader could not tell whether the
13	disagreement was stratification, dosing, duration, or cherry-picking.
14	
15	DESIGN:
16	This detector is deterministic and rule-based. It does NOT use an LLM
17	to decide whether two claims contradict because LLM-based contradiction
18	detection has known failure modes on numerical values (confuses 14 and
19	17 as "roughly similar" even when it's a 17% relative difference).
20	
21	Approach:
22	  1. Extract structured claims: (subject, predicate, numeric_value, unit)
23	     from each evidence row using regex on the direct_quote.
24	  2. Group claims by normalized (subject, predicate) pair.
25	  3. Within each group, flag numeric discrepancies > 10% relative
26	     difference OR > 2.0 absolute unit difference (configurable).
27	  4. Return a list of ContradictionRecord objects.
28	
29	The caller (Phase 4 synthesis) inserts a disclosure line for each
30	contradicted claim: "Sources report X (ref A) and Y (ref B). The
31	reasons for the difference are [analyst note]."
32	"""
33	
34	from __future__ import annotations
35	
36	import logging
37	import os
38	import re
39	from dataclasses import asdict, dataclass, field
40	from typing import Any, Optional
41	
42	logger = logging.getLogger("polaris_graph.contradiction_detector")
43	
44	
45	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
46	# Numeric + unit extraction
47	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
48	
49	# Percentage values. Captures the number + "%" or " percent".
50	# Note: \b after "%" fails because % and space are both non-word chars —
51	# use a non-capturing boundary or a literal end-of-token check instead.
52	_PCT_RE = re.compile(
53	    r"(?P<value>-?\d+(?:\.\d+)?)\s*(?P<unit>%|percent|pct)(?=\s|[,.;:)\]!?]|$)",
54	    re.IGNORECASE,
55	)
56	
57	# Mass / dose values with unit.
58	_DOSE_RE = re.compile(
59	    r"(?P<value>-?\d+(?:\.\d+)?)\s*(?P<unit>mg|µg|ug|mcg|g|kg)(?=\s|[,.;:)\]!?]|$)",
60	    re.IGNORECASE,
61	)
62	
63	# HbA1c points. "percentage points" uses an explicit trailing word
64	# boundary since "points" ends on a word char.
65	_HBA1C_RE = re.compile(
66	    r"(?P<value>-?\d+(?:\.\d+)?)\s*(?P<unit>%|percentage\s*points?)",
67	    re.IGNORECASE,
68	)
69	
70	# Time durations
71	_DURATION_RE = re.compile(
72	    r"(?P<value>-?\d+(?:\.\d+)?)\s*(?P<unit>weeks?|months?|years?)\b",
73	    re.IGNORECASE,
74	)
75	
76	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
77	# B9 domain-generalization — DOMAIN-AGNOSTIC numeric extraction.
78	#
79	# The clinical extractor (`extract_numeric_claims` clinical path) keys a claim
80	# on a CLINICAL predicate lexicon (`_EFFICACY_PREDICATES` / `_SAFETY_PREDICATES`)
81	# and a CLINICAL subject (`_DRUG_NAME_RE`). For a non-clinical corpus
82	# (economics / AI-labor / policy / science / tech) both return nothing, so
83	# `extract_numeric_claims` emits ZERO claims → every non-clinical numeric row
84	# becomes a SINGLETON in `finding_dedup` / `claim_graph` (the documented
85	# residual that blocks B6/B8 non-clinical baskets). The generic path below
86	# extracts a claim-atom WITHOUT any clinical literal: a generic metric cue (the
87	# SAME field-agnostic cues as `scope_gate.extract_research_frame_heuristic`) is
88	# the predicate, a generic number+unit is the value, and a generic content-word
89	# entity is the subject. This yields a REAL claim-key for non-clinical numerics
90	# (so corroborating sources can consolidate into a basket) WITHOUT loosening the
91	# clinical merge rule — clinical runs are byte-identical (gated on is_clinical).
92	# Faithfulness is unchanged: span-grounding / strict_verify remain the hard gate.
93	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
94	
95	# Any number with an OPTIONAL trailing unit token. Generic — no clinical
96	# vocabulary. Captures percent / currency-magnitude / plain counts so an
97	# economics or labor numeric ("GDP grew 3.2%", "$13 trillion", "75.5 percent")
98	# is extractable. The unit is normalized so two sources stating the same
99	# quantity in the same unit can consolidate.
100	_GENERIC_VALUE_RE = re.compile(
101	    r"(?P<value>-?\d+(?:,\d{3})*(?:\.\d+)?)\s*"
102	    r"(?P<unit>%|percent|percentage\s*points?|pp|bps|basis\s*points?|"
103	    r"trillion|billion|million|thousand|"
104	    # I-deepfix-001 rank-19 (#1344): the UNAMBIGUOUS money-multiplier abbreviations
105	    # ``bn`` (billion) / ``tn`` (trillion) / ``mn`` (million) — a magnitude suffix glued
106	    # onto a currency amount ("$16bn", "£4tn", "€7mn") is a real metric value, so it must
107	    # survive extraction (pre-fix "$16bn" returned None, suppressing a genuine numeric
108	    # disagreement). These three are unambiguous magnitude suffixes and normalize to their
109	    # long form so "$16bn" and "$16 billion" consolidate. BARE ``m``/``k``/``b`` are NOT
110	    # added — they collide with meters / thousands / other tokens ("18m" = 18 metres) and
111	    # would fabricate false magnitude reads.
112	    r"bn|tn|mn|"
113	    r"usd|eur|gbp|cad|jpy|"
114	    r"gw|mw|kw|kwh|mwh|twh|tonnes?|tons?|"
115	    r"jobs?|workers?|people|users?|points?|x|×)?"
116	    r"(?=\s|[,.;:)\]!?]|$)",
117	    re.IGNORECASE,
118	)
119	
120	
121	def _normalize_generic_unit(unit: str) -> str:
122	    """Canonicalize a generic unit token so equivalent spellings consolidate.
123	
124	    '%'/'percent' -> 'percent'; magnitude words and currencies stay as the
125	    lowercased token. Empty/None -> '' (a unit-less count). Pure."""
126	    u = (unit or "").strip().lower()
127	    if not u:
128	        return ""
129	    u = re.sub(r"\s+", " ", u)
130	    if u in ("%", "percent"):
131	        return "percent"
132	    if u in ("pp", "percentage point", "percentage points"):
133	        return "percentage_points"
134	    if u in ("bps", "basis point", "basis points"):
135	        return "basis_points"
136	    # I-deepfix-001 rank-19 (#1344): fold the unambiguous money-multiplier abbreviations
137	    # onto their long form so "$16bn" consolidates with "$16 billion" (same value, same
138	    # canonical unit) rather than splitting into a "bn" vs "billion" pseudo-mismatch.
139	    if u == "bn":
140	        return "billion"
141	    if u == "tn":
142	        return "trillion"
143	    if u == "mn":
144	        return "million"
145	    return u
146	
147	
148	def _normalize_predicate_generic(text: str) -> Optional[str]:
149	    """Return a field-agnostic predicate (a generic metric cue) present in the
150	    text, or None. Reuses `scope_gate.extract_research_frame_heuristic`'s
151	    metric-cue vocabulary (rate/ratio/cost/share/level/score/growth/emissions/
152	    accuracy/...). NO clinical literal. Pure, no LLM."""
153	    from src.polaris_graph.nodes.scope_gate import _FRAME_METRIC_CUES_RE
154	    m = _FRAME_METRIC_CUES_RE.search(text or "")
155	    if m:
156	        return m.group(1).lower()
157	    return None
158	
159	
160	# Generic stopwords for content-word subject extraction (no domain literal).
161	# Includes statistical / temporal / measurement FILLER words that are NOT real
162	# entities — a generic claim whose only nearby content word is filler resolves
163	# to "unknown" (a SAFE SINGLETON, never a false merge — the conservative rule).
164	_GENERIC_SUBJECT_STOPWORDS = frozenset({
165	    "the", "a", "an", "and", "or", "but", "is", "are", "was", "were", "of",
166	    "in", "on", "at", "to", "for", "with", "by", "from", "as", "that", "this",
167	    "these", "those", "it", "its", "be", "been", "has", "have", "had", "will",
168	    "would", "could", "should", "may", "can", "about", "into", "than", "such",
169	    "between", "over", "under", "per", "more", "most", "some", "all", "each",
170	    # Statistical / measurement / temporal filler — not an entity subject.
171	    "mean", "median", "average", "total", "observed", "reported", "estimated",
172	    "measured", "approximately", "roughly", "about", "value", "values",
173	    "rate", "ratio", "level", "amount", "number", "result", "results",
174	    "week", "weeks", "month", "months", "year", "years", "day", "days",
175	    "baseline", "change", "reduction", "increase", "decrease", "growth",
176	    "compared", "versus", "relative", "respectively", "overall", "general",
177	})
178	
179	
180	def _subject_generic(
181	    text: str, anchor_pos: int, predicate: str = "", window: int = 120,
182	) -> str:
183	    """Extract a generic content-word subject for a non-clinical claim — the
184	    noun that names WHAT the metric measures, NOT a drug name and NOT the metric
185	    cue itself.
186	
187	    Strategy (deterministic, no clinical regex):
188	      1. Anchor on the metric-cue (`predicate`) occurrence nearest the numeric
189	         value when available; the subject of "X rate" / "X growth" / "X share"
190	         is the content noun IMMEDIATELY PRECEDING the cue ("unemployment rate"
191	         -> "unemployment"). This makes two sources stating the SAME metric on
192	         the SAME entity agree on the subject (so they can consolidate).
193	      2. Fall back to the nearest non-stopword, non-cue content token to the
194	         numeric anchor.
195	    Returns the lowercased token, or "unknown" when no content word is found
196	    (keeps the claim a SAFE SINGLETON — the conservative default, never a false
197	    merge)."""
198	    if not text:
199	        return "unknown"
200	    pred = (predicate or "").strip().lower()
201	    # 1. Noun immediately preceding the metric cue nearest the value.
202	    if pred:
203	        cue_pos = None
204	        best_cue_dist = 10 ** 9
205	        for m in re.finditer(re.escape(pred), text, flags=re.IGNORECASE):
206	            dist = abs(m.start() - anchor_pos)
207	            if dist < best_cue_dist:
208	                best_cue_dist = dist
209	                cue_pos = m.start()
210	        if cue_pos is not None:
211	            preceding = text[max(0, cue_pos - 60):cue_pos]
212	            toks = re.findall(r"[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9\-]{2,}", preceding)
213	            for tok in reversed(toks):
214	                low = tok.lower()
215	                if low in _GENERIC_SUBJECT_STOPWORDS or low == pred:
216	                    continue
217	                return low
218	    # 2. Nearest content token to the numeric anchor (excluding the cue word).
219	    lo = max(0, anchor_pos - window)
220	    hi = min(len(text), anchor_pos + window)
221	    local = text[lo:hi]
222	    anchor_local = anchor_pos - lo
223	    best_tok: Optional[str] = None
224	    best_dist = 10 ** 9
225	    for m in re.finditer(r"[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9\-]{2,}", local):
226	        tok = m.group(0)
227	        low = tok.lower()
228	        if low in _GENERIC_SUBJECT_STOPWORDS or low == pred:
229	            continue
230	        center = (m.start() + m.end()) // 2
231	        dist = abs(center - anchor_local)
232	        if dist < best_dist or (dist == best_dist and tok[:1].isupper()):
233	            best_dist = dist
234	            best_tok = tok
235	    return best_tok.lower() if best_tok else "unknown"
236	
237	
238	# Generic year / count reject: a bare 4-digit year or a number that is the
239	# obvious sample-size / date is NOT the measured metric value. Used to avoid
240	# keying a generic claim on a date or N when a real metric value is present.
241	_GENERIC_YEAR_RE = re.compile(r"^(19|20)\d{2}$")
242	
243	
244	def _looks_like_year_or_count(value: float, unit: str, window: str, pos: int) -> bool:
245	    """Heuristic: True if this number is most likely a YEAR or a bare sample
246	    count rather than a measured metric value. A unit-bearing number (percent /
247	    currency / magnitude) is always a real value. A bare 4-digit year, or a
248	    number immediately preceded by 'N=' / 'n=' / 'in 20YY' / 'sample of', is
249	    rejected so it cannot become the claim value/key."""
250	    if unit:
251	        return False  # a unit-bearing number is a real metric value
252	    s = str(int(value)) if value == int(value) else str(value)
253	    # I-wire-013 (#1327) iter-3b: match the YEAR pattern on the ABSOLUTE magnitude so a year lifted
254	    # with a leading dash — e.g. "-2010" captured from a "2009-2010" date range or a citation dash —
255	    # is still recognised as a publication/observation YEAR, never a measured metric value. Without
256	    # this, "-2010" (which `^(19|20)\d{2}$` rejects because of the leading "-") leaked as a generic
257	    # value and fabricated the "-2010.0 to ..." possible_metric_mismatch render-noise (DOI-suffix /
258	    # year pair, §-1.1). Only UNIT-LESS numbers reach here, so no real metric is affected.
259	    if _GENERIC_YEAR_RE.match(s.lstrip("-")):
260	        return True
261	    near = window[max(0, pos - 12):pos].lower()
262	    if "n=" in near or "n =" in near or "sample of" in near or "in 20" in near:
263	        return True
264	    return False
265	
266	
267	# I-wire-012 (#1326) — CONTRADICTION: a real contradiction needs a real OPPOSING VALUE, not
268	# year-noise. A ``possible_metric_mismatch`` group whose every disagreeing value is a bare
269	# integer YEAR (in [1900, 2100], NO unit) is two publication/observation YEARS being mis-read as
270	# a metric disagreement (e.g. "2019" vs "2023") — not an opposing metric value. Default-OFF env
271	# flag ``PG_CONTRADICTION_DROP_YEAR_NOISE`` so the OFF path is BYTE-IDENTICAL; when ON, such a
272	# group is relabeled ``not_comparable`` (reason ``year_noise``) and kept OUT of the headline
273	# contradiction count — every claim/source is still DISCLOSED (never dropped — §-1.3). This is a
274	# VALIDITY/WEIGHT check, NOT a faithfulness-gate change.
275	_DROP_YEAR_NOISE_FLAG = "PG_CONTRADICTION_DROP_YEAR_NOISE"
276	_DROP_YEAR_NOISE_OFF_VALUES = frozenset({"", "0", "false", "off", "no"})
277	# Publication/observation-year validity bounds (mirrors the selector's [1900, 2100] convention).
278	_MIN_GENERIC_YEAR = 1900
279	_MAX_GENERIC_YEAR = 2100
280	
281	
282	def _drop_year_noise_enabled() -> bool:
283	    """True when ``PG_CONTRADICTION_DROP_YEAR_NOISE`` is on. OFF (default) => byte-identical."""
284	    return (
285	        os.environ.get(_DROP_YEAR_NOISE_FLAG, "").strip().lower()
286	        not in _DROP_YEAR_NOISE_OFF_VALUES
287	    )
288	
289	
290	def _group_is_year_noise(group: list["ExtractedNumericClaim"]) -> bool:
291	    """True iff EVERY claim in ``group`` has a unit-less value that is a bare integer YEAR
292	    (in [1900, 2100]). Such a 'disagreement' is two YEARS, not an opposing metric value, so it is
293	    not a real contradiction. Conservative: a single unit-bearing or non-year value makes the group
294	    a real numeric disagreement (returns False). Pure, never raises."""
295	    if not group:
296	        return False
297	    for c in group:
298	        if (getattr(c, "unit", "") or "").strip():
299	            return False  # a unit-bearing number is a real metric value, never year-noise
300	        v = getattr(c, "value", None)
301	        try:
302	            fv = float(v)
303	        except (TypeError, ValueError):
304	            return False
305	        if fv != int(fv):
306	            return False  # a non-integer is not a year
307	        iv = int(fv)
308	        if not (_MIN_GENERIC_YEAR <= iv <= _MAX_GENERIC_YEAR):
309	            return False
310	    return True
311	
312	
313	# I-wire-013 (#1327) — CONTRADICTION HARDEN.
314	#
315	# (a) Shared marker for the B9 same-metric-axes downgrade. ``detect_contradictions`` appends this to
316	#     a ContradictionRecord.predicate when it could NOT positively confirm a shared metric. A record
317	#     carrying the marker is NOT a confirmed contradiction, so the render layer
318	#     (``run_honest_sweep_r3._render_contradicts_block``) skips it from the headline
319	#     "Contradictions (both sides)" block — every claim is still disclosed in the contradictions.json
320	#     sidecar (never dropped — §-1.3). A single source-of-truth constant (no magic string).
321	#
322	# (b) Bibliographic-identifier screen. A UNIT-LESS number that sits inside a DOI suffix, an arXiv id,
323	#     an ISSN, or a page range is a CITATION ARTIFACT, never a measured metric value. The generic
324	#     extractor would otherwise lift e.g. the "07123" of a DOI or the "419" of "pp. 412-419" and,
325	#     grouped with an unrelated unit-less number under the same generic metric cue, surface a
326	#     FABRICATED ``possible_metric_mismatch`` (lethal in clinical context, §-1.1). We reject such a
327	#     number BEFORE it becomes a candidate. Only UNIT-LESS numbers are screened — a unit-bearing
328	#     value (47%, $13bn) is always a real metric, never a catalogue id. Faithfulness is unchanged:
329	#     span-grounding / strict_verify remain the hard gate; this only stops inventing a contradiction
330	#     between two catalogue numbers.
331	POSSIBLE_METRIC_MISMATCH_MARKER = "[possible_metric_mismatch]"
332	
333	_BIBLIOGRAPHIC_ID_RE = re.compile(
334	    r"""
335	    \b10\.\d{4,9}/\S+                                  # DOI: 10.1038/s41586-024-07123
336	    | arxiv:\s*\d{4}\.\d{4,5}(?:v\d+)?                 # arXiv id: arXiv:2401.12345v2
337	    # I-deepfix-001 FIX-#4 (drb_72): a BARE arXiv id with no "arXiv:" prefix
338	    # (e.g. an inline "2507.07935") is still a citation artifact, never a metric
339	    # value. YYMM.NNNNN shape, MONTH-VALIDATED (Codex preflight P2): 2-digit year +
340	    # 2-digit month 01-12 + dot + 4-5 fraction digits (+ optional vN). The month gate
341	    # rejects a real NNNN.NNNN(N) metric whose 3rd-4th digits are not a valid month
342	    # (e.g. 1234.5678 -> "34" is not 01-12), so this screens arXiv ids only.
343	    | \b\d{2}(?:0[1-9]|1[0-2])\.\d{4,5}(?:v\d+)?\b     # bare arXiv id: 2507.07935 (YYMM)
344	    | \bissn:?\s*\d{4}-\d{3}[\dxX]\b                   # ISSN: ISSN 0028-0836
345	    # I-wire-013 (#1327) iter-2 (Codex P2-2): the page-range dash class is written with EXPLICIT
346	    # codepoint escapes so it can never render ambiguously (e.g. `[\-?--?]`) under a non-UTF-8 read.
347	    # Members: literal hyphen-minus U+002D (\-), figure dash U+2012, en dash U+2013, em dash U+2014,
348	    # horizontal bar U+2015. `re` interprets the \uXXXX escapes inside the raw VERBOSE string; the
349	    # class is byte-for-byte equivalent to the prior literal class (hyphen + U+2012..U+2015).
350	    | \bpp?\.\s*\d+\s*[\-\u2012\u2013\u2014\u2015]\s*\d+   # page range: pp. 412-419 / p. 47-49
351	    | \bpages?\s+\d+\s*[\-\u2012\u2013\u2014\u2015]\s*\d+  # page range: pages 412-419
352	    """,
353	    re.IGNORECASE | re.VERBOSE,
354	)
355	
356	
357	def _bibliographic_id_regions(text: str) -> list[tuple[int, int]]:
358	    """Char spans covering bibliographic identifiers (DOI / arXiv / ISSN / page range).
359	
360	    A unit-less number inside one of these spans is a citation artifact, not a measured metric
361	    value (I-wire-013). Pure, never raises."""
362	    return [(m.start(), m.end()) for m in _BIBLIOGRAPHIC_ID_RE.finditer(text or "")]
363	
364	
365	def _in_bibliographic_region(num_pos: int, regions: list[tuple[int, int]]) -> bool:
366	    """True iff ``num_pos`` falls inside any bibliographic-identifier span. Pure."""
367	    return any(lo <= num_pos < hi for lo, hi in regions)
368	
369	
370	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
371	# I-deepfix-001 rank-19 (#1344) — NON-QUANTITY number screen.
372	#
373	# The generic extractor lifts ANY number from prose, so a UNIT-LESS number that is
374	# actually a NON-QUANTITY token becomes a claim value and then, grouped with an
375	# unrelated unit-less number under the same generic metric cue, fabricates a
376	# possible_metric_mismatch "magnitude disagreement" that the Limitations prose
377	# over-states as "sources disagree on magnitude". The cert artifact surfaced exactly
378	# this (finding #19): subject "aggregate" compared 64.0 (from ``ages 18-64``) vs 1.0
379	# (rel_diff rendered as a 5000% disagreement); subject "firm-" compared 16.0 (the
380	# volume of ``Sustainability 2024, 16, 8881``) vs 2.0 (an author affiliation
381	# superscript) at a 700% "disagreement". NONE of these are measured quantities.
382	#
383	# The classes screened (all NON-QUANTITY):
384	#   * age-range bounds — ``ages 18-64`` / ``aged 18 to 64`` / ``18-64 years``
385	#   * journal volume / issue / article numbers — ``Sustainability 2024, 16, 8881`` /
386	#     ``Vol. 16`` / ``issue 3`` / ``16(3):100``
387	#   * author affiliation superscripts — a digit glued directly onto a preceding
388	#     letter (``Smith2`` / ``Chen1,3`` / ``Lee1-3``), handled by the
389	#     ``_is_superscript_chain_member`` check (rejects EVERY member of a chain, so the
390	#     trailing ``3`` of ``Chen1,3`` is rejected too, not only the letter-glued head)
391	#   * bracketed reference markers — ``[16]`` / ``[2]``
392	#   * single page numbers — ``p. 47`` / ``page 47`` (page RANGES are already handled
393	#     by ``_BIBLIOGRAPHIC_ID_RE``)
394	#
395	# ONLY UNIT-LESS numbers are screened — a unit-bearing value (``16%``, ``$16bn``,
396	# ``47 percent``) is always a real metric, never a catalogue / age / superscript
397	# token, so a genuine numeric disagreement (14% vs 41%) is UNAFFECTED. This is a
398	# VALIDITY screen at the extraction seam, exactly like the sibling bibliographic-id /
399	# leading-dash / year-or-count rejects that surround it; faithfulness is unchanged
400	# (span-grounding / strict_verify remain the hard gate). Deterministic, never raises.
401	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
402	_NON_QUANTITY_RE = re.compile(
403	    r"""
404	    # ── Age ranges ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
405	    # The dash class uses EXPLICIT codepoint escapes (hyphen-minus U+002D, figure
406	    # dash U+2012, en dash U+2013, em dash U+2014, horizontal bar U+2015 — the same
407	    # convention as _BIBLIOGRAPHIC_ID_RE) so a non-ASCII-dashed range ("18–64")
408	    # is screened too — the exact form that slipped past the leading-dash reject
409	    # (which only matches ASCII '-'), and so the class can never render ambiguously
410	    # under a non-UTF-8 read.
411	      \bages?\s+\d{1,3}\s*(?:[\-‒–—―]|to|and|through)\s*\d{1,3}
412	    | \baged\s+\d{1,3}\s*(?:[\-‒–—―]|to|and|through)\s*\d{1,3}
413	    | \bbetween\s+\d{1,3}\s+and\s+\d{1,3}\s+years?\b
414	    | \b\d{1,3}\s*[\-‒–—―]\s*\d{1,3}\s+years?(?:\s+of\s+age|\s+old)?\b
415	    # ── Journal citation volume / issue / article number ─────────────────────
416	    | \b(?:19|20)\d{2}\s*,\s*\d{1,4}\s*,\s*\d{1,6}     # "Journal 2024, 16, 8881"
417	    | \bvol(?:ume)?\.?\s*\d{1,4}                        # Vol. 16 / volume 16
418	    | \b(?:issue|no)\.?\s*\d{1,4}                       # issue 3 / no. 3
419	    | \b\d{1,4}\s*\(\s*\d{1,4}\s*\)\s*:?\s*\d{0,6}      # 16(3):100 volume(issue):page
420	    # ── Bracketed reference markers ──────────────────────────────────────────
421	    | \[\s*\d{1,4}\s*\]                                 # [16] / [2]
422	    # ── Single page numbers (page RANGES: see _BIBLIOGRAPHIC_ID_RE) ──────────
423	    | \bpp?\.\s*\d{1,6}\b                               # p. 47 / pp. 47
424	    | \bpages?\s+\d{1,6}\b                              # page 47
425	    """,
426	    re.IGNORECASE | re.VERBOSE,
427	)
428	
429	
430	def _non_quantity_regions(text: str) -> list[tuple[int, int]]:
431	    """Char spans covering NON-QUANTITY numbers (age range / journal volume-issue /
432	    reference marker / single page number).
433	
434	    A UNIT-LESS number inside one of these spans is not a measured metric value
435	    (I-deepfix-001 rank-19). Author affiliation superscripts are handled separately by
436	    ``_is_superscript_chain_member`` in ``_find_value_generic`` (they have no surrounding
437	    cue token). Pure, never raises."""
438	    return [(m.start(), m.end()) for m in _NON_QUANTITY_RE.finditer(text or "")]
439	
440	
441	def _in_non_quantity_region(num_pos: int, regions: list[tuple[int, int]]) -> bool:
442	    """True iff ``num_pos`` falls inside any non-quantity span. Pure."""
443	    return any(lo <= num_pos < hi for lo, hi in regions)
444	
445	
446	# I-deepfix-001 rank-19 (#1344): author-affiliation / reference SUPERSCRIPT CHAIN separators —
447	# comma plus the dash family (hyphen-minus U+002D, figure dash U+2012, en dash U+2013, em dash
448	# U+2014, horizontal bar U+2015), the same dash convention as ``_NON_QUANTITY_RE``.
449	_SUPERSCRIPT_CHAIN_SEPS = ",-‒–—―"
450	
451	
452	def _is_superscript_chain_member(text: str, num_start: int) -> bool:
453	    """True iff the UNIT-LESS number starting at ``num_start`` is a member of an author
454	    affiliation / reference SUPERSCRIPT CHAIN — a digit glued directly onto a preceding
455	    LETTER, optionally continued as comma / hyphen / dash-separated further digits
456	    (``Smith2`` / ``Chen1,3`` / ``Smith2,4,6`` / ``Lee1-3``).
457	
458	    The pre-fix inline check only rejected the letter-glued HEAD (the ``1`` of ``Chen1,3``),
459	    so a trailing index (the ``3``, whose immediate predecessor is a comma, not a letter)
460	    still leaked as a fabricated claim value. This walks the chain back across any run of
461	    digits and chain separators: if it roots at a letter directly glued to a digit, EVERY
462	    member of the chain is a citation superscript, never a measured quantity, and is rejected.
463	    A number preceded by whitespace or any non-chain char (so NOT letter-rooted) is left
464	    intact — a genuine unit-less metric is never suppressed. Pure, never raises."""
465	    if num_start <= 0:
466	        return False
467	    prev = text[num_start - 1]
468	    # Case 1: the number is glued directly onto a letter ("Smith2").
469	    if prev.isalpha():
470	        return True
471	    # Case 2: it sits after a chain separator — walk back over <sep><digits> links to see
472	    # whether the chain roots at a letter-glued digit ("Chen1,3" -> the trailing "3").
473	    if prev not in _SUPERSCRIPT_CHAIN_SEPS:
474	        return False
475	    i = num_start - 1
476	    while i >= 0:
477	        ch = text[i]
478	        if ch.isdigit() or ch in _SUPERSCRIPT_CHAIN_SEPS:
479	            i -= 1
480	            continue
481	        # First char that is neither a digit nor a separator: a letter roots the chain.
482	        return ch.isalpha()
483	    return False
484	
485	
486	def _find_value_generic(
487	    text: str, cue_pos: Optional[int] = None,
488	) -> Optional[tuple[float, str, str, int]]:
489	    """Find the generic numeric value tied to the metric cue. Returns
490	    (value, normalized_unit, context_window, anchor_position) or None.
491	
492	    B9 (Codex P1.4): pick the number NEAREST the metric cue (``cue_pos``) rather
493	    than the first number in the row, so a leading date / sample-size does not
494	    become the claim value/dedup key. A unit-bearing number is always preferred;
495	    bare years / 'N=' counts are skipped. When ``cue_pos`` is None, fall back to
496	    the first non-year/non-count number. NO clinical reject contexts. Pure."""
497	    if not text:
498	        return None
499	    # B13: the confidence-interval BOUND region(s) ("... CI 2-9", "... CI 5 to 12").
500	    # Numbers inside are interval bounds, not a measured outcome value.
501	    ci_regions = _ci_bound_regions(text)
502	    # I-wire-013 (#1327): bibliographic-identifier spans (DOI / arXiv / ISSN / page range). A
503	    # unit-less number inside one is a citation artifact, not a metric value.
504	    biblio_regions = _bibliographic_id_regions(text)
505	    # I-deepfix-001 rank-19 (#1344): NON-QUANTITY spans (age range / journal volume-issue /
506	    # reference marker / single page number). A unit-less number inside one is not a measured
507	    # metric value — screened out so it can never fabricate a possible_metric_mismatch.
508	    non_quantity_regions = _non_quantity_regions(text)
509	    candidates: list[tuple[float, str, str, int]] = []
510	    for m in _GENERIC_VALUE_RE.finditer(text):
511	        raw = (m.group("value") or "").replace(",", "")
512	        try:
513	            value = float(raw)
514	        except ValueError:
515	            continue
516	        unit = _normalize_generic_unit(m.group("unit") or "")
517	        start_win = max(0, m.start() - 60)
518	        end_win = min(len(text), m.end() + 60)
519	        window = text[start_win:end_win]
520	        if _looks_like_year_or_count(value, unit, window, m.start() - start_win):
521	            continue
522	        # B13: skip a confidence-interval LEVEL ("95% CI" / "95 % confidence
523	        # interval"). It is the statistical confidence level, not a measured
524	        # outcome — anchored on THIS number's position so a real value elsewhere
525	        # in the row (the point estimate) is unaffected.
526	        if _is_confidence_level_at(text, m.start()):
527	            continue
528	        # B13: skip a CI BOUND that sits INSIDE the interval region (so "(95% CI
529	        # 2-9)" emits neither the 95 level nor the 2/-9 bounds). A real point
530	        # estimate outside the parenthetical is unaffected.
531	        if _in_ci_bound_region(m.start(), ci_regions):
532	            continue
533	        # I-wire-013 (#1327): a UNIT-LESS number inside a bibliographic identifier (DOI suffix /
534	        # arXiv id / ISSN / page range) is a citation artifact, not a metric value — reject it so it
535	        # never becomes a fabricated possible_metric_mismatch. Unit-bearing values are unaffected.
536	        if not unit and _in_bibliographic_region(m.start(), biblio_regions):
537	            continue
538	        # I-deepfix-001 rank-19 (#1344): a UNIT-LESS number that is a NON-QUANTITY token — an
539	        # age-range bound ("ages 18-64" -> 64), a journal volume / issue / article number
540	        # ("Sustainability 2024, 16, 8881" -> 16 / 8881), a bracketed reference marker
541	        # ("[16]" -> 16), or a single page number ("p. 47" -> 47) — is not a measured metric
542	        # value. Reject it so it never becomes a fabricated possible_metric_mismatch that the
543	        # Limitations prose over-states as "sources disagree on magnitude". Unit-bearing values
544	        # (16%, $16bn, 47 percent) are unaffected, so a genuine numeric disagreement is intact.
545	        if not unit and _in_non_quantity_region(m.start(), non_quantity_regions):
546	            continue
547	        # I-deepfix-001 rank-19 (#1344): a UNIT-LESS number that is a member of an author
548	        # affiliation / reference SUPERSCRIPT CHAIN — a digit glued directly onto a preceding
549	        # LETTER ("Smith2"), optionally continued as comma / hyphen / dash-separated further
550	        # digits ("Chen1,3" / "Smith2,4,6" / "Lee1-3") — is a citation superscript or a
551	        # model/identifier index, never a measured quantity. EVERY member of the chain is
552	        # rejected (not just the letter-glued head), so a trailing index like the "3" in
553	        # "Chen1,3" — whose immediate predecessor is a comma, not a letter — can no longer leak
554	        # as a claim value. A real metric always has a space or a non-letter (currency symbol,
555	        # digit, '<') before its digits. Unit-bearing values are unaffected (only unit-less
556	        # numbers are screened), so a genuine numeric disagreement is never suppressed.
557	        if not unit and _is_superscript_chain_member(text, m.start()):
558	            continue
559	        # I-deepfix-001 FIX-#4 (drb_72): a UNIT-LESS value whose leading '-' is the
560	        # hyphen of a "N-N" token — a law/statute number ("P.L. 87-415" -> "-415"),
561	        # a page/volume range ("412-419" -> "-419"), a dash-joined index/citation
562	        # ("GEZANI-5" -> "-5") — is an IDENTIFIER / RANGE fragment, never a measured
563	        # metric value. The generic '-?\d+' rule otherwise lifts the tail and
564	        # fabricates a contradiction against a real number. Reject it so it never
565	        # becomes a claim. Unit-bearing numbers (a real "-14.9%") are unaffected, and
566	        # a standalone negative (the '-' not glued to a preceding digit) is still
567	        # extracted.
568	        if (
569	            not unit
570	            and raw.startswith("-")
571	            and m.start() > 0
572	            and text[m.start() - 1].isdigit()
573	        ):
574	            continue
575	        candidates.append((value, unit, window, m.start()))
576	    if not candidates:
577	        return None
578	    if cue_pos is None:
579	        return candidates[0]
580	    # Nearest candidate to the metric cue; a unit-bearing number wins a near tie.
581	    def _key(c: tuple[float, str, str, int]) -> tuple[int, int]:
582	        dist = abs(c[3] - cue_pos)
583	        return (dist, 0 if c[1] else 1)
584	    return min(candidates, key=_key)
585	
586	
587	def _extract_numeric_claims_generic(
588	    evidence: list[dict[str, Any]],
589	) -> list["ExtractedNumericClaim"]:
590	    """DOMAIN-AGNOSTIC numeric claim extraction for NON-clinical corpora.
591	
592	    One claim per row at most (the first generic metric value), keyed on a
593	    generic predicate + generic subject + value + unit. A row with no generic
594	    metric cue OR no extractable number yields NO claim (kept as a safe
595	    singleton upstream). The clinical discriminator fields (dose / arm / Wave-3)
596	    stay at their UNKNOWN defaults so they never anchor a non-clinical merge —
597	    a non-clinical merge is anchored ONLY by a confidently-extracted
598	    subject+predicate+value+unit (the conservative rule). NO clinical literal.
599	    """
600	    claims: list[ExtractedNumericClaim] = []
601	    for ev in evidence:
602	        quote = ev.get("direct_quote") or ev.get("statement") or ""
603	        if not quote:
604	            continue
605	        predicate = _normalize_predicate_generic(quote)
606	        if not predicate:
607	            continue
608	        # Anchor the value on the number nearest the metric cue (Codex P1.4) so
609	        # a leading date / sample-size cannot become the claim value/key.
610	        _cue_m = re.search(re.escape(predicate), quote, flags=re.IGNORECASE)
611	        _cue_pos = _cue_m.start() if _cue_m else None
612	        found = _find_value_generic(quote, cue_pos=_cue_pos)
613	        if found is None:
614	            continue
615	        value, unit, ctx_window, anchor_pos = found
616	        subject = _subject_generic(quote, anchor_pos, predicate=predicate)
617	        # Codex iter-3 P1: do NOT set endpoint_phrase=predicate. The metric cue
618	        # IS the predicate, not a time-window/endpoint. Stamping it as
619	        # endpoint_phrase would make _shared_metric_axes read two same-predicate
620	        # generic claims as POSITIVELY-confirmed shared scope -> a hard
621	        # contradiction, when in fact their comparator/population/time-window are
622	        # UNCONFIRMED. Leaving the discriminator axes empty means an unconfirmed
623	        # generic numeric gap is correctly labeled possible_metric_mismatch
624	        # (conservative non-clinical default); a true contradiction needs a
625	        # positively-extracted shared scope axis. The predicate itself still
626	        # groups the claims (grouping key includes predicate).
627	        claims.append(ExtractedNumericClaim(
628	            evidence_id=str(ev.get("evidence_id", "")),
629	            subject=subject,
630	            predicate=predicate,
631	            value=value,
632	            unit=unit,
633	            context_snippet=ctx_window[:200],
634	            source_url=ev.get("source_url", ""),
635	            source_tier=ev.get("tier", ""),
636	        ))
637	    return claims
638	
639	
640	# Predicate keywords — the ones we most want to catch contradictions on.
641	# BUG-M-202 fix (deep-dive R7): expanded coverage beyond the original
642	# obesity/cardiometabolic-only set. A proper domain-YAML-driven
643	# profile loader is tracked as a follow-up (see docs/todo_list.md);
644	# this expansion is the minimum-viable fix to close the AF
645	# anticoagulation silent-failure reproducer.
646	_EFFICACY_PREDICATES_METABOLIC = (
647	    # Original obesity / GLP-1 / lipid
648	    "weight loss", "body weight", "weight reduction",
649	    "hba1c reduction", "a1c reduction",
650	    "systolic blood pressure reduction", "diastolic blood pressure reduction",
651	    "ldl reduction", "cholesterol reduction",
652	    "cardiovascular risk reduction", "mace reduction",
653	    "mortality reduction",
654	)
655	
656	_EFFICACY_PREDICATES_ANTICOAGULATION = (
657	    # AF anticoagulation + guideline endpoints (BUG-M-202 R7)
658	    "stroke rate", "stroke risk", "ischemic stroke",
659	    "systemic embolism", "stroke or systemic embolism",
660	    "major bleeding", "clinically relevant non-major bleeding",
661	    "intracranial hemorrhage", "gastrointestinal bleeding",
662	    "time in therapeutic range", "ttr", "inr",
663	    "cha2ds2-vasc", "has-bled",
664	    "hazard ratio", "relative risk", "odds ratio",
665	)
666	
667	_EFFICACY_PREDICATES_TECH = (
668	    # Tech / ML benchmark endpoints
669	    "accuracy", "f1 score", "f1-score",
670	    "precision", "recall", "auc", "roc auc",

732	@dataclass
733	class ExtractedNumericClaim:
734	    """A claim like 'X causes Y-value-unit change in Z' extracted from evidence."""
735	
736	    evidence_id: str
737	    subject: str      # e.g., "semaglutide"
738	    predicate: str    # e.g., "weight loss"
739	    value: float
740	    unit: str         # "%", "mg", "weeks", etc.
741	    context_snippet: str  # original text for display
742	    source_url: str = ""
743	    source_tier: str = ""
744	    dose: str = ""             # "2.4 mg", "7.2 mg", or "" if not present
745	    # arm: legacy default "treatment"; "comparator_adjacent" only when a
746	    # placebo/comparator cue fired. Kept at the LEGACY default (NOT None) for
747	    # OFF byte-identity: the OFF-path legacy key _normalized_key_numeric reads
748	    # ``arm`` into position 7 of the SHA-1-hashed cluster key
749	    # (claim_graph.py:241) and honest_pipeline/run_honest_sweep_r3 asdict it
750	    # into contradictions.json — so changing this default to None drifts the OFF
751	    # cluster ids + serialized bytes (Codex Slice-B P1). Flag-ON consolidation
752	    # is unaffected: claim_graph._unknown_arm treats "treatment" (the default,
753	    # never-cued) AS UNKNOWN, so a defaulted arm still forces a singleton and
754	    # only a positively-extracted "comparator_adjacent" anchors a merge
755	    # (design §4.3, the arm lesson — fail-closed without the None change).
756	    arm: str = "treatment"     # "treatment" (default/no-cue), "comparator_adjacent" (cue)
757	    endpoint_phrase: str = ""  # e.g., "at week 68", "from baseline", "mean change"
758	    # ── Wave-3 positive-known discriminator fields (I-arch-002 [1]) ──────────
759	    # All default '' = UNKNOWN sentinel. Dormant carriers: no consumer reads
760	    # them until claim_graph.build_merge_key reads them under
761	    # PG_SWEEP_CREDIBILITY_REDESIGN. A value is set ONLY when a positive token
762	    # was extracted (never derived/defaulted) per design §4.3.
763	    dose_frequency: str = ""    # "qd"/"bid"/"weekly"/... cadence token; '' = UNKNOWN
764	    comparator: str = ""        # the "vs X" comparator phrase; '' = UNKNOWN
765	    route_formulation: str = "" # "iv"/"po"/"sc"/"er"/...; '' = UNKNOWN
766	    effect_measure: str = ""    # "relative"/"absolute"/"hr"/"or"/"raw"; '' = UNKNOWN
767	    direction: str = ""         # "increase"/"decrease" TOKEN-only; '' = UNKNOWN
768	    population: str = ""         # "patients with X" cohort phrase; '' = UNKNOWN

771	@dataclass
772	class ContradictionRecord:
773	    """Two claims that contradict on the same (subject, predicate)."""
774	
775	    subject: str
776	    predicate: str
777	    claims: list[ExtractedNumericClaim]
778	    relative_difference: float      # 0.0-1.0+ (|a-b|/min(|a|,|b|))
779	    absolute_difference: float
780	    severity: str                   # "low" / "medium" / "high"
781	    recommended_action: str = (
782	        "Disclose both values with their source tiers in the final report. "
783	        "If one source is T1 (RCT) and another is T5 (industry), note the "
784	        "authority gap alongside the numeric gap."
785	    )
786	    # ── A17 commensurability guard (I-arch-006 #1262) ────────────────────────
787	    # not_comparable=True marks a group whose claims carry POSITIVELY-DIVERGENT
788	    # physical quantity kinds (e.g. a 0.5-degree yaw angle bucketed with a 100 m
789	    # radar distance because both flattened to unit="") — an INCOMMENSURABLE
790	    # pairing. The numeric gap across such a pairing is a category error (the run
791	    # surfaced absolute_difference 99.5 / 199% relative across degrees-vs-meters),
792	    # so when this fires the surfaced relative_difference/absolute_difference are
793	    # nulled to None (no misleading magnitude) and the record is kept OUT of the
794	    # headline contradiction count — but every claim/source is still DISCLOSED
795	    # (never dropped). This is a VALIDITY/WEIGHT check, NOT a faithfulness-gate
796	    # change: strict_verify / NLI / 4-role thresholds are untouched, and a real
797	    # same-quantity contradiction is unaffected (both default fields stay False/"").
798	    not_comparable: bool = False
799	    incommensurable_reason: str = ""
800	
801	
802	# The Wave-3 dormant discriminator fields added to ExtractedNumericClaim (I-arch-002 [2]).
803	# They are read ONLY by claim_graph.build_merge_key under PG_SWEEP_CREDIBILITY_REDESIGN and
804	# must NOT appear in ANY serialized artifact on the OFF path, or OFF byte-identity breaks
805	# (the legacy contradictions.json never had these keys). asdict() emits EVERY dataclass
806	# field, so every site that serializes a ContradictionRecord routes through
807	# serialize_contradiction_record() which strips them when the flag is OFF.
808	_WAVE3_DORMANT_NUMERIC_FIELDS = (
809	    "dose_frequency", "comparator", "route_formulation",
810	    "effect_measure", "direction", "population",
811	)
812	
813	
814	# A17 record-level commensurability fields (I-arch-006 #1262). asdict() emits
815	# every dataclass field, so to preserve byte-identity for the legacy / clinical /
816	# comparable records (whose JSON never had these keys) they are STRIPPED whenever
817	# they sit at their inert defaults (not_comparable=False AND empty reason). They
818	# are emitted ONLY on a record where the A17 guard positively fired — the new,
819	# correct behavior is allowed to add keys exactly on the records it changed.
820	_A17_RECORD_FIELDS = ("not_comparable", "incommensurable_reason")
821	
822	
823	def serialize_contradiction_record(record: "ContradictionRecord") -> dict:
824	    """asdict a numeric ContradictionRecord for contradictions.json / manifest / PT08.
825	
826	    When PG_SWEEP_CREDIBILITY_REDESIGN is OFF the Wave-3 dormant discriminator fields are
827	    STRIPPED from each nested claim so the serialized bytes are byte-identical to the
828	    pre-Slice-B tree (Claude Slice-B iter-2 Fix C). When ON the full field set is emitted
829	    (the redesign is the new behavior; ON is allowed to differ). endpoint_phrase/arm are
830	    pre-existing and always retained.
831	
832	    A17 (I-arch-006 #1262): the record-level commensurability fields
833	    (not_comparable / incommensurable_reason) are stripped whenever they carry their
834	    inert defaults, so a comparable / clinical record serializes byte-identically to
835	    the pre-A17 tree; they appear ONLY on a record the A17 guard actually marked.
836	    """
837	    d = asdict(record)
838	    if not _credibility_redesign_enabled():
839	        for claim in d.get("claims", []) or []:
840	            for fname in _WAVE3_DORMANT_NUMERIC_FIELDS:
841	                claim.pop(fname, None)
842	    # A17: keep inert (default) records byte-identical — strip the new keys unless
843	    # the guard positively fired on THIS record.
844	    if not d.get("not_comparable") and not (d.get("incommensurable_reason") or ""):
845	        for fname in _A17_RECORD_FIELDS:
846	            d.pop(fname, None)
847	    return d

850	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
851	# Extraction
852	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
853	
854	
855	def _normalize_predicate(
856	    text: str, domain: str | None = None,
857	) -> Optional[str]:
858	    """Return the predicate keyword if present in the text.
859	
860	    BUG-M-202 fix (deep-dive R7): when a domain is supplied, prefer
861	    domain-specific predicates first (higher specificity), then fall
862	    back to the union set. Previously, only the metabolic-centric
863	    union was checked — so AF anticoagulation queries returned zero
864	    matches because stroke/bleeding vocabulary wasn't in the table.
865	    """
866	    t = (text or "").lower()
867	    # Domain-specific first (more specific endpoints win ties).
868	    if domain and domain in _DOMAIN_PREDICATES:
869	        for p in _DOMAIN_PREDICATES[domain]:
870	            if p in t:
871	                return p
872	    # Fall back to union.
873	    for p in _EFFICACY_PREDICATES + _SAFETY_PREDICATES:
874	        if p in t:
875	            return p
876	    return None
877	
878	
879	def _normalize_subject(
880	    text: str, fallback: str = "unknown", general_fallback: bool = False,
881	) -> str:
882	    """Extract the salient subject of the clause: the first drug name, or fallback.
883	
884	    This is the legacy API used when no positional anchor is available.
885	    Prefer _subject_near_position() when you know where the numeric value
886	    is and want to attribute the number to the nearest drug, not the
887	    first drug to appear in the wider text.
888	
889	    B13 (I-arch-011) DOMAIN-GENERAL SUBJECT: ``general_fallback`` is OPT-IN and
890	    defaults False so EVERY pre-existing caller (the clinical numeric path via
891	    ``_subject_near_position`` line 621, and any other consumer) is byte-identical
892	    — the drug-name-only behaviour and its ``fallback`` are unchanged. When the
893	    caller opts in (the qualitative present-vs-absent detector, which runs on
894	    device / procedure / population corpora where NO drug name appears), and the
895	    drug allowlist finds nothing, we fall back to the in-file DOMAIN-AGNOSTIC noun
896	    extractor (``_subject_general_noun``) instead of the empty/`fallback` string.
897	    Without this, a non-drug clinical corpus (e.g. Parkinson / deep-brain-
898	    stimulation safety) resolved EVERY qualitative assertion's subject to "" — so
899	    Pass A (which skips empty-subject assertions) never fired and Pass B collapsed
900	    every unrelated flag into one ``("", concept_type)`` bucket: real T1 safety
901	    signals ("DBS is contraindicated in X") were diluted into indistinguishable
902	    noise. Drug-name precedence is preserved (a named drug still wins), so the
903	    existing clinical golden behaviour is unchanged. The contradiction surface is
904	    ADVISORY (labels, never drops/holds): a real entity subject only makes a true
905	    safety contradiction VISIBLE; faithfulness gates are untouched.
906	    """
907	    from src.polaris_graph.nodes.scope_gate import _DRUG_NAME_RE
908	    m = _DRUG_NAME_RE.search(text or "")
909	    if m:
910	        return m.group(1).lower()
911	    if general_fallback:
912	        noun = _subject_general_noun(text)
913	        if noun:
914	            return noun
915	    return fallback
916	
917	
918	# B13 (I-arch-011): light, dependency-free clause-subject (noun-phrase head)
919	# extraction for ANY domain (device / procedure / population / drug). Consistent
920	# with the in-file ``_subject_generic`` machinery (reuses ``_GENERIC_SUBJECT_STOPWORDS``)
921	# and the field-agnostic 2025 noun-phrase-head approach (head noun + descriptors,
922	# no parser): the salient subject of an English clause is overwhelmingly the
923	# noun-phrase head before the main verb. We take the FIRST content token (skipping
924	# generic statistical / temporal / filler stopwords) — deterministic, so the SAME
925	# entity phrased the same way ("DBS …" on both sides) resolves to the SAME token
926	# and the two assertions group/conflict. Acronyms / proper nouns are preferred so
927	# "DBS" / "Levodopa" win over a leading lowercase filler. Returns "" when no
928	# content token is found (a SAFE empty — the caller keeps its own fallback).
929	_SUBJECT_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(r"[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9\-]{1,}")
930	
931	
932	def _subject_general_noun(text: str) -> str:
933	    """First salient content-word subject token of the clause, or "" if none.
934	
935	    Domain-agnostic, deterministic, no LLM, no parser, never raises. Skips the
936	    shared generic statistical/temporal/measurement filler stopwords so a real
937	    entity (device, procedure, population, condition) anchors the subject. An
938	    ALL-CAPS acronym or a Capitalized proper noun in the clause is preferred over
939	    a leading lowercase common word (e.g. "DBS" over "the"), because the named
940	    entity is the safety subject the reader cares about."""
941	    if not text:
942	        return ""
943	    tokens = _SUBJECT_TOKEN_RE.findall(text)
944	    first_content: str = ""
945	    for tok in tokens:
946	        low = tok.lower()
947	        if low in _GENERIC_SUBJECT_STOPWORDS:
948	            continue
949	        # Prefer a named entity (acronym / proper noun) if one appears in-clause.
950	        if tok.isupper() or tok[:1].isupper():
951	            return low
952	        if not first_content:
953	            first_content = low
954	    return first_content
955	
956	
957	def _subject_near_position(
958	    text: str,
959	    anchor_pos: int,
960	    fallback: str = "unknown",
961	    window: int = 150,
962	) -> str:
963	    """R-5 Fix B: return the drug name whose match is CLOSEST to
964	    anchor_pos in `text`, within ±window chars.
965	
966	    Used for cross-drug comparisons like:
967	        "Eli Lilly's Zepbound achieving 25.5% compared to Novo Nordisk's
968	        CagriSema at 23%, with Lilly's retatrutide at 28.7% and ..."
969	
970	    Legacy `_normalize_subject(quote)` would return the first drug in
971	    the full quote (often 'retatrutide' here, even though 25.5% belongs
972	    to Zepbound). This function searches all drug matches inside the
973	    ±window chars around anchor_pos and returns the match with the
974	    smallest absolute distance.
975	
976	    Falls back to _normalize_subject(text) (first-in-text) only if
977	    ZERO matches are found inside the window. Returns `fallback` if
978	    still nothing.
979	    """
980	    from src.polaris_graph.nodes.scope_gate import _DRUG_NAME_RE
981	
982	    if not text:
983	        return fallback
984	    lo = max(0, anchor_pos - window)
985	    hi = min(len(text), anchor_pos + window)
986	    local = text[lo:hi]
987	    matches = list(_DRUG_NAME_RE.finditer(local))
988	    if not matches:
989	        # Widen search to full text as last resort
990	        return _normalize_subject(text, fallback)
991	    # Pick the match whose center is closest to the (anchor_pos - lo)
992	    # position inside `local`.
993	    anchor_local = anchor_pos - lo
994	    best = min(
995	        matches,
996	        key=lambda m: abs(((m.start() + m.end()) // 2) - anchor_local),
997	    )
998	    return best.group(1).lower()
999	
1000	
1001	# Fix-1 reject patterns. Numbers that sit inside these contexts are
1002	# filtered BEFORE we pull the value, because they're structurally not
1003	# the claim-under-measurement:
1004	#   - "placebo N%" / "vs placebo" / "versus placebo" → comparator arm
1005	#   - "≥5%" / "at least 5%" / "5% or more" / "5% threshold" → achievement
1006	#     threshold, not the value being measured
1007	#   - "STEP N" / "SUSTAIN N" → trial-program integer, not a value
1008	#   - "week N" / "month N" / "year N" → duration
1009	_PLACEBO_CONTEXT_RE = re.compile(
1010	    r"(?:vs\.?|versus|v\.)\s+placebo|"
1011	    r"placebo\s+(?:recipients|group|arm|patients)|"
1012	    r"in\s+(?:the\s+)?placebo",
1013	    re.IGNORECASE,
1014	)
1015	_ACHIEVEMENT_THRESHOLD_RE = re.compile(
1016	    r"(?:at\s+least|≥|>=|>)\s*-?\d+(?:\.\d+)?\s*%|"
1017	    r"-?\d+(?:\.\d+)?\s*%\s*(?:or\s+more|or\s+greater|threshold|achievement)",
1018	    re.IGNORECASE,
1019	)
1020	_TRIAL_ACRONYM_NUM_RE = re.compile(
1021	    r"\b(?:STEP|SUSTAIN|SURPASS|SURMOUNT|REWIND|LEADER|PIONEER|SELECT)\s*-?\s*\d+",
1022	    re.IGNORECASE,
1023	)
1024	_DURATION_NUM_RE = re.compile(
1025	    r"(?:week|month|year|day)s?\s+-?\d+(?:\.\d+)?|"
1026	    r"-?\d+(?:\.\d+)?\s*(?:week|month|year|day)s?",
1027	    re.IGNORECASE,
1028	)
1029	# B13 (I-arch-011) confidence-interval reject. A "95% CI" / "95 % confidence
1030	# interval" token is the STATISTICAL CONFIDENCE LEVEL, not a measured outcome
1031	# value — the run misparsed the "95%" in "Mortality reduction (95% CI 2-9)" as a
1032	# 95% mortality value, then flagged a fake 3-contradiction artifact against a real
1033	# single-digit mortality figure. We reject a number that is the confidence LEVEL
1034	# directly before a CI / confidence-interval cue. We DO NOT reject a number merely
1035	# because the wider sentence contains a CI elsewhere (that would suppress the real
1036	# point estimate "8%" in "reduced to 8% (95% CI 5-12)"), and we DO NOT reject a
1037	# plain "95% of patients" value (no CI cue). Word-boundary "CI" only, so it never
1038	# fires on an unrelated capitalised "Ci"-prefixed token mid-word.
1039	_CONFIDENCE_INTERVAL_LEVEL_RE = re.compile(
1040	    r"-?\d+(?:\.\d+)?\s*%?\s*"
1041	    r"(?:\bci\b|confidence\s+interval|credible\s+interval)",
1042	    re.IGNORECASE,
1043	)
1044	
1045	
1046	def _is_confidence_level_at(text: str, num_pos: int) -> bool:
1047	    """True iff the number at ``num_pos`` in ``text`` is the confidence LEVEL of a
1048	    confidence/credible interval ("95% CI", "95 % confidence interval").
1049	
1050	    Anchored on the number's own position: a CI cue elsewhere in the row does NOT
1051	    reject a real point-estimate value. Pure, never raises."""
1052	    if not text:
1053	        return False
1054	    for m in _CONFIDENCE_INTERVAL_LEVEL_RE.finditer(text):
1055	        if m.start() == num_pos:
1056	            return True
1057	    return False
1058	
1059	
1060	# The CI cue token itself ("CI" / "confidence interval" / "credible interval"),
1061	# used to mark the START of a CI BOUND region. The region runs from the cue to the
1062	# closing parenthesis/bracket (or the next ';' / sentence end), so the interval
1063	# BOUNDS inside it ("2-9", "5 to 12") are not emitted as metric values either.
1064	_CI_CUE_RE = re.compile(
1065	    r"\b(?:ci|confidence\s+interval|credible\s+interval)\b", re.IGNORECASE,
1066	)
1067	
1068	
1069	def _ci_bound_regions(text: str) -> list[tuple[int, int]]:
1070	    """Char spans that hold confidence/credible-interval BOUNDS (not a value).
1071	
1072	    Each region starts just after a CI cue and ends at the next closing ``)``/``]``
1073	    (or ``;`` / sentence end) — the numbers inside are interval bounds, not a
1074	    measured outcome. Pure, never raises."""
1075	    regions: list[tuple[int, int]] = []
1076	    for m in _CI_CUE_RE.finditer(text or ""):
1077	        start = m.end()
1078	        end = len(text)
1079	        for ch_pos in range(start, len(text)):
1080	            if text[ch_pos] in ")];":
1081	                end = ch_pos
1082	                break
1083	        regions.append((start, end))
1084	    return regions
1085	
1086	
1087	def _in_ci_bound_region(num_pos: int, regions: list[tuple[int, int]]) -> bool:
1088	    """True iff ``num_pos`` falls inside any CI-bound region. Pure."""
1089	    return any(lo <= num_pos < hi for lo, hi in regions)
1090	
1091	
1092	# A valid claim usually carries a value-phrase verb. "Achieved 14.9%",
1093	# "reduced by 12%", "loss of 15%", "mean change -14.9%". This
1094	# whitelist prevents us from pulling random decimals that happen to be
1095	# in the quote.
1096	_VALUE_PHRASE_VERBS = re.compile(
1097	    r"(?:achiev\w+|reduc\w+|los[ts]?|loss|decreas\w+|"
1098	    r"mean\s+(?:change|weight|reduction|loss|difference)|"
1099	    r"experienc\w+\s+a?\s*\d+|"
1100	    r"reported|produc\w+|demonstrat\w+|"
1101	    r"at\s+(?:week|month|year)\s+\d+)",
1102	    re.IGNORECASE,
1103	)
1104	
1105	# ── Wave-3 master flag (I-arch-002) ──────────────────────────────────────────
1106	# Read at CALL time (never import-time) so tests can monkeypatch os.environ and
1107	# the OFF path is honoured per-invocation. Mirrors
1108	# credibility_pass._OFF_VALUES semantics; inlined here to avoid importing the
1109	# synthesis layer into retrieval (no cross-layer / circular-import coupling).
1110	_CRED_REDESIGN_FLAG = "PG_SWEEP_CREDIBILITY_REDESIGN"
1111	_CRED_REDESIGN_OFF_VALUES = frozenset({"", "0", "false", "off", "no"})
1112	
1113	
1114	def _credibility_redesign_enabled() -> bool:
1115	    """True when PG_SWEEP_CREDIBILITY_REDESIGN is on. OFF => byte-identical."""
1116	    return (
1117	        os.environ.get(_CRED_REDESIGN_FLAG, "").strip().lower()
1118	        not in _CRED_REDESIGN_OFF_VALUES
1119	    )
1120	
1121	
1122	# Dose pattern (captures the full "X.X mg" string for grouping).
1123	_DOSE_CAPTURE_RE = re.compile(
1124	    r"(\d+(?:\.\d+)?\s*m[gc]g?|\d+(?:\.\d+)?\s*[µu]g|\d+(?:\.\d+)?\s*g\b)",
1125	    re.IGNORECASE,
1126	)
1127	
1128	# Wave-3 variant (I-arch-002 [2], DRIFT-MGKG): preserves a per-weight ('/kg') or
1129	# per-time ('/day','/m2',...) denominator so '5 mg/kg' is DISTINCT from '5 mg'
1130	# (design §4.1/§4.3 — prevents a false dose-response merge). This change feeds
1131	# the LEGACY detect_contradictions grouping key (:596 includes dose) and is
1132	# therefore NOT byte-inert when the master flag is OFF; per the non-negotiable
1133	# byte-identity rule it is GATED behind PG_SWEEP_CREDIBILITY_REDESIGN. When the
1134	# flag is OFF the original _DOSE_CAPTURE_RE path runs verbatim.
1135	_DOSE_CAPTURE_MGKG_RE = re.compile(
1136	    r"(\d+(?:\.\d+)?\s*m[gc]g?|\d+(?:\.\d+)?\s*[µu]g|\d+(?:\.\d+)?\s*g\b)"
1137	    r"(\s*/\s*(?:kg|m2|m\^?2|day|d|hr?|h|dose|week|wk))?",
1138	    re.IGNORECASE,
1139	)

1141	
1142	def _extract_dose(text: str) -> str:
1143	    """Extract the most-salient dose token from the text (for grouping).
1144	
1145	    DRIFT-MGKG (I-arch-002 [2]): under PG_SWEEP_CREDIBILITY_REDESIGN a trailing
1146	    per-weight/per-time denominator ('/kg', '/m2', ...) is preserved so
1147	    '5 mg/kg' != '5 mg'. Flag OFF => original behaviour byte-for-byte.
1148	    """
1149	    if _credibility_redesign_enabled():
1150	        m = _DOSE_CAPTURE_MGKG_RE.search(text or "")
1151	        if not m:
1152	            return ""
1153	        base = m.group(1)
1154	        denom = m.group(2) or ""
1155	        # Normalize internal whitespace out of the denominator ('  / kg' -> '/kg')
1156	        denom = re.sub(r"\s+", "", denom)
1157	        return (base + denom).replace(" ", " ").lower()
1158	    m = _DOSE_CAPTURE_RE.search(text or "")
1159	    if not m:
1160	        return ""
1161	    return m.group(1).replace(" ", " ").lower()
1162

1164	def _detect_placebo_arm(text: str) -> bool:
1165	    if not text:

1168	
1169	
1170	# Pre-existing endpoint patterns (legacy; OFF path uses ONLY these).
1171	_ENDPOINT_PATTERNS_LEGACY = (
1172	    r"at\s+week\s+\d+", r"at\s+\d+\s+weeks?",
1173	    r"at\s+month\s+\d+", r"at\s+\d+\s+months?",
1174	    r"from\s+baseline", r"mean\s+change",
1175	    r"intent\s*-?\s*to\s*-?\s*treat",
1176	    r"per\s+protocol",
1177	    r"trial\s+product\s+estimand",
1178	)
1179	# Wave-3 (I-arch-002 [2]): day + year endpoint patterns. They are appended AFTER the
1180	# legacy patterns so they only fire when no legacy pattern matched, BUT they still
1181	# change the OFF output for inputs that previously returned "" (e.g. "at day 28" with
1182	# no week/month phrase) — and endpoint_phrase feeds BOTH the legacy cluster key
1183	# (_normalized_key_numeric position 7) AND contradictions.json (asdict). Per the
1184	# non-negotiable byte-identity rule they are therefore GATED behind
1185	# PG_SWEEP_CREDIBILITY_REDESIGN (Claude Slice-B iter-2 P1); OFF runs the legacy set
1186	# verbatim.
1187	_ENDPOINT_PATTERNS_WAVE3 = (
1188	    r"at\s+day\s+\d+", r"at\s+\d+\s+days?",
1189	    r"at\s+year\s+\d+", r"at\s+\d+\s+years?",
1190	)
1191	
1192	
1193	def _extract_endpoint_phrase(text: str) -> str:
1194	    """Extract a short endpoint descriptor ('at week 68', 'from baseline').
1195	
1196	    The day/year forms are GATED behind PG_SWEEP_CREDIBILITY_REDESIGN (they would
1197	    otherwise resolve previously-"" inputs and drift the OFF cluster key + serialized
1198	    bytes — Claude Slice-B iter-2 P1). Flag OFF => legacy patterns only, byte-for-byte.
1199	    """
1200	    if not text:
1201	        return ""
1202	    low = text.lower()
1203	    patterns = _ENDPOINT_PATTERNS_LEGACY
1204	    if _credibility_redesign_enabled():
1205	        patterns = patterns + _ENDPOINT_PATTERNS_WAVE3
1206	    for pat in patterns:
1207	        m = re.search(pat, low)
1208	        if m:
1209	            return m.group(0)
1210	    return ""

1213	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
1214	# Wave-3 positive-known discriminator extractors (I-arch-002 [2])
1215	#
1216	# Each returns '' (the UNKNOWN sentinel) unless a POSITIVE token was extracted.
1217	# A defaulted or PREDICATE-DERIVED value is NOT "known" (design §4.3): these
1218	# extractors read the evidence text ONLY, never the predicate. Dormant carriers
1219	# until claim_graph.build_merge_key reads them under PG_SWEEP_CREDIBILITY_REDESIGN.
1220	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1222	# Dosing cadence (per-time schedule). Orthogonal to the per-mass dose axis:
1223	# "15 mg weekly" != "15 mg daily" (the ISMP methotrexate sentinel error).
1224	_DOSE_FREQUENCY_RE = re.compile(
1225	    r"\b(?P<tok>"
1226	    r"q\.?d\.?|b\.?i\.?d\.?|t\.?i\.?d\.?|q\.?i\.?d\.?|"   # latin abbreviations
1227	    r"q\s*\d+\s*h|q\s*\d+\s*hours?|"                       # q8h / q 12 hours
1228	    r"once[-\s]?(?:daily|a\s+day|per\s+day|weekly|a\s+week)|"
1229	    r"twice[-\s]?(?:daily|a\s+day|per\s+day|weekly|a\s+week)|"
1230	    r"three\s+times[-\s]?(?:daily|a\s+day|per\s+day)|"
1231	    r"daily|weekly|biweekly|fortnightly|monthly"
1232	    r")\b",
1233	    re.IGNORECASE,
1234	)
1235	
1236	# Normalize a matched cadence token to a canonical key.
1237	_DOSE_FREQUENCY_NORMALIZE = (
1238	    (re.compile(r"^q\.?d\.?$", re.IGNORECASE), "qd"),
1239	    (re.compile(r"^b\.?i\.?d\.?$", re.IGNORECASE), "bid"),
1240	    (re.compile(r"^t\.?i\.?d\.?$", re.IGNORECASE), "tid"),
1241	    (re.compile(r"^q\.?i\.?d\.?$", re.IGNORECASE), "qid"),
1242	    (re.compile(r"^q\s*(\d+)\s*h(?:ours?)?$", re.IGNORECASE), r"q\1h"),
1243	    (re.compile(r"^once[-\s]?(?:daily|a\s+day|per\s+day)$", re.IGNORECASE), "qd"),
1244	    (re.compile(r"^twice[-\s]?(?:daily|a\s+day|per\s+day)$", re.IGNORECASE), "bid"),
1245	    (re.compile(r"^three\s+times[-\s]?(?:daily|a\s+day|per\s+day)$", re.IGNORECASE), "tid"),
1246	    (re.compile(r"^once[-\s]?(?:weekly|a\s+week)$", re.IGNORECASE), "weekly"),
1247	    (re.compile(r"^twice[-\s]?(?:weekly|a\s+week)$", re.IGNORECASE), "biweekly"),
1248	)

1251	def _extract_dose_frequency(text: str) -> str:
1252	    """Cadence token (qd/bid/tid/q\\d+h/once-daily/weekly/...) or '' if none."""
1253	    if not text:
1254	        return ""
1255	    m = _DOSE_FREQUENCY_RE.search(text)
1256	    if not m:
1257	        return ""
1258	    raw = re.sub(r"\s+", " ", m.group("tok").strip()).lower()
1259	    for pat, repl in _DOSE_FREQUENCY_NORMALIZE:
1260	        if pat.match(raw):
1261	            return pat.sub(repl, raw)
1262	    return raw

1266	# noun phrase (short, stops at clause punctuation).
1267	_COMPARATOR_RE = re.compile(
1268	    r"(?:vs\.?|versus|compared\s+(?:to|with)|relative\s+to)\s+"
1269	    r"(?P<comp>[a-z0-9][a-z0-9\-\s]{0,40}?)"
1270	    r"(?=[,.;:)\]]|\s+(?:at|in|with|for|over|by|and|was|were|achiev|reduc)\b|$)",
1271	    re.IGNORECASE,
1272	)

1275	def _extract_comparator(text: str) -> str:
1276	    """The 'vs/versus/compared-to X' comparator phrase, or '' if none."""

1285	# Route / formulation: IV / PO / SC / IM / ER / XR / SR, plus spelled-out forms.
1286	_ROUTE_FORMULATION_RE = re.compile(
1287	    r"\b(?P<tok>"
1288	    r"i\.?v\.?|intravenous(?:ly)?|"
1289	    r"p\.?o\.?|per\s+os|oral(?:ly)?|by\s+mouth|"
1290	    r"s\.?c\.?|subcutaneous(?:ly)?|subq|"
1291	    r"i\.?m\.?|intramuscular(?:ly)?|"
1292	    r"e\.?r\.?|x\.?r\.?|s\.?r\.?|"
1293	    r"extended[-\s]?release|sustained[-\s]?release|immediate[-\s]?release"
1294	    r")\b",
1295	    re.IGNORECASE,

1297	_ROUTE_FORMULATION_NORMALIZE = (
1298	    (re.compile(r"^(?:i\.?v\.?|intravenous(?:ly)?)$", re.IGNORECASE), "iv"),
1299	    (re.compile(r"^(?:p\.?o\.?|per\s+os|oral(?:ly)?|by\s+mouth)$", re.IGNORECASE), "po"),
1300	    (re.compile(r"^(?:s\.?c\.?|subcutaneous(?:ly)?|subq)$", re.IGNORECASE), "sc"),
1301	    (re.compile(r"^(?:i\.?m\.?|intramuscular(?:ly)?)$", re.IGNORECASE), "im"),
1302	    (re.compile(r"^(?:e\.?r\.?|extended[-\s]?release)$", re.IGNORECASE), "er"),
1303	    (re.compile(r"^(?:x\.?r\.?)$", re.IGNORECASE), "xr"),
1304	    (re.compile(r"^(?:s\.?r\.?|sustained[-\s]?release)$", re.IGNORECASE), "sr"),
1305	    (re.compile(r"^immediate[-\s]?release$", re.IGNORECASE), "ir"),

1310	    """Route/formulation token (iv/po/sc/im/er/...) or '' if none."""

1324	# measure word. "30% relative risk reduction" != "30% absolute risk reduction".
1325	_EFFECT_MEASURE_RE = (
1326	    (re.compile(r"\brelative\s+risk\s+reduction\b|\brelative\s+reduction\b|"
1327	                r"\brelative\s+risk\b|\brrr\b", re.IGNORECASE), "relative"),
1328	    (re.compile(r"\babsolute\s+risk\s+reduction\b|\babsolute\s+reduction\b|"
1329	                r"\babsolute\s+risk\b|\barr\b", re.IGNORECASE), "absolute"),
1330	    (re.compile(r"\bhazard\s+ratio\b|\bhr\b", re.IGNORECASE), "hr"),
1331	    (re.compile(r"\bodds\s+ratio\b|\bor\b", re.IGNORECASE), "or"),
1332	    (re.compile(r"\brisk\s+ratio\b|\brate\s+ratio\b|\brr\b", re.IGNORECASE), "rr"),

1337	    """Effect-measure type (relative/absolute/hr/or/rr) on explicit word, else ''."""
1338	    if not text:
1339	        return ""
1340	    for pat, label in _EFFECT_MEASURE_RE:
1341	        if pat.search(text):
1342	            return label
1343	    return ""

1345	
1346	# Direction: EXPLICIT clinical increase/decrease token ONLY. NEVER inferred from
1347	# the predicate (design §4.3 — the predicate-derived fallback made "rose 5%" and
1348	# "fell 5%" merge and broke design test #5).
1349	#
1350	# Codex Slice-B P1 (over-merge narrowing): BARE QUANTITY COMPARATIVES + ambiguous
1351	# nouns are EXCLUDED — "more"/"less"/"fewer" (a magnitude comparison, not a
1352	# clinical direction) and "loss"/"lost" (the outcome NOUN, e.g. "weight loss" is
1353	# the predicate, not a fall in a metric). Including them converted an otherwise-
1354	# UNKNOWN direction into a positive discriminator, making two distinct claims
1355	# merge-eligible instead of safe singletons. The retained tokens are explicit
1356	# directional verbs/adjectives only (increased/decreased/rose/fell/reduced/
1357	# reduction/elevated/declined/lowered/raised + clear motion synonyms). Unknown
1358	# direction returns '' so the merge-key slot forces a singleton (safe under-merge).
1359	_DIRECTION_INCREASE_RE = re.compile(
1360	    r"\b(?:increas\w+|rose|rise|risen|rising|higher|elevat\w+|"
1361	    r"rais\w+|up\s+by|grew|grow\w*)\b",
1362	    re.IGNORECASE,
1363	)
1364	_DIRECTION_DECREASE_RE = re.compile(
1365	    r"\b(?:decreas\w+|reduc\w+|reduction|fell|fall\w*|fallen|drop\w*|lower\w*|"
1366	    r"declin\w+|down\s+by|shrank|shrunk)\b",
1367	    re.IGNORECASE,
1368	)

1371	def _extract_direction(text: str) -> str:
1372	    """'increase'/'decrease' from an EXPLICIT token only; '' if none/ambiguous.
1373	
1374	    Token-only by design (§4.3): never derived from the predicate. When BOTH an
1375	    increase and a decrease token appear the direction is ambiguous -> '' (keep
1376	    separate / fail-closed at the merge key).
1377	    """
1378	    if not text:
1379	        return ""
1380	    up = bool(_DIRECTION_INCREASE_RE.search(text))
1381	    down = bool(_DIRECTION_DECREASE_RE.search(text))
1382	    if up and not down:
1383	        return "increase"
1384	    if down and not up:
1385	        return "decrease"
1386	    return ""

1389	# Population / cohort: "in patients with X" / "in adults with X" / "among ...".
1390	# The terminating lookahead stops the cohort phrase at a clause boundary or the
1391	# onset of an outcome verb. Verb stems (achiev/reduc/...) intentionally carry NO
1392	# trailing \b so they match the inflected forms ("achieved", "reduced").
1393	_POPULATION_RE = re.compile(
1394	    r"\b(?:in|among)\s+"
1395	    r"(?P<pop>(?:patients?|adults?|children|subjects?|participants?|women|men|"
1396	    r"individuals?|people)\b[a-z0-9\-\s]{0,50}?)"
1397	    r"(?=[,.;:)\]]|\s+(?:achiev|reduc|experienc|demonstrat|report|produc|"
1398	    r"had\b|who\s+receiv|with\s+a\b)|$)",
1399	    re.IGNORECASE,

1403	def _extract_population(text: str) -> str:
1404	    """The 'in/among patients with X' cohort phrase, or '' if none."""
1405	    if not text:
1406	        return ""
