2419	# A record whose predicate carries the ``[possible_metric_mismatch]`` marker is the
2420	# detector DECLARING it could NOT positively confirm a shared metric (comparator /
2421	# population / time-window may differ). Its surfaced ``relative_difference`` is the
2422	# RAW magnitude across two numbers that may not measure the same quantity — on the
2423	# cert artifact this rendered ``rel_diff`` up to 437,481.7% as a headline "quality
2424	# signal", which is meaningless (and lethal in clinical context per §-1.1). This is
2425	# the same class the production render path already suppresses
2426	# (``run_honest_sweep_r3._render_contradicts_block`` skips the marker), but the
2427	# plain-text ``format_contradictions_for_user`` summary still counted + rendered it
2428	# with the junk magnitude. We route a marker-bearing record OUT of the headline
2429	# contradiction count into a dedicated "possible metric mismatch" disclosure (no
2430	# misleading rel/abs magnitude) — every claim/source still DISCLOSED (never dropped,
2431	# §-1.3). Default-ON kill-switch ``PG_CONTRADICTION_SUPPRESS_METRIC_MISMATCH`` so the
2432	# OFF path is byte-identical to the pre-fix output. This is a render/telemetry change
2433	# only; no faithfulness threshold is touched and a CONFIRMED shared-metric
2434	# contradiction (no marker) is unaffected.
2435	_SUPPRESS_METRIC_MISMATCH_FLAG = "PG_CONTRADICTION_SUPPRESS_METRIC_MISMATCH"
2436	_SUPPRESS_METRIC_MISMATCH_OFF_VALUES = frozenset({"0", "false", "off", "no"})
2437	
2438	
2439	def _suppress_metric_mismatch_enabled() -> bool:
2440	    """True (default) unless ``PG_CONTRADICTION_SUPPRESS_METRIC_MISMATCH`` is OFF.
2441	
2442	    Default-ON: an unset/empty env var keeps the suppression ON. Set to
2443	    ``0``/``false``/``off``/``no`` to restore the pre-fix byte-identical headline."""
2444	    return (
2445	        os.environ.get(_SUPPRESS_METRIC_MISMATCH_FLAG, "").strip().lower()
2446	        not in _SUPPRESS_METRIC_MISMATCH_OFF_VALUES
2447	    )
2448	
2449	
2450	def _is_possible_metric_mismatch_record(record: "ContradictionRecord") -> bool:
2451	    """True iff ``record``'s predicate carries the possible_metric_mismatch marker.
2452	
2453	    A marker-bearing record is an UNCONFIRMED-shared-metric pairing, not a confirmed
2454	    contradiction. Pure, never raises."""
2455	    return POSSIBLE_METRIC_MISMATCH_MARKER in str(getattr(record, "predicate", "") or "")
2456	
2457	
2458	def format_contradictions_for_user(
2459	    records: list[ContradictionRecord],
2460	) -> str:
2461	    """Human-readable plain-text summary.
2462	
2463	    A17 (I-arch-006 #1262): an incommensurable / `not_comparable` record (a bucket
2464	    that mixed divergent physical quantity kinds) is EXCLUDED from the headline
2465	    contradiction count and rendered as "not comparable" instead of a misleading
2466	    rel/abs magnitude. Its claims are still listed (disclosed, never dropped) under
2467	    a separate notice so the reader sees why the numbers are not a real
2468	    disagreement.
2469	
2470	    I-deepfix-001 B18 (#1344): a ``possible_metric_mismatch``-marked record is
2471	    likewise routed OUT of the headline contradiction count into a dedicated
2472	    "possible metric mismatch" disclosure (no junk rel/abs magnitude), matching the
2473	    production render path. Default-ON via ``PG_CONTRADICTION_SUPPRESS_METRIC_MISMATCH``;
2474	    set the flag OFF for the pre-fix byte-identical headline. Faithfulness untouched.
2475	    """
2476	    if not records:
2477	        return "No contradictions detected in the evidence corpus."
2478	    suppress_mismatch = _suppress_metric_mismatch_enabled()
2479	    not_comparable = [r for r in records if getattr(r, "not_comparable", False)]
2480	    metric_mismatch = [
2481	        r
2482	        for r in records
2483	        if not getattr(r, "not_comparable", False)
2484	        and suppress_mismatch
2485	        and _is_possible_metric_mismatch_record(r)
2486	    ]
2487	    comparable = [
2488	        r
2489	        for r in records
2490	        if not getattr(r, "not_comparable", False)
2491	        and not (suppress_mismatch and _is_possible_metric_mismatch_record(r))
2492	    ]
2493	    lines = [
2494	        f"Detected {len(comparable)} contradiction(s) in the evidence corpus.",
2495	        "",
2496	    ]
2497	    for i, r in enumerate(comparable, 1):
2498	        lines.append(
2499	            f"[{i}] {r.subject} / {r.predicate} — "
2500	            f"severity={r.severity}, rel_diff={r.relative_difference*100:.1f}%, "
2501	            f"abs_diff={r.absolute_difference:.2f} {r.claims[0].unit}"
2502	        )
2503	        for c in r.claims:
2504	            lines.append(
2505	                f"    - {c.value} {c.unit}   "
2506	                f"[ev={c.evidence_id}, tier={c.source_tier}]   "
2507	                f"{c.context_snippet[:120]}"
2508	            )
2509	        lines.append(f"    Action: {r.recommended_action}")
2510	        lines.append("")
2511	    if metric_mismatch:
2512	        lines.append(
2513	            f"Also surfaced {len(metric_mismatch)} possible-metric-mismatch bucket(s) "
2514	            "(shared metric NOT confirmed — comparator/population/time-window may "
2515	            "differ; NOT counted as a contradiction):"
2516	        )
2517	        lines.append("")
2518	        for k, r in enumerate(metric_mismatch, 1):
2519	            lines.append(
2520	                f"[PM{k}] {r.subject} / {r.predicate} — possible metric mismatch "
2521	                "(no asserted disagreement magnitude until a shared metric is confirmed)"
2522	            )
2523	            for c in r.claims:
2524	                lines.append(
2525	                    f"    - {c.value} {c.unit}   "
2526	                    f"[ev={c.evidence_id}, tier={c.source_tier}]   "
2527	                    f"{c.context_snippet[:120]}"
2528	                )
2529	            lines.append(f"    Action: {r.recommended_action}")
2530	            lines.append("")
2531	    if not_comparable:
2532	        lines.append(
2533	            f"Also surfaced {len(not_comparable)} not-comparable bucket(s) "
2534	            "(numbers measure different quantities — NOT a contradiction):"
2535	        )
2536	        lines.append("")
2537	        for j, r in enumerate(not_comparable, 1):
2538	            lines.append(
2539	                f"[NC{j}] {r.subject} / {r.predicate} — not comparable: "
2540	                f"{r.incommensurable_reason}"
2541	            )
2542	            for c in r.claims:
2543	                lines.append(
2544	                    f"    - {c.value} {c.unit}   "
2545	                    f"[ev={c.evidence_id}, tier={c.source_tier}]   "
2546	                    f"{c.context_snippet[:120]}"
2547	                )
2548	            lines.append(f"    Action: {r.recommended_action}")
2549	            lines.append("")

content.text: This large file is exhaustively documented, but names themselves (functions/classes/vars) are all professional and descriptive. No flags here. Now crag_retriever.py.

content.content:
1	"""CRAG Retriever (v2 Layer 1 — Loopholes L6, L7).
2	
3	Replaces v1's 126-call LLM analyzer with $0 local embeddings.
4	
5	Pipeline:
6	    1. Fetch raw documents (URLs from search node)
7	    2. Context-Enriched Chunking (L6): prepend title+abstract to every chunk
8	    3. MinHash dedup (L7): eliminate near-duplicate chunks BEFORE scoring
9	    4. Embed chunks + query via all-MiniLM-L6-v2
10	    5. Cosine-score → quality tier (GOLD / SILVER / BRONZE)
11	    6. CRAG confidence gate: CORRECT / AMBIGUOUS / INCORRECT
12	    7. Register unique sources in SourceRegistry (L1)
13	    8. Return EvidencePiece list ready for LangGraph state
14	
15	Cost: $0 (all local). Time: ~5-10s for 200 chunks.
16	"""
17	
18	from __future__ import annotations
19	
20	import asyncio
21	import hashlib
22	import logging
23	import os
24	import re
25	import uuid
26	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
27	from typing import Any, Optional
28	
29	import numpy as np
30	from dotenv import load_dotenv
31	
32	from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.pooled_embedder import embed_with_pooling
33	from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.source_registry import SourceRegistry
34	
35	load_dotenv()
36	logger = logging.getLogger("polaris_graph")
37	
38	
39	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
40	# Configuration (all from .env, LAW VI)
41	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
42	
43	@dataclass(frozen=True)
44	class CRAGConfig:
45	    """Immutable configuration for the CRAG retriever."""
46	
47	    # Chunking (L6)
48	    chunk_size_tokens: int = int(os.getenv("PG_CRAG_CHUNK_SIZE", "1024"))
49	    chunk_overlap_tokens: int = int(os.getenv("PG_CRAG_CHUNK_OVERLAP", "128"))
50	    abstract_max_chars: int = int(os.getenv("PG_CRAG_ABSTRACT_MAX_CHARS", "500"))
51	
52	    # Dedup (L7) — reuses existing MinHash infra
53	    dedup_threshold: float = float(os.getenv("PG_CRAG_DEDUP_THRESHOLD", "0.85"))
54	    dedup_num_hashes: int = int(os.getenv("PG_CRAG_DEDUP_HASHES", "128"))
55	    dedup_shingle_size: int = int(os.getenv("PG_CRAG_DEDUP_SHINGLE", "3"))
56	
57	    # Embedding scoring
58	    gold_threshold: float = float(os.getenv("PG_CRAG_GOLD_THRESHOLD", "0.55"))
59	    silver_threshold: float = float(os.getenv("PG_CRAG_SILVER_THRESHOLD", "0.35"))
60	    min_relevance: float = float(os.getenv("PG_CRAG_MIN_RELEVANCE", "0.20"))
61	
62	    # CRAG gate thresholds (Yan et al. 2024)
63	    correct_threshold: float = float(os.getenv("PG_CRAG_CORRECT_THRESHOLD", "0.50"))
64	    incorrect_threshold: float = float(os.getenv("PG_CRAG_INCORRECT_THRESHOLD", "0.25"))
65	
66	    # Capacity
67	    max_evidence: int = int(os.getenv("PG_CRAG_MAX_EVIDENCE", "200"))
68	    max_chunks_per_doc: int = int(os.getenv("PG_CRAG_MAX_CHUNKS_PER_DOC", "30"))
69	    min_chunk_chars: int = int(os.getenv("PG_CRAG_MIN_CHUNK_CHARS", "100"))
70	
71	    # Fix #3 (Table Bomb): max chars for a single table before row-splitting
72	    max_table_chars: int = int(os.getenv("PG_CRAG_MAX_TABLE_CHARS", "4000"))
73	
74	    # Fix #4 (Paywall Poison): min chars for fetched content to be usable
75	    paywall_min_chars: int = int(os.getenv("PG_CRAG_PAYWALL_MIN_CHARS", "1000"))
76	
77	
78	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
79	# Data classes
80	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
81	
82	@dataclass
83	class RawDocument:
84	    """A fetched document from the search node."""
85	
86	    url: str
87	    title: str
88	    content: str
89	    source_type: str = "web"          # web, academic, pdf, government
90	    authors: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
91	    year: Optional[int] = None
92	    venue: str = ""
93	    doi: str = ""
94	    abstract: str = ""                # extracted or first paragraph
95	    authority_score: float = 0.0      # from source_confidence pipeline
96	
97	
98	@dataclass
99	class EnrichedChunk:
100	    """A chunk with context prefix prepended (L6).
101	
102	    The context prefix (title + abstract) gives the embedding model
103	    document-level context that a bare chunk would lack.
104	
105	    IMPORTANT (Fix #4 — Attention Dilution):
106	        enriched_text is for EMBEDDING SCORING ONLY.
107	        When building synthesis prompts, use raw_text and group chunks
108	        by SRC-NNN, printing source metadata ONCE per source.
109	        Do NOT feed enriched_text to the section writer LLM.
110	    """
111	
112	    chunk_id: str                     # deterministic hash
113	    doc_url: str
114	    doc_title: str
115	    raw_text: str                     # original chunk text (without prefix)
116	    enriched_text: str                # prefix + raw_text (for EMBEDDING ONLY)
117	    char_start: int                   # offset in original document
118	    char_end: int
119	    chunk_index: int                  # 0-based position within document
120	    is_table: bool = False            # True if chunk is an intact markdown table
121	    source_type: str = "web"
122	    authors: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
123	    year: Optional[int] = None
124	    venue: str = ""
125	    doi: str = ""
126	    authority_score: float = 0.0
127	
128	
129	@dataclass
130	class CRAGResult:
131	    """Output of the CRAG retriever pipeline."""
132	
133	    evidence: list[dict[str, Any]]    # EvidencePiece-compatible dicts
134	    registry: SourceRegistry
135	    stats: dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
136	
137	
138	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
139	# Context-Enriched Chunker (L6)
140	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
141	
142	class ContextEnrichedChunker:
143	    """Markdown-aware splitter with title+abstract prefix (L6).
144	
145	    Two-pass approach (Fix #1 — Markdown Table Cleaver):
146	        Pass 1: Extract structural blocks (tables, code fences) that must
147	                 remain intact. Replace them with sentinel tokens.
148	        Pass 2: Sentence-split the remaining prose normally.
149	        Merge:  Re-insert structural blocks as standalone chunks.
150	
151	    This prevents a markdown table sitting at the 1024-token boundary from
152	    being cleaved in half — chunk A gets headers + 5 rows, chunk B gets
153	    10 headerless rows, and the LLM hallucinates column associations.
154	
155	    Loophole L6: bare 1024-token chunks lose document context.
156	    Fix: prepend "Title: ... | Abstract: ..." to every chunk so the
157	    embedding model scores relevance with full document awareness.
158	    enriched_text is for EMBEDDING ONLY (see Fix #4).
159	    """
160	
161	    # Regex: detect a contiguous markdown table (header + separator + rows)
162	    # Matches: | col | col |\n| --- | --- |\n| val | val |\n...
163	    _TABLE_RE = re.compile(
164	        r"(?:^|\n)"                        # start of text or newline
165	        r"(\|[^\n]+\|\n"                   # header row:  | ... |
166	        r"\|[\s:]*-+[\s:]*(?:\|[\s:]*-+[\s:]*)*\|\n"  # separator: | --- | --- |
167	        r"(?:\|[^\n]+\|\n?)+)",            # data rows:   | ... | (1 or more)
168	        re.MULTILINE,
169	    )
170	
171	    # Regex: detect fenced code blocks (``` ... ```)
172	    _CODE_FENCE_RE = re.compile(
173	        r"(```[^\n]*\n[\s\S]*?```)",
174	        re.MULTILINE,
175	    )
176	
177	    _SENTINEL_PREFIX = "\x00BLOCK_"
178	
179	    def __init__(self, config: CRAGConfig) -> None:
180	        self._config = config
181	        # Approximate tokens as chars/4 (conservative for English)
182	        self._chunk_chars = config.chunk_size_tokens * 4
183	        self._overlap_chars = config.chunk_overlap_tokens * 4
184	
185	    def chunk_document(self, doc: RawDocument) -> list[EnrichedChunk]:
186	        """Split one document into context-enriched chunks."""
187	        content = (doc.content or "").strip()
188	        if len(content) < self._config.min_chunk_chars:
189	            return []
190	
191	        # Build context prefix (L6 — for embedding only, Fix #4)
192	        abstract = self._extract_abstract(doc)
193	        prefix = self._build_prefix(doc.title, abstract)
194	
195	        # Pass 1: extract structural blocks (tables, code fences)
196	        structural_blocks, prose_with_sentinels = self._extract_structural_blocks(content)
197	
198	        # Pass 2: sentence-split the prose (sentinels mark block positions)
199	        raw_chunks = self._split_markdown_aware(prose_with_sentinels, structural_blocks)
200	
201	        enriched: list[EnrichedChunk] = []
202	        for idx, (text, char_start, char_end, is_table) in enumerate(raw_chunks):
203	            if idx >= self._config.max_chunks_per_doc:
204	                break
205	            chunk_id = self._deterministic_id(doc.url, char_start, char_end)
206	            enriched.append(EnrichedChunk(
207	                chunk_id=chunk_id,
208	                doc_url=doc.url,
209	                doc_title=doc.title,
210	                raw_text=text,
211	                enriched_text=f"{prefix}\n\n{text}" if prefix else text,
212	                char_start=char_start,
213	                char_end=char_end,
214	                chunk_index=idx,
215	                is_table=is_table,
216	                source_type=doc.source_type,
217	                authors=doc.authors,
218	                year=doc.year,
219	                venue=doc.venue,
220	                doi=doc.doi,
221	                authority_score=doc.authority_score,
222	            ))
223	
224	        return enriched
225	
226	    def chunk_documents(self, docs: list[RawDocument]) -> list[EnrichedChunk]:
227	        """Chunk multiple documents."""
228	        all_chunks: list[EnrichedChunk] = []
229	        for doc in docs:
230	            all_chunks.extend(self.chunk_document(doc))
231	        return all_chunks
232	
233	    # -- Pass 1: structural block extraction -------------------------------
234	
235	    def _extract_structural_blocks(
236	        self, text: str
237	    ) -> tuple[dict[str, tuple[str, int, int, bool]], str]:
238	        """Extract tables and code fences, replace with sentinels.
239	
240	        Returns:
241	            (blocks_map, modified_text)
242	            blocks_map: sentinel_key -> (original_text, char_start, char_end, is_table)
243	        """
244	        blocks: dict[str, tuple[str, int, int, bool]] = {}
245	        counter = 0
246	
247	        def _replace(match: re.Match, is_table: bool) -> str:
248	            nonlocal counter
249	            key = f"{self._SENTINEL_PREFIX}{counter}\x00"
250	            counter += 1
251	            original = match.group(1) if match.lastindex else match.group(0)
252	            start = match.start(1) if match.lastindex else match.start(0)
253	            end = match.end(1) if match.lastindex else match.end(0)
254	            blocks[key] = (original.strip(), start, end, is_table)
255	            return f"\n{key}\n"
256	
257	        # Extract tables first (higher priority — they contain pipes)
258	        modified = self._TABLE_RE.sub(lambda m: _replace(m, True), text)
259	        # Then code fences
260	        modified = self._CODE_FENCE_RE.sub(lambda m: _replace(m, False), modified)
261	
262	        return blocks, modified
263	
264	    # -- Pass 2: markdown-aware splitting ----------------------------------
265	
266	    def _split_markdown_aware(
267	        self,
268	        text: str,
269	        blocks: dict[str, tuple[str, int, int, bool]],
270	    ) -> list[tuple[str, int, int, bool]]:
271	        """Split prose at sentence boundaries; re-insert structural blocks intact.
272	
273	        Returns list of (chunk_text, char_start, char_end, is_table).
274	        """
275	        # Split into segments: prose segments and sentinel tokens
276	        segments: list[tuple[str, bool]] = []  # (text, is_sentinel)
277	        remaining = text
278	        while remaining:
279	            # Find next sentinel
280	            sentinel_pos = remaining.find(self._SENTINEL_PREFIX)
281	            if sentinel_pos == -1:
282	                segments.append((remaining, False))
283	                break
284	            # Prose before sentinel
285	            if sentinel_pos > 0:
286	                segments.append((remaining[:sentinel_pos], False))
287	            # Find end of sentinel (terminated by \x00)
288	            end_pos = remaining.find("\x00", sentinel_pos + len(self._SENTINEL_PREFIX))
289	            if end_pos == -1:
290	                segments.append((remaining[sentinel_pos:], False))
291	                break
292	            sentinel_key = remaining[sentinel_pos:end_pos + 1]
293	            segments.append((sentinel_key, True))
294	            remaining = remaining[end_pos + 1:]
295	
296	        # Process segments into chunks
297	        chunks: list[tuple[str, int, int, bool]] = []
298	        prose_buffer: list[str] = []
299	        prose_len = 0
300	        # Track approximate char position in original document
301	        approx_pos = 0
302	
303	        for segment_text, is_sentinel in segments:
304	            if is_sentinel and segment_text in blocks:
305	                # Flush prose buffer first
306	                if prose_buffer:
307	                    prose_chunks = self._split_sentences(" ".join(prose_buffer))
308	                    for pc_text, pc_start, pc_end in prose_chunks:
309	                        chunks.append((pc_text, approx_pos + pc_start, approx_pos + pc_end, False))
310	                    approx_pos += prose_len
311	                    prose_buffer = []
312	                    prose_len = 0
313	
314	                # Emit structural block — split oversized tables (Fix R2-#3)
315	                block_text, block_start, block_end, is_table = blocks[segment_text]
316	                if is_table and len(block_text) > self._config.max_table_chars:
317	                    # Split by rows, re-attach header to each sub-table
318	                    for sub_text, sub_start, sub_end in self._split_large_table(
319	                        block_text, block_start, block_end
320	                    ):
321	                        chunks.append((sub_text, sub_start, sub_end, True))
322	                else:
323	                    chunks.append((block_text, block_start, block_end, is_table))
324	            else:
325	                cleaned = segment_text.strip()
326	                if cleaned:
327	                    prose_buffer.append(cleaned)
328	                    prose_len += len(cleaned) + 1
329	
330	        # Flush remaining prose
331	        if prose_buffer:
332	            prose_chunks = self._split_sentences(" ".join(prose_buffer))
333	            for pc_text, pc_start, pc_end in prose_chunks:
334	                chunks.append((pc_text, approx_pos + pc_start, approx_pos + pc_end, False))
335	
336	        # Filter too-small chunks (but never filter tables)
337	        return [
338	            (text, start, end, is_tbl)
339	            for text, start, end, is_tbl in chunks
340	            if is_tbl or len(text) >= self._config.min_chunk_chars
341	        ]
342	
343	    # -- Internal helpers --------------------------------------------------
344	
345	    def _extract_abstract(self, doc: RawDocument) -> str:
346	        """Extract abstract from doc or use first paragraph."""
347	        if doc.abstract:
348	            return doc.abstract[:self._config.abstract_max_chars]
349	        # Heuristic: first paragraph as abstract proxy
350	        paragraphs = doc.content.split("\n\n")
351	        for p in paragraphs:
352	            stripped = p.strip()
353	            if len(stripped) > 80:
354	                return stripped[:self._config.abstract_max_chars]
355	        return ""
356	
357	    def _build_prefix(self, title: str, abstract: str) -> str:
358	        """Build context prefix string (for embedding only — Fix #4)."""
359	        parts: list[str] = []
360	        if title.strip():
361	            parts.append(f"Title: {title.strip()}")
362	        if abstract.strip():
363	            parts.append(f"Abstract: {abstract.strip()}")
364	        return " | ".join(parts)
365	
366	    def _split_sentences(
367	        self, text: str
368	    ) -> list[tuple[str, int, int]]:
369	        """Split prose text into overlapping chunks at sentence boundaries.
370	
371	        Returns list of (chunk_text, char_start, char_end).
372	        Only called on prose segments (tables/code already extracted).
373	        """
374	        sentences = re.split(r"(?<=[.!?])\s+", text)
375	        chunks: list[tuple[str, int, int]] = []
376	        current_sents: list[str] = []
377	        current_len = 0
378	        chunk_start = 0
379	        pos = 0
380	
381	        for sent in sentences:
382	            sent_len = len(sent)
383	            if current_len + sent_len > self._chunk_chars and current_sents:
384	                chunk_text = " ".join(current_sents)
385	                chunks.append((chunk_text, chunk_start, chunk_start + len(chunk_text)))
386	
387	                # Overlap: keep last N chars worth of sentences
388	                overlap_sents: list[str] = []
389	                overlap_len = 0
390	                for s in reversed(current_sents):
391	                    if overlap_len + len(s) > self._overlap_chars:
392	                        break
393	                    overlap_sents.insert(0, s)
394	                    overlap_len += len(s)
395	
396	                current_sents = overlap_sents
397	                current_len = overlap_len
398	                chunk_start = pos - overlap_len
399	
400	            current_sents.append(sent)
401	            current_len += sent_len
402	            pos += sent_len + 1  # +1 for split separator
403	
404	        # Final chunk
405	        if current_sents:
406	            chunk_text = " ".join(current_sents)
407	            chunks.append((chunk_text, chunk_start, chunk_start + len(chunk_text)))
408	
409	        return chunks
410	
411	    def _split_large_table(
412	        self,
413	        table_text: str,
414	        char_start: int,
415	        char_end: int,
416	    ) -> list[tuple[str, int, int]]:
417	        """Split an oversized table by rows, re-attaching header to each split.
418	
419	        Fix R2-#3 (Table Bomb): A 400-row supplementary table from a PDF can be
420	        25,000 chars. The embedding model truncates at 512 tokens (~2K chars),
421	        and it would eat the entire section writer context window.
422	
423	        Each sub-table gets the original header row + separator row prepended
424	        so the LLM retains column context.
425	        """
426	        lines = table_text.split("\n")
427	        if len(lines) < 3:
428	            return [(table_text, char_start, char_end)]
429	
430	        # Header = first line, separator = second line, data = rest
431	        header_line = lines[0]
432	        separator_line = lines[1]
433	        data_lines = [l for l in lines[2:] if l.strip()]
434	
435	        if not data_lines:
436	            return [(table_text, char_start, char_end)]
437	
438	        header_block = f"{header_line}\n{separator_line}"
439	        header_len = len(header_block) + 1  # +1 for newline after header
440	
441	        # Calculate how many data rows fit in max_table_chars
442	        max_data_chars = self._config.max_table_chars - header_len
443	        if max_data_chars < 100:
444	            max_data_chars = 100
445	
446	        sub_tables: list[tuple[str, int, int]] = []
447	        current_rows: list[str] = []
448	        current_len = 0
449	        row_offset = char_start + len(header_block) + 1
450	
451	        for row in data_lines:
452	            row_len = len(row) + 1  # +1 for newline
453	            if current_len + row_len > max_data_chars and current_rows:
454	                sub_text = header_block + "\n" + "\n".join(current_rows)
455	                sub_start = char_start  # approximate — all sub-tables share parent offset
456	                sub_end = sub_start + len(sub_text)
457	                sub_tables.append((sub_text, sub_start, sub_end))
458	                current_rows = []
459	                current_len = 0
460	
461	            current_rows.append(row)
462	            current_len += row_len
463	            row_offset += row_len
464	
465	        # Final sub-table
466	        if current_rows:
467	            sub_text = header_block + "\n" + "\n".join(current_rows)
468	            sub_tables.append((sub_text, char_start, char_start + len(sub_text)))
469	
470	        logger.info(
471	            "Table split: %d chars -> %d sub-tables of ~%d chars each",
472	            len(table_text), len(sub_tables),
473	            self._config.max_table_chars,
474	        )
475	        return sub_tables
476	
477	    @staticmethod
478	    def _deterministic_id(url: str, char_start: int, char_end: int) -> str:
479	        """Deterministic chunk ID from URL + offsets."""
480	        raw = f"{url}:{char_start}:{char_end}"
481	        return f"ev_{hashlib.sha256(raw.encode()).hexdigest()[:16]}"
482	
483	
484	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
485	# CRAG Retriever
486	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
487	
488	class CRAGRetriever:
489	    """Corrective Retrieval-Augmented Generation retriever.
490	
491	    Replaces v1's 126-call LLM analyzer with $0 local embeddings.
492	    Implements CRAG gate (Yan et al. 2024): classify retrieval confidence
493	    as CORRECT / AMBIGUOUS / INCORRECT to decide follow-up action.
494	
495	    Pipeline:
496	        documents -> chunk -> dedup -> embed -> score -> gate -> register
497	
498	    Thread-safe: SourceRegistry uses internal lock.
499	    """
500

500	
501	    def __init__(
502	        self,
503	        config: Optional[CRAGConfig] = None,
504	        registry: Optional[SourceRegistry] = None,
505	    ) -> None:
506	        self._config = config or CRAGConfig()
507	        self._registry = registry or SourceRegistry()
508	        self._chunker = ContextEnrichedChunker(self._config)
509	
510	        # Lazy-loaded embedding service (avoids import-time model load)
511	        self._embed_fn = None
512	
513	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
514	    # Public API
515	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
516	
517	    async def retrieve(
518	        self,
519	        query: str,
520	        documents: list[RawDocument],
521	    ) -> CRAGResult:
522	        """Full CRAG pipeline: chunk -> dedup -> embed -> score -> gate -> register.
523	
524	        Fix R3-#1 (CPU Lock): All CPU-bound operations (chunking, MinHash dedup,
525	        embedding) are wrapped in asyncio.to_thread() to prevent GIL from blocking
526	        the async event loop. Without this, 200+ chunks of model.encode() holds the
527	        GIL for seconds, silently expiring pending asyncio.wait_for timeouts and
528	        dropping network heartbeats across the graph.
529	
530	        Args:
531	            query: The research query (for relevance scoring).
532	            documents: Fetched documents from search node.
533	
534	        Returns:
535	            CRAGResult with EvidencePiece-compatible dicts, registry, and stats.
536	        """
537	        stats: dict[str, Any] = {
538	            "input_documents": len(documents),
539	        }
540	
541	        # 1. Chunk all documents (L6: context-enriched)
542	        # CPU-bound: regex + string ops on all documents
543	        chunks = await asyncio.to_thread(
544	            self._chunker.chunk_documents, documents
545	        )
546	        stats["total_chunks"] = len(chunks)
547	        logger.info("CRAG: %d documents -> %d chunks", len(documents), len(chunks))
548	
549	        # 2. MinHash dedup (L7: before scoring, not after)
550	        # CPU-bound: MinHash signature generation + pairwise comparison
551	        unique_chunks = await asyncio.to_thread(
552	            self._dedup_chunks, chunks
553	        )
554	        stats["after_dedup"] = len(unique_chunks)
555	        stats["dedup_removed"] = len(chunks) - len(unique_chunks)
556	        logger.info("CRAG dedup: %d -> %d chunks (-%d)",
557	                     len(chunks), len(unique_chunks), stats["dedup_removed"])
558	
559	        # 3. Embed query + chunks
560	        # CPU-bound: model.encode() on all-MiniLM-L6-v2 (heaviest operation)
561	        scored_chunks = await asyncio.to_thread(
562	            self._score_chunks, query, unique_chunks
563	        )
564	
565	        # 4. Quality tier assignment + relevance gate (lightweight, stays in-thread)
566	        evidence = self._assign_tiers_and_filter(scored_chunks)
567	        stats["after_filter"] = len(evidence)
568	
569	        # 5. CRAG confidence gate
570	        gate_result = self._crag_gate(evidence)
571	        stats["crag_gate"] = gate_result
572	        logger.info("CRAG gate: %s (%d evidence above threshold)",
573	                     gate_result, len(evidence))
574	
575	        # 6. Register unique sources (L1) — thread-safe via SourceRegistry lock
576	        for ev in evidence:
577	            ev["citation_key"] = self._registry.register(
578	                url=ev["source_url"],
579	                title=ev["source_title"],
580	                source_type=ev["source_type"],
581	                authors=ev.get("authors") or [],
582	                year=ev.get("year"),
583	                doi=ev.get("doi", ""),
584	                authority_score=ev.get("source_confidence", 0.0),
585	            )
586	
587	        # 7. Cap at max_evidence (sorted by relevance)
588	        evidence.sort(key=lambda e: e["relevance_score"], reverse=True)
589	        if len(evidence) > self._config.max_evidence:
590	            evidence = evidence[: self._config.max_evidence]
591	            stats["capped_at"] = self._config.max_evidence
592	
593	        stats["final_evidence"] = len(evidence)
594	        stats["unique_sources"] = self._registry.size
595	
596	        return CRAGResult(
597	            evidence=evidence,
598	            registry=self._registry,
599	            stats=stats,
600	        )
601	
602	    @property
603	    def registry(self) -> SourceRegistry:
604	        """Access the source registry."""
605	        return self._registry
606	
607	    @staticmethod
608	    def state_cleanup_keys() -> dict[str, Any]:
609	        """Return state keys that MUST be cleared after CRAG processing.
610	
611	        Fix R4-#3 (State Memory Bloat): LangGraph serializes the entire state
612	        dict at every node boundary. If 100+ raw HTML pages (50-100MB) stay in
613	        state["fetched_content"], the checkpointer will time out or OOM.
614	
615	        The CRAG graph node MUST merge these keys into its return dict:
616	            return {
617	                "evidence": crag_result.evidence,
618	                **CRAGRetriever.state_cleanup_keys(),  # <-- MANDATORY
619	            }
620	
621	        This acts as garbage collection, replacing heavy payloads with empty
622	        containers before passing control to the LLM-heavy synthesis nodes.
623	        """
624	        return {
625	            "fetched_content": [],       # 50-100MB of raw HTML/PDF text
626	            "search_results": [],        # redundant after evidence extraction
627	            "web_results": [],           # raw Serper/DDG results
628	            "academic_results": [],      # raw S2/OpenAlex results
629	        }
630	
631	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
632	    # Step 2: MinHash dedup (L7)
633	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
634	
635	    def _dedup_chunks(self, chunks: list[EnrichedChunk]) -> list[EnrichedChunk]:
636	        """Remove near-duplicate chunks using MinHash.
637	
638	        CRITICAL (Fix R2-#1 — Context/Dedup Paradox):
639	            Dedup MUST use raw_text, NOT enriched_text. Every chunk from the
640	            same document shares an identical L6 prefix (title + abstract).
641	            If we dedup on enriched_text, the shared 300-word prefix dominates
642	            the Jaccard signature, and MinHash thinks chunks 2-10 are duplicates
643	            of chunk 1. This silently deletes 90% of valid document content.
644	
645	        Reuses existing ContentDeduplicator infrastructure.
646	        """
647	        if len(chunks) <= 1:
648	            return chunks
649	
650	        from src.utils.content_deduplicator import (
651	            ContentDeduplicator,
652	            DeduplicationConfig,
653	        )
654	
655	        dedup_config = DeduplicationConfig(
656	            near_duplicate_threshold=self._config.dedup_threshold,
657	            num_hashes=self._config.dedup_num_hashes,
658	            shingle_size=self._config.dedup_shingle_size,
659	        )
660	        deduplicator = ContentDeduplicator(config=dedup_config)
661	
662	        # INVARIANT: use raw_text (not enriched_text) to avoid L6 prefix domination
663	        items = [{"content": c.raw_text, "_idx": i} for i, c in enumerate(chunks)]
664	        result = deduplicator.deduplicate(items)
665	
666	        # Map back to EnrichedChunk objects
667	        unique_indices = {item["_idx"] for item in result.unique_items}
668	        return [c for i, c in enumerate(chunks) if i in unique_indices]
669	
670	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
671	    # Step 3: Embedding-based scoring
672	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
673	
674	    def _get_embed_fn(self):
675	        """Lazy-load embedding function."""
676	        if self._embed_fn is None:
677	            from src.utils.embedding_service import embed_texts
678	            self._embed_fn = embed_texts
679	        return self._embed_fn
680	
681	    def _score_chunks(
682	        self,
683	        query: str,
684	        chunks: list[EnrichedChunk],
685	    ) -> list[tuple[EnrichedChunk, float]]:
686	        """Embed query + chunks, return (chunk, cosine_score) pairs.
687	
688	        Fix R4-#1 (Embedding Context Truncation): Uses pooled embedding to
689	        handle texts longer than the model's 256-token max_seq_length.
690	        Without this, the model silently truncates enriched_text, seeing only
691	        the L6 prefix (title + abstract) and producing near-identical embeddings
692	        (0.87 cosine) for all chunks from the same document.
693	        """
694	        if not chunks:
695	            return []
696	
697	        embed = self._get_embed_fn()
698	
699	        # Fix R4-#1: Use pooled embedding for long enriched texts
700	        # Query is short (fits in 256 tokens), but enriched chunks are 1024+ tokens
701	        texts = [query] + [c.enriched_text for c in chunks]
702	        embeddings = embed_with_pooling(texts, embed)
703	
704	        query_vec = np.array(embeddings[0], dtype=np.float32)
705	        query_norm = np.linalg.norm(query_vec)
706	        if query_norm < 1e-8:
707	            return [(c, 0.0) for c in chunks]
708	
709	        scored: list[tuple[EnrichedChunk, float]] = []
710	        for i, chunk in enumerate(chunks):
711	            chunk_vec = np.array(embeddings[i + 1], dtype=np.float32)
712	            chunk_norm = np.linalg.norm(chunk_vec)
713	            if chunk_norm < 1e-8:
714	                scored.append((chunk, 0.0))
715	                continue
716	            cosine = float(np.dot(query_vec, chunk_vec) / (query_norm * chunk_norm))
717	            scored.append((chunk, cosine))
718	
719	        return scored
720	
721	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
722	    # Step 4: Tier assignment + relevance gate
723	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
724	
725	    def _assign_tiers_and_filter(
726	        self,
727	        scored: list[tuple[EnrichedChunk, float]],
728	    ) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
729	        """Assign quality tiers and filter below minimum relevance.
730	
731	        Returns EvidencePiece-compatible dicts.
732	        """
733	        evidence: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
734	        cfg = self._config
735	
736	        for chunk, score in scored:
737	            if score < cfg.min_relevance:
738	                continue
739	
740	            # Tier from relevance score
741	            if score >= cfg.gold_threshold:
742	                tier = "GOLD"
743	            elif score >= cfg.silver_threshold:
744	                tier = "SILVER"
745	            else:
746	                tier = "BRONZE"
747	
748	            # Build EvidencePiece-compatible dict
749	            ev: dict[str, Any] = {
750	                "evidence_id": chunk.chunk_id,
751	                "source_url": chunk.doc_url,
752	                "source_title": chunk.doc_title,
753	                "source_type": chunk.source_type,
754	                "direct_quote": chunk.raw_text,
755	                "statement": chunk.raw_text[:500],
756	                "fact_category": "table" if chunk.is_table else "extracted",
757	                "relevance_score": round(score, 4),
758	                "llm_relevance_score": None,
759	                "quality_tier": tier,
760	                "citation_key": "",  # filled in register step
761	                "year": chunk.year,
762	                "authors": chunk.authors,
763	                "venue": chunk.venue,
764	                "doi": chunk.doi,
765	                "perspective": None,
766	                "corroborating_sources": None,
767	                "source_confidence": chunk.authority_score,
768	                "nli_self_check_score": None,
769	                "quote_substance": None,
770	                "tier_composite_score": score,
771	                "quote_char_start": chunk.char_start,
772	                "quote_char_end": chunk.char_end,
773	                "is_table": chunk.is_table,
774	            }
775	            evidence.append(ev)
776	
777	        return evidence
778	
779	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
780	    # Step 5: CRAG confidence gate (Yan et al. 2024)
781	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
782	
783	    def _crag_gate(self, evidence: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> str:
784	        """Classify retrieval confidence: CORRECT / AMBIGUOUS / INCORRECT.
785	
786	        CORRECT:   >= 1 GOLD chunk exists. Proceed to synthesis.
787	        AMBIGUOUS: No GOLD, but SILVER exists. Trigger web search refinement.
788	        INCORRECT: All below SILVER. Trigger full re-search.
789	
790	        The caller (graph node) decides action based on this signal.
791	        """
792	        gold_count = sum(1 for e in evidence if e["quality_tier"] == "GOLD")
793	        silver_count = sum(1 for e in evidence if e["quality_tier"] == "SILVER")
794	
795	        if gold_count > 0:
796	            return "CORRECT"
797	        if silver_count > 0:
798	            return "AMBIGUOUS"
799	        return "INCORRECT"
800	
801	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
802	    # Utility: build RawDocument from search results
803	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
804	
805	    # Fix R5-#2 (Base64 Image Bomb): Jina Reader converts embedded images to
806	    # inline Base64 strings (e.g., ![img](data:image/png;base64,iVBOR...)).
807	    # A single Base64 image can be 50,000+ chars of random alphanumeric noise.
808	    # This obliterates embedding vectors, wastes 10K+ tokens of LLM context,
809	    # and skyrockets API costs — all to feed unreadable garbage to the model.
810	    _BASE64_IMAGE_RE = re.compile(
811	        r"!\[[^\]]*\]\(data:image/[^;]+;base64,[A-Za-z0-9+/=\s]+\)",
812	        re.DOTALL,
813	    )
814	
815	    # Fix R5-#5 (Phantom Figure): References to figures/tables that won't
816	    # exist in the final report. The LLM copies "As shown in Figure 4..."
817	    # from source text, but we can't render Figure 4 — reader sees nothing.
818	    # Scrubbed BEFORE chunking so evidence chunks are clean.
819	    _PHANTOM_REF_RE = re.compile(
820	        r"(?:as\s+(?:shown|illustrated|depicted|presented|summarized|seen)"
821	        r"\s+in\s+(?:Fig(?:ure)?|Table|Chart|Exhibit|Appendix)\s*\.?\s*\d+[a-z]?"
822	        r"[,;.]?\s*)",
823	        re.IGNORECASE,
824	    )
825	
826	    # Fix R2-#4 (Paywall Poison): keywords that indicate paywalled content
827	    _PAYWALL_KEYWORDS = (
828	        "purchase pdf", "buy this article", "log in to access",
829	        "sign in to view", "subscribe to read", "institutional access",
830	        "rent this article", "add to cart", "access through your institution",
831	        "full text is not available", "pdf available for purchase",
832	    )
833	
834	    @classmethod
835	    def documents_from_search_results(
836	        cls,
837	        search_results: list[dict[str, Any]],
838	        fetched_content: dict[str, str],
839	        paywall_min_chars: int = 1000,
840	    ) -> list[RawDocument]:
841	        """Convert search node output into RawDocument list.
842	
843	        Fix R2-#4 (Paywall Poison): If fetched content is too short or
844	        contains paywall keywords, discard it and use the S2 API abstract
845	        instead. This prevents the LLM from trying to synthesize facts
846	        from a shopping cart page.
847	
848	        Args:
849	            search_results: List of dicts with url, title, snippet, source_type, etc.
850	            fetched_content: Map of url -> fetched full text content.
851	            paywall_min_chars: Minimum content length before paywall check.
852	
853	        Returns:
854	            List of RawDocument ready for retrieve().
855	        """
856	        docs: list[RawDocument] = []
857	        paywall_count = 0
858	
859	        base64_total_stripped = 0
860	
861	        for result in search_results:
862	            url = result.get("url", "")
863	            raw_content = fetched_content.get(url, "")
864	            abstract = result.get("snippet", "") or result.get("abstract", "")
865	            title = result.get("title", "")
866	
867	            # Fix R5-#2 (Base64 Image Bomb): Strip inline Base64 images
868	            # BEFORE any length checks — a single image can be 50K+ chars,
869	            # making paywalled content appear voluminous.
870	            if raw_content and "data:image" in raw_content:
871	                original_len = len(raw_content)
872	                raw_content = cls._BASE64_IMAGE_RE.sub("", raw_content)
873	                stripped = original_len - len(raw_content)
874	                if stripped > 0:
875	                    base64_total_stripped += stripped
876	                    logger.debug(
877	                        "Base64 scrub: removed %d chars from %s",
878	                        stripped, url[:60],
879	                    )
880	
881	            # Determine usable content
882	            content = raw_content
883	            is_paywall = False
884	
885	            if raw_content and len(raw_content) < paywall_min_chars:
886	                # Short content — check for paywall indicators
887	                lower = raw_content.lower()
888	                if any(kw in lower for kw in cls._PAYWALL_KEYWORDS):
889	                    is_paywall = True
890	            elif not raw_content:
891	                is_paywall = True
892	
893	            if is_paywall:
894	                paywall_count += 1
895	                # Fall back to S2 abstract if available
896	                if abstract and len(abstract) > 50:
897	                    content = abstract
898	                    logger.debug(
899	                        "Paywall detected for %s — using abstract (%d chars)",
900	                        url[:80], len(abstract),
901	                    )
902	                else:
903	                    # No abstract either — skip entirely
904	                    logger.debug("Paywall + no abstract — skipping %s", url[:80])
905	                    continue
906	
907	            if not content or len(content) < 50:
908	                continue
909	
910	            docs.append(RawDocument(
911	                url=url,
912	                title=title,
913	                content=content,
914	                source_type=result.get("source_type", "web"),
915	                authors=result.get("authors", []),
916	                year=result.get("year"),
917	                venue=result.get("venue", ""),
918	                doi=result.get("doi", ""),
919	                abstract=abstract,
920	                authority_score=result.get("authority_score", 0.0),
921	            ))
922	
923	        if paywall_count:
924	            logger.info(
925	                "CRAG: %d/%d sources were paywalled (abstract fallback or skipped)",
926	                paywall_count, len(search_results),
927	            )
928	        if base64_total_stripped > 0:
929	            logger.info(
930	                "CRAG: stripped %d chars of Base64 image data across %d documents",
931	                base64_total_stripped, len(docs),
932	            )
